title: GitHub account preferences for GitHub/Copilot extensions are not persisted per profile
body:
<!-- ⚠️⚠️ Do Not Delete This! bug_report_template ⚠️⚠️ -->
<!-- Please read our Rules of Conduct: https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/ -->
<!-- 🕮 Read our guide about submitting issues: https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/wiki/Submitting-Bugs-and-Suggestions -->
<!-- 🔎 Search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates. -->
<!-- 🧪 Test using the latest Insiders build to see if your issue has already been fixed: https://code.visualstudio.com/insiders/ -->
<!-- 💡 Instead of creating your report here, use 'Report Issue' from the 'Help' menu in VS Code to pre-fill useful information. -->
<!-- 🔧 Launch with `code --disable-extensions` to check. -->
Does this issue occur when all extensions are disabled?: Yes

<!-- 🪓 If you answered No above, use 'Help: Start Extension Bisect' from Command Palette to try to identify the cause. -->
<!-- 📣 Issues caused by an extension need to be reported directly to the extension publisher. The 'Help > Report Issue' dialog can assist with this. -->
-  VS Code Version: 1.128.0 (Universal)
- OS: macOS / Darwin arm64
- Profiles enabled: Yes
- Workspace: No workspace folder open
- GitHub accounts: Two accounts, both with active Copilot access

### Description

I have two VS Code profiles and two GitHub accounts, both with Copilot access:

* Profile A → GitHub Account A
* Profile B → GitHub Account B

Using Manage Extension Account Preferences, I can add both GitHub accounts and select which account should be used by the GitHub and Copilot extensions.

However, the selected account does not appear to be persisted per profile. When I switch profiles, the account preference seems to remain global or shared.

### Steps to Reproduce

1. Open a new VS Code window with no workspace folder open: Cmd + Shift + N.
2. Create Profile A.
3. Create Profile B.
4. Open Accounts → Manage Extension Account Preferences.
5. Add two GitHub accounts:
    * GitHub Account A
    * GitHub Account B
6. Both accounts have active GitHub Copilot access.
7. Switch to Profile A.
8. In Manage Extension Account Preferences, select GitHub Account A for:
    * GitHub extension
    * GitHub Copilot extension
9. Switch to Profile B.
10. In Manage Extension Account Preferences, select GitHub Account B for:
  * GitHub extension
  * GitHub Copilot extension
11. Switch back to Profile A.

### Expected Behavior

Profile A should still use GitHub Account A for the GitHub and Copilot extensions.
Profile B should still use GitHub Account B for the GitHub and Copilot extensions.

In other words, extension account preferences should be persisted independently per profile.

### Actual Behavior

After switching back to Profile A, GitHub Account B appears to be selected for both the GitHub and Copilot extensions.

The account preference appears to be shared across profiles rather than stored per profile.

### Additional Context

From my testing, this appears to work correctly on a workspace basis. If I open a folder (workspace) and select the desired GitHub and Copilot accounts using Manage Extension Account Preferences, those selections are remembered for that workspace.

However, this means the account selection must be configured for every new folder/workspace that is opened. For users who organize their work using VS Code profiles (for example, a Work profile and a Personal profile), it would be much more convenient if the account preferences could also be persisted at the profile level, so that any workspace opened within a given profile automatically uses the associated GitHub and Copilot accounts.

### Question

Is it currently supported to persist GitHub/Copilot account preferences on a per-profile basis?

If yes, this appears to be a bug.

If not, it would be very useful to support this behavior for users who need to separate personal and work GitHub/Copilot accounts.
author: ivanbacher

<!-- 📣 Issues caused by an extension need to be reported directly to the extension publisher. The 'Help > Report Issue' dialog can assist with this. -->
- VS Code Version: 1.1280
- OS Version: window 10 home 

Steps to Reproduce:

1. Hi team, I am facing an issue with the VS Code terminal. When I run a Python script that takes input using the input() function, the terminal automatically appends the complete file path along with the user input. Because of this, when I check the length using len(name), it gives an incorrect length (like 91 or 93) instead of just counting the letters I typed.
2.
author: aliyan706-code

author: pralaX2007

title: Copilot CLI agent unusable on GitHub Enterprise — CopilotCLIModels model fetch returns 401 Bad credentials
body:
## Bug Report

**VS Code:** 1.126.0
**Copilot Chat:** bundled built-in
**Copilot CLI:** 1.0.63
**Auth provider:** `github-enterprise` (`*.ghe.com`)
**OS:** macOS arm64

---

### What happens

The "Copilot CLI" agent type in the Agents / Chat Sessions view cannot be used. On every window open, the extension fails to fetch the CLI model list:

```
[error] [CopilotCLIModels] Failed to fetch available models:
GitHubApiError: Failed to fetch Copilot user info: 401 Unauthorized: {"message":"Bad credentials"}
```

The error fires **immediately after** a successful enterprise auth:

```
[info] Logged in as <enterprise-user>
[error] [CopilotCLIModels] Failed to fetch available models: ... 401 Bad credentials
[info] Got Copilot token for <enterprise-user>
```

This repeats on every window across multiple VS Code sessions.

---

### What works fine

- **Normal Copilot Chat** — succeeds against the enterprise account (e.g. `claude-opus-4.8 | success`)
- **Standalone Copilot CLI** — `copilot -p "..."` works fully, bills AI Credits, with no auth errors
- **GitHub API** — `gh api user` returns the correct enterprise identity; `gh auth status` shows active session

Only `CopilotCLIModels` 401s.

---

### Root cause hypothesis

VS Code is configured as:

```json
"github-enterprise.uri": "https://<company>.ghe.com",
"authProvider": "github-enterprise"
```

The `CopilotCLIModels` fetch appears to route to **github.com**'s Copilot user-info endpoint while presenting the **enterprise token**, which github.com rejects with 401. The main chat and standalone CLI both work because they respect the enterprise host routing; `CopilotCLIModels` does not.

With no model list, the CLI agent type silently fails to initialize and is unusable in the Agents view.

---

### Steps to reproduce

1. Configure VS Code with a GitHub Enterprise host (`github-enterprise.uri` + `authProvider: github-enterprise`)
2. Sign in with an enterprise account that has an active Copilot seat
3. Open the Chat Agents view and attempt to use the Copilot CLI agent type
4. Observe in **Output → GitHub Copilot Chat**:
   ```
   [CopilotCLIModels] Failed to fetch available models: ... 401 Unauthorized: Bad credentials
   ```

---

### Expected behaviour

`CopilotCLIModels` should route through the configured enterprise host so the Copilot CLI agent type is usable on GitHub Enterprise.

---

### Workaround

Use the standalone `copilot` CLI in an integrated terminal — auth works correctly there.
author: thatapplefreak

author: eyza-cod2

title: Chat: GitHub Copilot quota/billing gates the entire Chat surface even when a separate (BYOK/subscription) provider is selected
body:
<!-- Please search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates -->
<!-- Please attach logs to help us diagnose your issue -->

- Copilot Chat Extension Version: bundled with VS Code (no longer versioned separately)
- VS Code Version: 1.124.2 (Universal), commit 6928394f91b684055b873eecb8bc281365131f1c — also reproduced on 1.123.x
- OS Version: macOS (Darwin arm64 25.5.0)
- Feature (e.g. agent/edit/ask mode): agent mode
- Selected model: a **Claude** provider backed by a separate Anthropic/Claude subscription (BYOK/subscription), switched from the default `Local` (GitHub Copilot) provider
- Logs: not attached

Steps to Reproduce:

1. Configure Chat with two providers: `Local` (GitHub Copilot) and a `Claude` provider authenticated with a *separate* Anthropic/Claude subscription (a different account/subscription, not just a different model).
2. Reach the Copilot limit, or let Copilot billing lapse/expire, so the `Local` provider returns a quota/billing error.
3. In the provider/model picker, switch from `Local` to `Claude`.
4. Send a message.

---

### Summary

The first-party **Chat** UI supports multiple providers — the default `Local` (GitHub Copilot) provider and a separate `Claude` provider backed by my own Anthropic/Claude subscription. When the Copilot entitlement is exhausted/expired, I expect to hit the limit on `Local` — that's correct. But **switching the provider to `Claude` does not let me keep working**: the Copilot quota/billing state appears to gate the whole Chat surface, so an independently-funded provider that has plenty of available usage is also blocked.

### Expected

Requests route through the selected `Claude` provider's subscription (which has available usage), and Chat keeps working. The Copilot limit should only affect the Copilot-backed provider — switching providers should be a graceful fallback.

### Actual

Chat stays blocked by the Copilot quota/billing state regardless of the selected provider. The only ways to keep working are to leave the first-party Chat entirely (Anthropic's standalone Claude Code extension, or the Claude CLI), which defeats the purpose of the integrated provider picker.

### Additional observation (possible cause)

Even with the `Claude` provider selected, my Chat debug history shows requests going through a **`Claude Copilot Proxy`**. That suggests the `Claude` provider's traffic is still routed through GitHub Copilot infrastructure rather than directly to Anthropic — which would explain why the Copilot quota/billing gate applies regardless of which provider is selected. If the gate is enforced at that proxy layer, switching providers can't serve as a fallback by design.

### Impact

- A healthy, separately-paid subscription is unusable precisely when it's needed most (Copilot exhausted/expired).
- Users are pushed out of the integrated editor Chat experience for a billing reason unrelated to the provider they actually selected.
- The provider picker implies provider-level isolation that doesn't hold for entitlement/quota.

### Related issues (different scenarios; this one is specifically about provider-switch fallback)

- #320227 — Copilot Free tier: BYOK models lose tool access after downgrade.
- #316404 — Claude (Max) subscription hits phantom Copilot session/rate limits; a comment there describes requests routing through the Copilot API gateway.
- #314952 — Feature request: allow Claude Agent to run through an Anthropic subscription instead of Copilot quota.
- #311136 — Copilot Chat doesn't work with the Claude Code Agent.

### Question for maintainers

Is gating the Chat surface on Copilot entitlement — even when a non-Copilot provider is explicitly selected — intended? Given the `Claude Copilot Proxy` observation above: can the `Claude` provider talk to Anthropic directly (bypassing the Copilot proxy) when an alternate subscription is configured, or can the Copilot limit at least be scoped to the Copilot provider so an explicitly-selected alternate-subscription provider can serve as a working fallback?

author: IrishBruse

title: Enterprise policy templates have a syntax problem
body:
You're using backticks to highlight setting names and value examples in policy setting descriptions in the ADML and plist files. However, you've overlooked or mistakenly assumed it would escape angle brackets in XML. This is causing group policy to choke and the policies to not work on macOS.

There are two policies where this is present and I will use release 1.124.0 ADML en-US template for the example.

Setting name: ChatEnabledPlugins
line 42, see the plugin ID example in the string:
```<string id="ChatEnabledPlugins_chat_plugins_enabledPlugins_policy">Plugin enablement. Keys are plugin IDs in `<plugin>@<marketplace>` form; values enable or disable the plugin.</string>```

Setting name: ChatExtraMarketplaces
line 44, see the Git URI example in the string:
```<string id="ChatExtraMarketplaces_chat_plugins_extraMarketplaces_policy">Additional plugin marketplaces to query. Keys are marketplace names; values are GitHub shorthand (`owner/repo[#ref]`) or Git URIs (`<url>[#ref]`).</string>```

The angle brackets in the plain text description strings should be replaced with their standard entity equivalent, i.e., 
use  `<` for `<` and `>` for `>`.

title: hindi language pack for vs code
body:
I would like to request official Hindi language pack support for Visual Studio Code. Many Hindi-speaking students and beginners in India use VS Code for learning programming, but the interface is mostly available only in English and a few other languages.

Adding a Hindi language pack would make VS Code more accessible and beginner-friendly for millions of users who are more comfortable reading Hindi. It would especially help school and college students understand menus, settings, extensions, and basic features more easily.

Please consider adding full Hindi localization support in future updates of VS Code. Thank you for creating such a useful editor for developers and learners around the world. 😊
author: ankitdwivedi265985-eng

title: `serve-web` is still unusable for non-English users – broken for over 10 versions (1.110.0 → 1.123.2) or three months.
body: Does this issue occur when all extensions are disabled?: Yes - VS Code Version: 1.123.2 - OS Version: MacOS Tahoe 26.5.1 Steps to Reproduce: 1. use `code serve-web` 2. open vscode in web browser 3. switch display language to any other language except English. 4. the UI translation is totally wrong everywhere. # Description I am reopening / escalating the long-standing `serve-web` disaster. The official `code serve-web` feature has been broken for non-English languages across a wide range of VS Code versions, starting from 1.110.0 up to the current 1.123.0. Despite multiple reports, the problem has never been properly fixed. # Current status (1.123.2) English (US) – works correctly. Any other language (e.g. Chinese, Japanese, French, German) – the web UI shows random strings / gibberish / malformed translations. Core actions (open file, open folder, use terminal, install extensions) are either broken or extremely unreliable. The web version is effectively unusable for any non-English user. # History of failures (chronology) Version | Problem -- | -- 1.110.0 | Blank page with `Uncaught Error: !!! NLS MISSING: 16444 !!!` 1.116.0 | Same issue persists 1.117.0 | English works, but translations are completely corrupt – random characters everywhere 1.120.0 | English version also broken; all core actions fail 1.123.0 | English works, but translations are completely corrupt – random characters everywhere This is not a gradual improvement – it’s a series of regressions spanning more than 10 versions. The `serve-web` feature has never recovered to a stable state after 1.109.5. # Why this is unacceptable `serve-web` is an official feature, not an experimental toy. It is critical for cloud IDEs, self-hosted web environments, and developers using non-English languages. The VS Code team switched to a weekly release cycle, but the quality of `serve-web` has become terrible. Each new version either brings back old bugs or introduces new ones. No one is taking responsibility for this feature. # What the community expects A clear statement from the VS Code team: is `serve-web` still supported? If yes, prioritize fixing it and prevent further regressions. Add automated language tests for `serve-web` so that basic translation loading is validated every release. Provide a working stable version that non-English users can rely on. # Workarounds (that should not be necessary) Use version 1.109.5 (last known somewhat working version). Manually patch `nls.messages.js` (breaks again after every update). Force English language (not an option for many users). Avoid `serve-web` entirely. None of these are acceptable for an official product. # Additional context This issue is a continuation of [#299425](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/299425) and the many comments that followed. The problem is not isolated – dozens of users have reported similar experiences. It’s time to stop ignoring `serve-web` and either fix it or deprecate it properly.

title: VSCode update breaks debugger toolbar
body:
I was in the middle of a debug sessions when I noticed the 'Update now' button, which I clicked.

When the app restarted, launching a debug session failed to show the debug toolbar and resulted in an `Unable to attach to browser` error.

I've stumbled on this error a few times and while searching for an answer, the only working fix is as per the following:

> I doubt it has anything to do with running as administrator. I've never used vscode as admin, but still get this error regularly. The cycle of this error appearing is always the same though:
> 1. Update vscode
> 2. Restart
>     * I don't actually restart immediately, but at the end of the day I close it, or I turn off the laptop, put it to sleep, etc. Then I launch the vscode the next day.
> 4. Launch browser through debugger -> Unable to attach to browser
> 5. Restart
> 6. Launch browser through debugger -> success
>  

 _Originally posted by @bmakan in [#239705](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/239705#issuecomment-3183443109)_

body: Does this issue occur when all extensions are disabled?: N/A - VS Code Version: 1.123.0 - OS Version: macOS 26.5.1 Steps to Reproduce: 1. I use the default product icon theme.

title: old school Refactoring not working when Quota is used up
body:
Type: Bug I have the student package of copilot and my quaota ran out. When i press F2 (my refactor rename shortcut) on a variable the renaming window pops up for a split second before closing and being replaced with a warning that the quota is used up. when you close the warning the refactoring is canceled. title: VSCode Agents misattributes my Claude code consumption to Copilot
body:
Type: Bug Window: Agents Hello VSCode team, I have been using the new "VSCode Agents" app to work with Claude Code and I never touched the Copilot agent. I started seeing a rate limit messge in the IDE telling me about hitting my organization limit. It eventually hit my org limit and stopped working. The problem here is that it misattributed my Claude consumption to be Copilot one and ate up all my orgnization Copilot limit for the month. This is a critical bug in my opinion as basically now, the organization is double charged for both Claude consumption (The real usage) and the ghost consumption (Copilot). Could you please look into this issue ? Thanks, Hassan Radi VS Code version: Code 1.123.0 (Universal) (6a44c352bd24569c417e530095901b649960f9f8, 2026-06-03T11:29:03+02:00) OS version: Darwin arm64 25.4.0 Modes:

author: Hassan-Radi

title: SSH: extension icons missing in Remote Explorer & secondary side bar since 1.123.0
body:
Type: Bug Steps to Reproduce: 1. Connect to a remote host using the Remote - SSH extension. 2. Open the Remote Explorer in the left Activity Bar, and make sure one or more extensions contribute views/icons to the secondary side bar. 3. Observe the icon areas of the Remote Explorer (left side) and the secondary side bar. Expected behavior: The icons of the installed/contributing extensions are displayed in the Remote Explorer (left Activity Bar) and in the secondary side bar — as they were before updating to 1.123.0. Actual behavior: Since updating to 1.123.0, the extension icons are no longer rendered in SSH sessions. The views and extensions themselves still work, but their icons appear blank/missing. This only happens inside Remote - SSH sessions; when working locally the icons render correctly. Additional information: - This is a regression that started with version 1.123.0. In the previous version the icons were displayed correctly. - The problem persists across reconnects and restarts of the SSH session. - The issue does not occur when all extensions are disabled, which is expected since the missing icons belong to extensions. title: Terminal missing letters and black blocks replacing content
body:
Type: Bug - Open the terminal - Run a few commands that display multicolored/styled text, it happenst to me with atuin, and Maven daemons, even Angular CLI output. Using the setting terminal.integrated.gpuAcceleration = off workaround the problem. There was no kernel update or Nvidia kernel module rebuilt between the vscode update from 1.122.1 to 1.123.0. Running in Fedora 44 GNOME session. <!-- Please read our Rules of Conduct: https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/ -->
<!-- Please search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates. -->

<!-- Describe the feature you'd like. -->

Re the [2 hour delay for VS Code extension updates](https://code.visualstudio.com/updates/v1_123#_delayed-extension-autoupdates) it would be good to be able to customise this. 

A 3 day cooldown is pretty standard for other update cooldowns so the ability to set to a 72 hour delay would be great.
author: gleeblezoid

title: New Agents windows does not use my profiles and use Default instead
body:
<!-- ⚠️⚠️ Do Not Delete This! bug_report_template ⚠️⚠️ -->
<!-- Please read our Rules of Conduct: https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/ -->
<!-- 🕮 Read our guide about submitting issues: https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/wiki/Submitting-Bugs-and-Suggestions -->
<!-- 🔎 Search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates. -->
<!-- 🧪 Test using the latest Insiders build to see if your issue has already been fixed: https://code.visualstudio.com/insiders/ -->
<!-- 💡 Instead of creating your report here, use 'Report Issue' from the 'Help' menu in VS Code to pre-fill useful information. -->
<!-- 🔧 Launch with `code --disable-extensions` to check. -->
Does this issue occur when all extensions are disabled?: Yes/No
No
<!-- 🪓 If you answered No above, use 'Help: Start Extension Bisect' from Command Palette to try to identify the cause. -->
<!-- 📣 Issues caused by an extension need to be reported directly to the extension publisher. The 'Help > Report Issue' dialog can assist with this. -->
- VS Code Version: 1.123.0
- OS Version: Windows 11

Hello, the new Agents window has problems with profiles.
It simply does not respect the current profile and tries to open with Default (probably) profile settings.

Steps to Reproduce:

1.  In Default profile, disable Copilot and Agents. (chat.disableAIFeatures and chat.enabled.agents)
2. Create a new profile, name it Copilot.
3. Switch to new profile, enable Copilot and Agents
4. Observe that Copilot Chat works in the side panel.
5. Open the new Agents windows, and observe that it shows "Agents are disabled" in it.
6. Switch to default, enable AI and agents, switch back to this profile, and observe agents are fine

variant: it does not matter whether the new profile inherets the Default or used None in the "Copy From" section during its creation.

title: [Remote SSH] Activity bar icons missing due to CORS blocking vscode-managed-remote-resource:// font scheme (1.123 regression)
body:
Type: Bug 1. Connect to a remote host via Remote SSH extension 2. Open VSCode with a remote workspace 3. Observe that activity bar icons from extensions (GitLens, Database Client, Claude Code, vscode-icons) are missing/invisible **Expected:** Icons render normally in the activity bar **Actual:** Icons are invisible (buttons still clickable but no icon shown) **Root cause visible in Developer Tools console:** Access to font at 'vscode-managed-remote-resource://...' from origin 'vscode-file://vscode-app' has been blocked by CORS policy: Cross origin requests are only supported for protocol schemes: chrome, chrome-extension, chrome-untrusted, data, http, https, vscode-file, vscode-remote-resource, vscode-webview. The scheme `vscode-managed-remote-resource` is not in the allowlist, while `vscode-remote-resource` is. This regression was introduced in 1.123.0 — downgrading to 1.122.1 resolves the issue. **VSCode version:** 1.123.0 **OS:** Windows (client), Linux (remote) **Remote SSH extension:** 0.124.0 VS Code version: Code 1.122.0 (6a49527b96e326fe62fbdb56f60e16877c9aa724, 2026-05-27T14:03:01-07:00) OS version: Windows_NT x64 10.0.26200 Modes: Remote OS version: Linux x64 5.10.0-35-amd64

author: Yahya-khodr

title: Terminal copy corrupts UTF-8 (Japanese/CJK/emoji) text via OSC 52 round-trip in 1.123 (atob in @xterm/addon-clipboard)
body:
# Terminal copy mojibakes UTF-8 (Japanese/CJK/emoji) text via OSC 52 round-trip in 1.123

## TL;DR

In **VS Code 1.123.0** the integrated terminal pipes the user's selection through an **OSC 52 round-trip** that is then decoded by `@xterm/addon-clipboard@0.3.0-beta.220`. That addon's `Base64.decodeText` calls **raw `atob`** and pushes the resulting binary string straight to `navigator.clipboard.writeText`, so every UTF-8 byte becomes a separate Latin-1 character on the system clipboard. Any non-ASCII selection (Japanese, CJK, Cyrillic, emoji, ...) is corrupted in every paste target (Notepad, browsers, `Get-Clipboard`).

`Base64.encodeText` has the symmetrical bug (raw `btoa` rejects U+0080+ characters or silently misencodes them depending on browser).

## Environment

| Item | Value |
|---|---|
| VS Code version | 1.123.0 |
| Commit | `6a44c352bd24569c417e530095901b649960f9f8` |
| Build date | 2026-06-03 |
| OS | Windows 11 |
| Affected file | `resources/app/node_modules/@xterm/addon-clipboard/lib/addon-clipboard.js` |
| Bundled `@xterm/xterm` | `6.1.0-beta.220` |
| Bundled `@xterm/addon-clipboard` | `0.3.0-beta.220` |
| Regression introduced | this 1.123 build (1.122 was not affected on the same machine) |

## Reproduction

1. Open the integrated terminal (default profile, default settings; `terminal.integrated.copyOnSelection` is its default `false`).
2. Run `echo 'こんにちは 別の新規ターミナル'` (or any line containing non-ASCII text).
3. Mouse-select the printed Japanese text.
4. Press `Ctrl+C`.
5. Paste into Notepad (or run `Get-Clipboard` from a separate PowerShell window, or paste into a browser address bar).

### Expected

Clipboard contains `こんにちは 別の新規ターミナル` exactly.

### Actual

Clipboard contains `ãããã«ã¡ã¯ å¥ã®æ°è¦ã¿ã¼ãã«` (each UTF-8 byte mapped 1:1 to a U+0080–U+00FF code unit).

## Bit-level evidence

Selected text: `ナルで日本語を` (7 code points).

UTF-8 bytes of selection: `E3 83 8A E3 83 AB E3 81 A7 E6 97 A5 E6 9C AC E8 AA 9E E3 82 92` (21 bytes).

Base64 encoding of those bytes: `44OK44Or44Gn5pel5pys6Kqe44KS`.

What VS Code's terminal writes to PTY (as OSC 52 echo): `ESC ] 52 ; c ; 44OK44Or44Gn5pel5pys6Kqe44KS ST`.

After PTY echo, `ClipboardAddon._setOrReportClipboard` receives `"c;44OK44Or44Gn5pel5pys6Kqe44KS"`, calls `Base64.decodeText("44OK44Or44Gn5pel5pys6Kqe44KS")`, and the **buggy** `decodeText` returns the 21-character JS string

```
U+00E3 U+0083 U+008A  U+00E3 U+0083 U+00AB  U+00E3 U+0081 U+00A7  U+00E6 U+0097 U+00A5
U+00E6 U+009C U+00AC  U+00E8 U+00AA U+009E  U+00E3 U+0082 U+0092
```

which is the UTF-8 byte stream zero-extended into UTF-16 code units. `navigator.clipboard.writeText` then writes this corrupted JS string as `CF_UNICODETEXT` and every paste consumer sees mojibake.

## Buggy code

`resources/app/node_modules/@xterm/addon-clipboard/lib/addon-clipboard.js` (un-minified):

```js
class Base64 {
  encodeText(e) { return btoa(e); }                       // (1)
  decodeText(e) { try { return atob(e); } catch {} return ""; }  // (2)
}
```

- **(1)** `btoa` is defined only over characters in the Latin-1 range. With any U+0080+ code unit it either throws `InvalidCharacterError` or silently emits wrong base64 (browser-dependent).
- **(2)** `atob` returns a "binary string" — each character is a byte in U+0000–U+00FF. For UTF-8-encoded payloads the result is the raw byte stream, **not** the decoded text. Passing this to `navigator.clipboard.writeText` (which expects a regular UTF-16 JS string) writes 1 code unit per UTF-8 byte to `CF_UNICODETEXT`.

## Call path (proven via DevTools instrumentation)

`Ctrl+C` does **not** go through any direct renderer-side clipboard call. It is routed via OSC 52:

```
xterm._customKeyEventHandler returns true (no VS Code keybinding for plain Ctrl+C)
  → xterm Ctrl+C handling encodes the selection as OSC 52 and writes it to PTY
  → PTY echoes the OSC 52 back as input
  → xterm parser dispatches OSC 52 handler
  → ClipboardAddon._setOrReportClipboard(data)
  → Base64.decodeText(b64)               // <-- CORRUPTION HAPPENS HERE
  → BrowserClipboardProvider.writeText
  → navigator.clipboard.writeText        // writes corrupted JS string
```

Live stack trace captured by hooking `Base64.decodeText` (top frame is the hook):

```
addon[9]._base64.decodeText
  in[28] = "44OK44Or44Gn5pel5pys6Kqe44KS"
  out[21] = "ãã«ã§æ¥æ¬èªã"
  out cp:  U+00E3 U+0083 U+008A U+00E3 U+0083 U+00AB ...

addon[9]._setOrReportClipboard
  ← addon-clipboard.js:1 (OSC handler)
  ← xterm.js end → parse → _innerWrite → write
  ← _writeProcessData @ terminalInstance.ts:1682
  ← _onProcessData    @ terminalInstance.ts:1675
  ← TerminalProcessManager fire
  ← LocalTerminalBackend handleData
  ← ipc.ts onResponse / onBuffer
```

`navigator.clipboard.writeText` and `_clipboardService.writeText` on every reachable object (DOM walk over `.terminal-wrapper` expandos, `xterm._addonManager._addons[*].instance`, `vscode.ipcRenderer.{send,invoke}`, `vscode.ipcMessagePort.acquire`, `MessageChannel.prototype.postMessage`, `MessagePort.prototype.postMessage`, `document.execCommand('copy')`, document/window `copy` events at capture phase) were all instrumented; **only the OSC-52 path fires on `Ctrl+C`**.

## Fix

Replace the `Base64` class in `addon-clipboard.js` (and in the upstream `@xterm/addon-clipboard` source) with UTF-8-aware encode/decode:

```js
class Base64 {
  encodeText(text) {
    const bytes = new TextEncoder().encode(text);
    let bin = "";
    for (let i = 0; i < bytes.length; i++) bin += String.fromCharCode(bytes[i]);
    return btoa(bin);
  }
  decodeText(b64) {
    try {
      const bin = atob(b64);
      const bytes = new Uint8Array(bin.length);
      for (let i = 0; i < bin.length; i++) bytes[i] = bin.charCodeAt(i);
      return new TextDecoder("utf-8").decode(bytes);
    } catch {}
    return "";
  }
}
```

The pre-allocated `Uint8Array` + index assignment avoids the `Function.prototype.apply` argument-count limit (~65k) that `String.fromCharCode(...bytes)` / `btoa(String.fromCharCode(...bytes))` would hit on large selections.

This restores parity with the OSC 52 spec, every other terminal emulator (xterm, iTerm2, kitty, Windows Terminal, Alacritty, foot, ...), and what xterm.js itself does in 5.x prior to the addon refactor.

Verified locally by patching `addon-clipboard.js` in place: after restart, copying `こんにちは 別の新規ターミナル` from the integrated terminal yields the exact same string on the clipboard.

## Related notes

- `terminal.integrated.copyOnSelection` is irrelevant; the bug fires on `Ctrl+C` only because the copy goes through OSC 52 and `decodeText`.
- The pre-1.123 path apparently used a direct `IClipboardService.writeText(this.raw.getSelection())` (the in-memory xterm string was a real UTF-16 JS string and was not subject to base64 round-trip), so this is a clean regression introduced by the OSC-52 round-trip refactor.
- `Base64.encodeText` is exercised by the dual OSC 52 "read clipboard" path (`OSC 52 ; c ; ? ST`), so applications that request the clipboard from inside the terminal will also see corruption on any non-ASCII content stored in the system clipboard.
- The bundled `@xterm/addon-clipboard` is at version `0.3.0-beta.220`. If the bug is fixed in a later beta or in `0.3.0` final, the fix is to roll forward the bundled dependency.

## How this affects users today

Any developer whose primary language uses non-ASCII characters — Japanese, Chinese, Korean, Russian, Greek, Arabic, Hebrew, emoji, accented Latin — cannot reliably copy terminal output out of VS Code 1.123. The display in the terminal is correct so the regression is silent: the user only discovers it after pasting somewhere else.

---

author: shapeflux

title: GitHub Copilot Chat in WSL experiences 40-80 second startup delays due to CopilotCLIChatSessionContentProvider.listSessions
body:
## Environment

* VS Code: 1.122.1
* GitHub Copilot Chat: 0.50.1
* WSL: Ubuntu
* OS Host: Windows 11
* Copilot account: Educational quota
* Remote Development: WSL

## Description

GitHub Copilot Chat takes approximately 40-80 seconds to become available when running inside WSL.

The delay is reproducible across VS Code restarts and occurs even after:

* Clearing Copilot state
* Clearing workspace storage
* Reinstalling Copilot Chat
* Disabling local indexing
* Reducing workspace index size
* Disabling memory features
* Disabling MCP features
* Optimizing Git settings
* Verifying filesystem permissions

The extension itself activates quickly, but startup appears blocked by `CopilotCLIChatSessionContentProvider.listSessions`.

## Observed Logs

```text
ConversationFeature: Waiting for copilot token

[CopilotCLIChatSessionContentProvider] listSessions took 40514ms

Logged in as <account>

Got Copilot token

Copilot Chat: 0.50.1

Failed to create database. Falling back to in-memory db: Error: unable to open database file
```

Subsequent calls are fast:

```text
[CopilotCLIChatSessionContentProvider] listSessions took 14ms
[CopilotCLIChatSessionContentProvider] listSessions took 38ms
[CopilotCLIChatSessionContentProvider] listSessions took 5ms
```

The large delay occurs only once during startup.

## What Has Been Verified

### Extension activation is not the problem

VS Code reports:

```text
GitHub Copilot Chat
Activation: 2348ms
```

### Network is not the problem

```bash
time curl -I https://api.github.com
```

Result:

```text
real 0m0.174s
```

### Session database is tiny

```text
session-store.db       4 KB
session-store.db-shm  32 KB
session-store.db-wal 149 KB
```

### Session state is tiny

```bash
du -sh ~/.copilot/session-state
```

Result:

```text
4.0K
```

### Workspace indexing does not affect the issue

Tested:

```json
"github.copilot.chat.localIndex.enabled": false
"github.copilot.chat.workspace.maxLocalIndexSize": 100
```

No measurable improvement.

### Git is not the problem

Git operations complete in milliseconds and repository scanning is fast.

## Additional Observation

Copilot reports:

```text
Failed to create database. Falling back to in-memory db.
```

However, Copilot successfully creates:

```text
~/.vscode-server/data/User/globalStorage/github.copilot-chat/session-store.db
~/.vscode-server/data/User/globalStorage/github.copilot-chat/session-store.db-wal
~/.vscode-server/data/User/globalStorage/github.copilot-chat/session-store.db-shm
```

which suggests the failing database may be a different internal store.

## Expected

Copilot Chat should become available within a few seconds, similar to Codex and other AI extensions running in the same WSL environment.

## Actual

Copilot Chat consistently requires 40-80 seconds before becoming usable due to an extended `listSessions` operation during startup.

## Reproducibility

100% reproducible across restarts.

title: VS Code 1.122.0 on Arch Linux GNOME Wayland opens xdg-desktop-portal ScreenCast picker on startup
body:
Hello,

After upgrading `visual-studio-code-bin` from `1.121.0-1` to `1.122.0-1` on Arch Linux, VS Code started showing the GNOME/xdg-desktop-portal screen sharing picker every time I launch the editor.

The dialog says that `org.chromium.Chromium` wants to share the screen, similar to what appears when Discord or a browser requests screen sharing. However, this happens immediately when opening VS Code, without starting any screen sharing action.

I confirmed that the request comes from VS Code itself, not from an extension or another Chromium/Electron application.

## Environment

* OS: Arch Linux
* Desktop: GNOME
* Session: Wayland
* VS Code package: `visual-studio-code-bin`
* Affected version: `1.122.0-1`
* Previous working version: `1.121.0-1`
* xdg-desktop-portal: `1.20.4-1`
* xdg-desktop-portal-gnome: `50.0-1`
* xdg-desktop-portal-gtk: `1.15.3-1`

Relevant environment variables:

```text
XDG_SESSION_TYPE=wayland
XDG_CURRENT_DESKTOP=GNOME
XDG_SESSION_DESKTOP=gnome
WAYLAND_DISPLAY=wayland-0
GDK_BACKEND=
GTK_USE_PORTAL=
ELECTRON_OZONE_PLATFORM_HINT=
```

## Steps to reproduce

1. Install or upgrade to `visual-studio-code-bin 1.122.0-1` on Arch Linux.
2. Launch VS Code with a clean profile and extensions disabled:

```bash
code --disable-extensions --user-data-dir /tmp/vscode-clean --extensions-dir /tmp/vscode-empty
```

3. The xdg-desktop-portal screen sharing picker appears immediately.

## Debugging performed

I verified that this still happens with:

```bash
code --disable-extensions
```

and also with a clean user data directory and empty extensions directory:

```bash
code --disable-extensions --user-data-dir /tmp/vscode-clean --extensions-dir /tmp/vscode-empty
```

I monitored D-Bus calls to the ScreenCast portal:

```bash
dbus-monitor --session "interface='org.freedesktop.portal.ScreenCast'"
```

When launching VS Code, the following calls appear:

```text
method call sender=:1.158 -> destination=:1.58 path=/org/freedesktop/portal/desktop; interface=org.freedesktop.portal.ScreenCast; member=CreateSession
  session_handle_token "webrtc_session475676062"
  handle_token "webrtc_1473632979"

method call sender=:1.158 -> destination=:1.58 path=/org/freedesktop/portal/desktop; interface=org.freedesktop.portal.ScreenCast; member=SelectSources
  types uint32 1
  multiple false
  cursor_mode uint32 4
  persist_mode uint32 1

method call sender=:1.158 -> destination=:1.58 path=/org/freedesktop/portal/desktop; interface=org.freedesktop.portal.ScreenCast; member=Start
```

Then I mapped the D-Bus sender with:

```bash
busctl --user status :1.158
```

The result showed that the sender is VS Code itself:

```text
Comm=code
Exe=/opt/visual-studio-code/code
CommandLine=/opt/visual-studio-code/code --disable-extensions --user-data-dir /tmp/vscode-clean --extensions-dir /tmp/vscode-empty
CGroup=/user.slice/user-1000.slice/user@1000.service/app.slice/app-org.chromium.Chromium-310897.scope
```

I also checked the portal logs:

```bash
journalctl --user -f -u xdg-desktop-portal -u xdg-desktop-portal-gnome -u xdg-desktop-portal-gtk
```

The GNOME portal logs showed repeated messages like:

```text
xdg-desktop-portal-gnome: Failed to associate portal window with parent window
```

## Version comparison

The issue appeared after upgrading:

```text
visual-studio-code-bin 1.121.0-1 -> 1.122.0-1
```

After downgrading back to:

```text
visual-studio-code-bin 1.121.0-1
```

the issue disappeared.

## Additional notes

Because the issue still occurs with extensions disabled, a clean user data directory, and an empty extensions directory, this does not appear to be caused by a VS Code extension.

It looks like VS Code/Electron/Chromium is making a WebRTC ScreenCast portal request on startup in version `1.122.0`, at least on Arch Linux + GNOME Wayland.

Monitoring org.freedesktop.portal.ScreenCast shows VS Code creating a WebRTC screencast session during startup:

org.freedesktop.portal.ScreenCast SelectSources

org.freedesktop.portal.ScreenCast Start

The session tokens are named:

webrtc_session...

webrtc_...

which suggests the request originates from Electron/Chromium WebRTC desktop capture code.

Notes
Reproducible on every launch of VS Code 1.122.0
Not reproducible on VS Code 1.121.0
Appears to be a regression introduced between 1.121.0 and 1.122.0
author: surajskrv

title: Feature Request: Voice Control support in Agent Window (Copilot Chat)
body:
## Feature Request: Voice Control in Agent Window

### Summary

Voice control via the [VS Code Speech](https://marketplace.visualstudio.com/items?itemName=ms-vscode.vscode-speech) extension works well in the standard VS Code editor and chat interface, but is **not available in the Agent Window** (Copilot agent mode panel).

### Current Behavior

- VS Code Speech extension is installed and functioning correctly in VS Code (e.g., dictation in editor, voice input in standard Copilot Chat)
- The Agent Window does **not** expose a microphone / voice input button
- There is no way to use voice control to interact with the Copilot agent from the Agent Window

### Expected Behavior

The Agent Window should support voice input via the VS Code Speech extension, consistent with the experience in the standard Copilot Chat panel. A microphone button (or equivalent shortcut) should be available to activate voice dictation when composing prompts in the Agent Window.

### Steps to Reproduce

1. Install the [VS Code Speech](https://marketplace.visualstudio.com/items?itemName=ms-vscode.vscode-speech) extension
2. Verify voice input works in VS Code editor or standard Copilot Chat
3. Open the Copilot **Agent Window** (agent mode)
4. Observe that there is no voice input option available

### Environment

- VS Code Speech extension: installed & working
- Feature gap: Agent Window lacks voice control integration

### Motivation

Voice control significantly improves accessibility and productivity. As the Agent Window becomes a primary interface for interacting with Copilot, parity with the rest of the VS Code speech experience is important for users who rely on or prefer voice input.

body:

I’d like to suggest a way to improve the Voice Chat experience.  Currently, the “Start Voice Chat” button triggers a live speech-to-text function that doesn’t handle out-loud hesitations well.

For example, in OpenAI’s ChatGPT or Mistral AI’s Le Chat, the voice is recorded.  Once the user finishes speaking (either by going silent or pressing the submit button) the system begins using their model to generate the prompt.

This would significantly enhance the user experience.

title: Wayland: Portal screen share dialog appears on every startup (desktopCapturer.getSources in warmUpScreenSources)
body:
## Environment

| | |
|---|---|
| **VS Code** | 1.122.0 (`visual-studio-code-bin`) |
| **Electron** | 39.8.8 |
| **Chrome** | 142.0.7444.265 |
| **OS** | Arch Linux, kernel 6.18.33 |
| **Compositor** | niri (Wayland) |
| **PipeWire** | 1.6.6 |
| **xdg-desktop-portal** | 1.20.4 |
| **xdg-desktop-portal-gnome** | 50.0 |

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Launch VS Code on any Wayland compositor (niri, Sway, Hyprland, etc.)
2. An xdg-desktop-portal screen share dialog appears immediately:

```
org.chromium.Chromium wants to share your screen.
Choose what you'd like to share.
```

3. Dialog appears on every cold start, regardless of extensions or settings.

## Root Cause

`src/vs/code/electron-main/app.ts` lines 234-256:

```typescript
const warmUpScreenSources = () => {
    desktopCapturer.getSources({         // <-- triggers portal dialog on Wayland
        types: ['screen'],
        thumbnailSize: { width: 0, height: 0 },
    }).then(...)
};

if (!isMacintosh || systemPreferences.getMediaAccessStatus('screen') === 'granted') {
    warmUpScreenSources();               // <-- always true on Linux (!isMacintosh)
}
```

On macOS, `getMediaAccessStatus('screen')` guards this call — if screen recording permission is denied, the warm-up is skipped. On Linux, `!isMacintosh` is unconditionally `true`, so `desktopCapturer.getSources()` always runs at startup.

On Wayland, `desktopCapturer.getSources()` goes through the xdg-desktop-portal ScreenCast portal, which requires user consent and pops up the screen share permission dialog. The same code on X11 works fine because screen enumeration does not require portal mediation.

## Confirmed Not the Cause

- Extensions (`--disable-extensions` has no effect)
- `issueReporter.wizard.enabled` setting
- `--disable-features=DesktopCapture,WebRTCPipeWireCapturer` Chromium flags (dialog still appears)
- Google Chrome 148 on the same system does **not** exhibit this behavior (only Electron 39 `desktopCapturer.getSources()` triggers it)

## Suggested Fix

Either:

**a)** Skip `warmUpScreenSources()` on Linux (same as macOS when permission is denied):

```typescript
if (isMacintosh && systemPreferences.getMediaAccessStatus('screen') === 'granted') {
    warmUpScreenSources();
}
```

**b)** Or defer the warm-up to only run when the issue reporter actually needs screen sources, rather than proactively in the constructor.

This is a regression — older versions of VS Code (<1.121) on the same system did not show this dialog.

title: hindi Language Pack for Visual Studio Code
body:
I would like to request official Hindi language pack support for Visual Studio Code. Many Hindi-speaking students and beginners in India use VS Code for learning programming, but the interface is mostly available only in English and a few other languages.

Adding a Hindi language pack would make VS Code more accessible and beginner-friendly for millions of users who are more comfortable reading Hindi. It would especially help school and college students understand menus, settings, extensions, and basic features more easily.

Please consider adding full Hindi localization support in future updates of VS Code. Thank you for creating such a useful editor for developers and learners around the world. 😊
author: ankitdwivedi265985-eng

title: Creating a new Profile from Python template silently fails.
body:
<!-- ⚠️⚠️ Do Not Delete This! bug_report_template ⚠️⚠️ -->
<!-- Please read our Rules of Conduct: https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/ -->
<!-- 🕮 Read our guide about submitting issues: https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/wiki/Submitting-Bugs-and-Suggestions -->
<!-- 🔎 Search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates. -->
<!-- 🧪 Test using the latest Insiders build to see if your issue has already been fixed: https://code.visualstudio.com/insiders/ -->
<!-- 💡 Instead of creating your report here, use 'Report Issue' from the 'Help' menu in VS Code to pre-fill useful information. -->
<!-- 🔧 Launch with `code --disable-extensions` to check. -->
Does this issue occur when all extensions are disabled?: I started an Extension Bisect, and was able to reproduce this behavior in the very first trial, i.e. all extensions disabled.

<!-- 🪓 If you answered No above, use 'Help: Start Extension Bisect' from Command Palette to try to identify the cause. -->
<!-- 📣 Issues caused by an extension need to be reported directly to the extension publisher. The 'Help > Report Issue' dialog can assist with this. -->

Hi all, I believe this could be related to https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/299728, but was able to get some additional information from the logs.

Version Information:
```
Version: 1.121.0 (user setup)
Commit: f6cfa2ea2403534de03f069bdf160d06451ed282
Date: 2026-05-19T11:51:53+02:00
Electron: 39.8.8
ElectronBuildId: 13870025
Chromium: 142.0.7444.265
Node.js: 22.22.1
V8: 14.2.231.22-electron.0
OS: Windows_NT x64 10.0.26200
```

Steps to Reproduce:

0. Open a new VSCode window.
1. `File` -> `Preferences` -> `Profile (X)` -> `Profiles`
2. `New Profile/More Actions` -> `From Template` -> `Python`.
3. Changed `Name` to `Python222` to make it easier to distinguish from other profiles, and later, to make it easier to find in logs.
4. Click either the `Preview` or `Create` button, and it silently fails.

Expected Result:
1. For manually created Profiles, clicking `Preview` opens a new VSCode window with the profile loaded. I would expect a similar result when creating a Profile from a template.

Debugging Process:
* Obviously something related to Profiles + Extensions is misbehaving.
* Wasn't sure if the current VSCode window was failing to launch a new window, or the new window itself was failing during init.
* Command Palette -> `Developer: Open Logs Folder` -> copied `C:\Users\<me>\AppData\Roaming\Code\logs`.
* Opened terminal (I have msys tools on `%PATH%`), `find C:\Users\<me>\AppData\Roaming\Code\logs -type f -mmin -3`, found only logs in `20260524T221149` were changing on pressing `Preview`.
* Created a before snapshot: `cp -r 20260524T221149 "C:\Users\<me>\Desktop\logs-before"`.
* Clicked `Preview` yet again.
* Creared an after snapshot: `cp -r 20260524T221149 "C:\Users\<me>\Desktop\logs-after"`.
* `diff -r logs-before logs-after > logs.diff` and `code logs.diff`.
* Lots of sync logs, but the relevant logs appear to be:
```
diff -r logs-before/window3/renderer.log logs-after/window3/renderer.log
36a37
> 2026-05-24 22:57:12.515 [info] Importing Profile (Python222): Started installing extensions.
```

```
diff -r logs-before/sharedprocess.log logs-after/sharedprocess.log
156a157,166
> 2026-05-24 22:57:12.583 [info] Getting Manifest... charliermarsh.ruff
> 2026-05-24 22:57:12.675 [info] Installing extension: charliermarsh.ruff {"isMachineScoped":false,"donotIncludePackAndDependencies":true,"installGivenVersion":false,"installPreReleaseVersion":true,"profileLocation":{"$mid":1,"external":"vscode-userdata:/c%3A/Users/<me>/AppData/Roaming/Code/User/profiles/-be920c9/extensions.json","path":"/C:/Users/<me>/AppData/Roaming/Code/User/profiles/-be920c9/extensions.json","scheme":"vscode-userdata"},"context":{"skipWalkthrough":true},"isBuiltin":false,"isApplicationScoped":false,"productVersion":{"version":"1.121.0","date":"2026-05-19T11:51:53+02:00"}}
> 2026-05-24 22:57:12.675 [info] Installing the extension without checking dependencies and pack charliermarsh.ruff
> 2026-05-24 22:57:12.821 [info] Extension signature verification result for charliermarsh.ruff: Success. Internal Code: 0. Executed: true. Duration: 103ms.
> 2026-05-24 22:57:12.918 [info] Extension installed successfully: charliermarsh.ruff vscode-userdata:/c%3A/Users/<me>/AppData/Roaming/Code/User/profiles/-be920c9/extensions.json
> 2026-05-24 22:57:12.930 [info] The 'github.copilot' extension is deprecated, fetching the compatible 'GitHub.copilot-chat' extension instead.
> 2026-05-24 22:57:13.117 [info] Getting Manifest... github.copilot-chat
> 2026-05-24 22:57:13.408 [info] Installing extension: github.copilot-chat {"isMachineScoped":false,"donotIncludePackAndDependencies":true,"installGivenVersion":false,"installPreReleaseVersion":true,"profileLocation":{"$mid":1,"external":"vscode-userdata:/c%3A/Users/<me>/.vscode/extensions/extensions.json","path":"/c:/Users/<me>/.vscode/extensions/extensions.json","scheme":"vscode-userdata"},"context":{"skipWalkthrough":true},"isBuiltin":true,"isApplicationScoped":true,"productVersion":{"version":"1.121.0","date":"2026-05-19T11:51:53+02:00"}}
> 2026-05-24 22:57:13.409 [info] Installing the extension without checking dependencies and pack github.copilot-chat
> 2026-05-24 22:57:13.497 [error] Error while installing the extension github.copilot-chat Extension 'github.copilot-chat' is a built-in extension with version '0.49.0' and cannot be downgraded to version '0.48.1'. vscode-userdata:/c%3A/Users/<me>/.vscode/extensions/extensions.json
```

* Changing the new `Python222` Profile's "Extension Source" from `Python` (i.e. the template) to `Default` allows `Preview` to work, but kind of defeats the purpose of using the template.
* I tried variations of disabling/enabling the `@builtin/github.copilot-chat` in the `Default` profile, restarting the window, and then creating a new Profile from the Python template, but same issue.
* Couldn't find any reference to this extension under `~/.vscode/`, or within other profile definitions, that could require the extension's version to be locked.
* After more grepping and parsing of logs, I was able to find the definition of the "Python template", and found it was requesting a pre-release version of the problematic extension.

```
        {
            "identifier": {
                "id": "github.copilot-chat",
                "uuid": "7ec7d6e6-b89e-4cc5-a59b-d6c4d238246f"
            },
            "displayName": "GitHub Copilot Chat",
            "preRelease": true,
            "applicationScoped": true
        },
```

* Under Extensions -> `@builtin`, I checked if there was any "Switch to Pre-Release" button, but there wasn't.
* The "Download Specific Version VSIX..." option also only offered versions up to and including `0.48.1`, so looks to be the cause.
author: mcteo

title: Long terminal tab title (set via OSC sequence) overflows into the panel action buttons in single-tab mode
body:
### Steps to reproduce

1. Run a terminal application that sets a long title via the OSC `]0;TITLE\a` escape sequence (e.g. Claude Code CLI, which sets the title to the current task description).
2. Have only one terminal open so the panel uses the single-tab header layout (title shown next to the "TERMINAL" tab, action buttons on the right).
3. Make the panel narrow enough that the title would not fit.

### Expected

The title is truncated (with ellipsis) before it reaches the action buttons (Split, New Terminal, Kill, …). The actions stay visible and clickable.

### Actual

The title is rendered at full length and visually overlaps the action buttons. The button row becomes unreadable behind the text and harder to hit.

Screenshot will be attached as a follow-up comment.

### Environment

- VS Code: 1.121.0 (`f6cfa2ea2403534de03f069bdf160d06451ed282`), arm64
- macOS: 26.5, Apple Silicon
- Terminal title set via OSC sequence from Claude Code CLI

Launch VS Code Insiders on Wayland (GNOME, Ubuntu 24.04) 2. Wait for the main window to load — no further action needed Expected: VS Code starts normally without any system dialogs. title: Please Add Official Hindi Language Pack Support in VS Code
body:
<!-- ⚠️⚠️ Do Not Delete This! feature_request_template ⚠️⚠️ -->
<!-- Please read our Rules of Conduct: https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/ -->
<!-- Please search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates. -->

<!-- Describe the feature you'd like. -->


I would like to request official Hindi language pack support for Visual Studio Code. Many Hindi-speaking students and beginners in India use VS Code for learning programming, but the interface is mostly available only in English and a few other languages.

Adding a Hindi language pack would make VS Code more accessible and beginner-friendly for millions of users who are more comfortable reading Hindi. It would especially help school and college students understand menus, settings, extensions, and basic features more easily.

Please consider adding full Hindi localization support in future updates of VS Code. Thank you for creating such a useful editor for developers and learners around the world. 😊

title: Copilot Chat tool calls hang on file write operations and end as orphaned tool calls
body:
- Copilot Chat Extension Version: 0.48.1
- VS Code Version: 1.120.0
- OS Version: Linux (remote environment via VS Code Server)
- Feature (e.g. agent/edit/ask mode): Agent mode / file editing
- Selected model (e.g. GPT 4.1, Claude 3.7 Sonnet): GPT-5.4 in Copilot CLI runtime, with runtime logs also showing gpt-4o-mini requests
- Logs:
  [CopilotCLI] waitForPendingBackgroundTasks timed out after 600s — proceeding with exit.
  [CopilotCLI] Completing 1 orphaned tool calls.
  [CopilotCLI] Closing session <session-id>

Steps to Reproduce:

1. Start a new Copilot Chat session in a remote Linux VS Code Server environment.
2. Ask the agent to read a few files and then update or create a file.
3. Read operations complete normally.
4. When the agent reaches the file write step, the chat often hangs for a long time around messages like "Applying patch", "Applying edits", or "Processing".
5. If I wait long enough or re-enter the session later, runtime logs show: "Completing 1 orphaned tool calls."

Additional details:

- This is reproducible across multiple attempts.
- It also happens in a newly created chat session, so it does not seem to be limited to stale session state.
- Restarting VS Code did not resolve it.
- Trying "Developer: Restart Extension Host" led to a reconnect failure dialog ("Cannot reconnect. Please reload the window."), and after reloading the window the issue still reproduced.
- The pattern seems specific to file write operations. Read-only operations appear normal.
- In the affected session debug log, only repeated session_start entries were written, without the expected complete tool-call lifecycle.

Logs: 

2026-05-19 20:15:59 [warning] [CopilotCLI] waitForPendingBackgroundTasks timed out after 600s — proceeding with exit.
2026-05-19 20:20:49 [info] [CopilotCLI] Completing 1 orphaned tool calls.
2026-05-19 20:27:02 [info] [CopilotCLI] Closing session <session-id>
2026-05-19 20:28:59 [info] [CopilotCLI] Completing 1 orphaned tool calls.

title: AgentHost: subsession support
author: roblourens

title: Chronicle: SQLite store is unreliable on SSH-Remote workspaces
body:
## Problem

The chronicle local store uses SQLite for persistence. On SSH-Remote workspaces (Linux), SQLite encounters cascading failures due to network filesystems not providing the POSIX locking guarantees that SQLite requires.

The failure pattern is:
1. Initial locking protocol errors or disk I/O errors
2. Escalates to database disk image is malformed
3. Then file is not a database (corruption is now permanent)
4. All subsequent flush operations fail until the database file is deleted

This disproportionately affects heavy users in remote development scenarios — exactly the cohort most reliant on session history.

## Observed error categories (flush operation)

- database disk image is malformed — persistent corruption
- file is not a database — corruption past recovery
- locking protocol — concurrent access on non-POSIX FS
- cannot rollback - no transaction is active — state desync
- database is locked — contention

## Suggested mitigations

1. **Detect remote filesystem** and switch to WAL mode or a non-SQLite fallback (e.g., append-only JSON log) for workspaces where POSIX locking is not guaranteed
2. **Auto-recover from corruption** — detect malformed DB at init and recreate (with optional backup of raw data)
3. **Add retry with backoff** for transient locking errors before failing the flush

## Environment

Primarily affects: Linux + SSH-Remote (vscode-server)
Also observed in smaller numbers: Linux + tunnel connections

author: alexr00

title: Copilot Chat in VS Code does not resolve #changes as git context
body: - Copilot Chat Extension Version: 0.48.1 - VS Code Version: 1.120.0 - OS Version: Ubuntu (WSL) in Windows 11 (10.0.26200) - Feature: any mode - Selected model: any model I tried to use #changes in Copilot Chat on VS Code to reference my uncommitted git changes, but it does not resolve. Instead, the prompt behaves like a normal # reference and the suggestion list shows unrelated items such as agent markdown files. Steps to reproduce: Open a local git repo with uncommitted changes. Open Copilot Chat in VS Code. Type #changes. Observe that the git changes are not attached as context. Expected: #changes should attach the current uncommitted changes as chat context, or VS Code should clearly indicate that this reference is unsupported. Actual: No git-context attachment happens. In my case, the suggestions are unrelated to Git changes.

author: aeschli

title: Javascript Debugger Unable to Attach
body:
<!-- ⚠️⚠️ Do Not Delete This! bug_report_template ⚠️⚠️ -->
<!-- Please read our Rules of Conduct: https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/ -->
<!-- 🕮 Read our guide about submitting issues: https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/wiki/Submitting-Bugs-and-Suggestions -->
<!-- 🔎 Search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates. -->
<!-- 🧪 Test using the latest Insiders build to see if your issue has already been fixed: https://code.visualstudio.com/insiders/ -->
<!-- 💡 Instead of creating your report here, use 'Report Issue' from the 'Help' menu in VS Code to pre-fill useful information. -->
<!-- 🔧 Launch with `code --disable-extensions` to check. -->
Does this issue occur when all extensions are disabled?: Yes

<!-- 🪓 If you answered No above, use 'Help: Start Extension Bisect' from Command Palette to try to identify the cause. -->
<!-- 📣 Issues caused by an extension need to be reported directly to the extension publisher. The 'Help > Report Issue' dialog can assist with this. -->
- VS Code Version: 1.120
- OS Version: Windows 11

Steps to Reproduce:

1. Create index.html file with this content: 
```
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html>
  <body>
    <script>
      console.log("hello");
    </script>
  </body>
</html>
```
2. Add breakpoint to console.log
3. Add this profile to launch.json
```
{
      "name": "test browser",
      "type": "msedge",
      "request": "launch",
      "file": "${workspaceFolder}/test.html"
    }
```
5. Launch that profile. You will see the error: Unable to launch browser: Unable to attach to browser

This happens only on version 1.120, once I downgraded to 1.119 this worked fine.

title: Repeated crashes in Dev Container workflow: ChatSessionStore "Invalid string length" plus renderer listener leaks in chat/tool UI
body: Title: Repeated crashes in Dev Container workflow: ChatSessionStore "Invalid string length" plus renderer listener leaks in chat/tool UI VS Code repeatedly crashes or restarts in a Dev Container workflow. Around the crashes, the local workbench shows chat-session persistence errors, and the host renderer logs also show repeated listener leak warnings. Environment - Host OS: Windows - VS Code launched from an AMD AI Bundle installation path - Remote environment: Dev Container on Linux - Workspace opened through VS Code Remote / Dev Containers - GitHub Copilot Chat active - Remote extension version observed: github.copilot-chat-0.48.1 - VS Code build/commit visible in stack paths: 0958016b2a... Main visible error VS Code showed this notification: "Error serializing chat session for storage. The session will be lost if the window is closed. Please report this issue to the VS Code team." The visible stack included: RangeError: Invalid string length at JSON.stringify () at m6.write (...) at dne.writeSession (...) at async dne.storeSessions (...) at async Object.willDisposeModel (...) at async eVe.doDestroyReferencedObject (...) What happens - VS Code crashes or restarts repeatedly while working in a Dev Container - Before or around the crash, the local renderer logs repeatedly show: - ChatSessionStore: Error writing chat session Invalid string length - potential listener LEAK detected - Some of the listener leak stacks reference chat/tool-related workbench paths such as: - UU.createToolInvocationSubPart - onDidRegisterTerminalInstanceWithToolSession - onDidContinueInBackground - onWillShutdown Why this looks important This makes the issue look like a local workbench chat/tool-session persistence problem, not only a remote extension-host problem. I cannot prove from outside that the listener leaks are the sole root cause of the crash, but they are a strong correlated signal in the same host workbench path where chat-session persistence is already failing. Additional observations Host-side observations - Host renderer logs repeatedly show ChatSessionStore: Error writing chat session Invalid string length - Host renderer logs also repeatedly show potential listener LEAK detected - A newly collected host bundle added one additional Crashpad dump for the latest crash - Host-side chat metadata is relatively small and stable: - 11 files - 719332 bytes - transcript line counts 774 and 54 Remote-side observations - Remote extension host logs show repeated: - PendingMigrationError: navigator is now a global in nodejs - This now points at github.copilot-chat-0.48.1 during activation - Remote chat storage looks much larger and appears duplicated across two workspace storage roots - Observed remote storage sizes: - one storage around 71 MB - another around 18 MB - Observed large transcript counts include: - 45440 lines - 24550 lines - 11599 lines - The duplicate remote storage roots appear after lock conflicts / stale lock recovery following crashes Expected behavior VS Code should either: - persist chat session state successfully, or - detect oversized or corrupt chat state and recover gracefully It should not: - repeatedly fail with Invalid string length - accumulate listener leak warnings in the renderer - crash or restart the window Actual behavior - Repeated ChatSessionStore write failures with Invalid string length - Repeated renderer-side listener leak warnings - Repeated window crashes or restarts in the Dev Container workflow Attachments I have collected the following and can provide them: - host log bundle - remote log bundle - host Crashpad dump files - chat storage metadata summaries I will attach the host and remote bundles first. I can also provide Crashpad dumps on request, but I may hold those back initially because they can contain sensitive information. [20260517T142342.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/27900598/20260517T142342.zip) [20260517T122018Z.tar.gz](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/27900602/20260517T122018Z.tar.gz)

author: ankitdwivedi265985-eng

title: Add a clear UI affordance to manage sandbox mode / exit the sandbox
body:
## Summary
Sandbox mode is useful as a safer default, but it’s hard to manage if users can’t easily tell when they’re in a sandboxed environment or quickly choose to leave it when appropriate. We should consider adding an explicit UI affordance—either standalone or integrated with approval modes—that makes sandbox state visible and actionable.

## Problem
Today, users may be willing to keep sandboxing enabled by default, but only if they can more easily:
- understand whether they are currently operating in a sandbox, and
- intentionally transition out of the sandbox when needed.

Without a clear control or status indicator, sandboxing can feel opaque or cumbersome, even if it is the preferred safety default.

<!-- Please search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates. -->

<!-- Describe the feature you'd like. -->
Currently, the agent built into Copilot Chat cannot be disabled. This often disrupts the workflow when new, more powerful skills (such as superpowers) are added, because the built‑in agent interferes with or overrides them.

I would like to request a configuration option that allows users or developers to disable the internal plan agent when needed. This would make it easier to fully leverage custom skills without having their flow interrupted by the default orchestration logic.

Thank you for considering this improvement

```
# DevPod Start vscode-remote-try-python.devpod
Host vscode-remote-try-python.devpod
  ForwardAgent yes
  LogLevel error
  StrictHostKeyChecking no
  UserKnownHostsFile /dev/null
  HostKeyAlgorithms rsa-sha2-256,rsa-sha2-512,ssh-rsa
  ConnectTimeout 60
  ProxyCommand "/Users/<user>/.local/bin/devpod" ssh --stdio --context default --user vscode vscode-remote-try-python"
  User vscode
# DevPod End vscode-remote-try-python.devpod
```
Which work fine for traditional VSCode Remote SSH workspaces but do not for the Agents window

title: Agents Window: WSL workspace fails with UNC wsl.localhost access denied error
body:
<!-- ⚠️⚠️ Do Not Delete This! bug_report_template ⚠️⚠️ -->
<!-- Please read our Rules of Conduct: https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/ -->
<!-- 🕮 Read our guide about submitting issues: https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/wiki/Submitting-Bugs-and-Suggestions -->
<!-- 🔎 Search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates. -->
<!-- 🧪 Test using the latest Insiders build to see if your issue has already been fixed: https://code.visualstudio.com/insiders/ -->
<!-- 💡 Instead of creating your report here, use 'Report Issue' from the 'Help' menu in VS Code to pre-fill useful information. -->
<!-- 🔧 Launch with `code --disable-extensions` to check. -->
Does this issue occur when all extensions are disabled?: Yes

<!-- 🪓 If you answered No above, use 'Help: Start Extension Bisect' from Command Palette to try to identify the cause. -->
<!-- 📣 Issues caused by an extension need to be reported directly to the extension publisher. The 'Help > Report Issue' dialog can assist with this. -->
- VS Code Version: 1.120.0
- OS Version: Windows 11, WSL2 Ubuntu 24.04

**Title:** Agents Window: UNC host 'wsl.localhost' access denied when selecting a WSL workspace — unhandled Node.js error

---

**Steps to Reproduce:**

1. Open VS Code on Windows (host) — no extensions required to reproduce
2. Open the Agents Window via the Command Palette: `Chat: Open Agents Window`
   (or select the "Open in Agents" button in the VS Code title bar)
3. Click **New** to start a new agent session
4. In the workspace dropdown, select any folder located inside WSL
   (any path under `\\wsl.localhost\<distro>\...`)
5. Observe the error in the VS Code **Output** panel
   (select "JSON Language Server" from the Output channel dropdown)

---

**Expected behavior:**

Either:
- WSL workspaces are supported as a remote target in the Agents Window
  (similar to SSH and Dev Tunnels already listed in the Remote tab), OR
- A clear, actionable error message is shown, such as:
  *"WSL workspaces are not yet supported in the Agents Window.
  Use SSH or a dev tunnel to connect to WSL."*

Currently, a raw unhandled Node.js exception is thrown with no
user-facing guidance.

---

**Actual behavior:**

The JSON Language Server client crashes with the following error:

```
JSON Language Server client: couldn't create connection to server.
Error: UNC host 'wsl.localhost' access is not allowed
  at lstat (node:fs:1640:10)
  at t.lstat (node:electron/js2c/node_init:2:5015)
  at c:\Program Files\Microsoft VS Code\<build-hash>\resources\app\
     extensions\json-language-features\client\dist\node\jsonClientMain.js:19:49494
  at new Promise (<anonymous>)
  at WT._getServerWorkingDir (jsonClientMain.js:19:49475)
  at WT.createMessageTransports (jsonClientMain.js:19:44240)
  at WT.createConnection (jsonClientMain.js:16:11289)
  at WT.start (jsonClientMain.js...)
```

---

**Root cause (analysis):**

The Agents Window is a Windows-native Electron/Node.js process.
When a WSL workspace path (`\\wsl.localhost\...` UNC) is passed to
the JSON Language Server client, it calls `lstat` via `node:fs`.
Node.js on Windows disallows UNC host access for `wsl.localhost`
at the OS level — so the call fails unconditionally.

Unlike the main VS Code editor window (which uses the Remote-WSL
extension to tunnel through `vscode-server` running inside WSL),
the Agents Window has no equivalent WSL remote transport.

---

**Additional context:**

- WSL2 is a primary development environment for a large segment of
  Windows developers. The lack of WSL support in the Agents Window
  is a significant gap, especially since the main editor window
  supports it fully via the Remote-WSL extension.
- The SSH-to-WSL workaround (enabling an SSH server inside WSL and
  connecting via the Agents Window Remote → SSH tab) exists but
  requires manual setup that is not practical in managed/corporate
  environments.
- Labels: `bug`, `agents-window`
author: arvindk1

title: Agents window: cannot open WSL remote projects (path resolution fails)
body:
## Summary

The Agents window (Preview, introduced in 1.120) cannot work with WSL-based projects.
When attempting to open a project that lives inside a WSL filesystem, the window
fails to resolve paths, reporting that the path cannot be found.

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Open VS Code on Windows with the Remote - WSL extension installed.
2. Connect to a WSL distro and open a project (e.g. `/home/user/myproject`).
3. Click **"Open in Agents"** from the title bar (or open the Agents window manually).
4. Try to add the WSL project as a session target.

## Expected Behavior

The Agents window should be able to connect to WSL the same way a regular
Remote - WSL window does, and run agents against projects on the Linux filesystem.

## Actual Behavior

The window runs in the Windows host context and cannot resolve Linux-side paths
(e.g. `/home/user/myproject`). An error is shown indicating the path cannot be found.

## Environment

- VS Code version: 1.120.0
- OS: Windows (with WSL2, Ubuntu 24.04)
- Remote - WSL extension: latest

## Additional Context

The regular VS Code Remote - WSL window works correctly for Copilot Chat agent
mode. The limitation appears to be that the Agents window does not inherit or
establish a remote connection (WSL / SSH / Dev Containers) the way a standard
editor window does.

Being able to run agents on WSL projects is important for developers who do all
their work inside WSL. The workaround of accessing WSL files via `\\wsl$\...`
UNC paths is unreliable and has significant I/O performance penalties.

## Suggested Solution

Allow the Agents window to establish WSL (and more broadly, Remote extension)
connections per-session, similar to how the regular editor window does. The
existing "run agents on remote machines" direction mentioned in the 1.120 release
notes suggests this is already on the roadmap.

**Labels to add:** `feature-request`, `agents-window`, `remote-wsl`

`Failed to install plugin 'https://github.com/xxx/yyy': spawn git ENOENT`

I use VSCode Remote-SSH, so I've also tried to put this entry in .vscode-server/data/User/settings.json, but this setting doesn't seem to be loaded by VSCode.
I would expect VSCode to invoke git on the remote server and not locally.

- VS Code Version: 1.118.1
- OS Version: Windows 11

Steps to Reproduce:

1. Use Remote SSH
2. Add any plugin marketplace in VSCode settings
3. VSCode will invoke git locally and not on remote server

title: ChatDebugFileLogger fails on Windows: EINVAL mkdir for path with trailing ':' (copilotcli:)
body: Type: Bug VS Code Insiders fails to create the chat debug log directory because the directory name contains a trailing colon (`copilotcli:`), which is not a valid path character on Windows (NTFS reserves `:` for alternate data streams). `fs.mkdir` therefore returns `EINVAL`. ### Error ``` 2026-05-04 13:27:43.898 [error] [ChatDebugFileLogger] Failed to write debug log entries: Unknown (FileSystemError) (FileSystemError): Error: EINVAL: invalid argument, mkdir 'c:\Users\\AppData\Roaming\Agents - Insiders\User\workspaceStorage\-256c0f40\GitHub.copilot-chat\debug-logs\copilotcli:' ``` ### Steps to Reproduce 1. Use VS Code Insiders on Windows with Copilot Chat / `copilotcli` debug logging enabled. 2. Trigger any chat activity that causes `ChatDebugFileLogger` to write entries. 3. Observe repeated errors in the log. ### Expected The directory name should be sanitized (e.g. strip or replace `:`) so `mkdir` succeeds on Windows, and debug log entries are written. ### Actual `mkdir` fails with `EINVAL` because the path ends in `:`. No debug logs are written. ### Notes - The offending segment appears to be the session/agent identifier (`copilotcli:` — the trailing `:` looks like a separator that wasn't stripped before being used as a folder name). - Likely fix: sanitize identifiers used as path segments, especially on Windows where `< > : " / \ | ? *` are reserved. VS Code version: Insiders OS: Windows
author: bryanchen-d

title: Partial interface in English despite Chinese language pack installed
body:
Type: Bug 1. Install Visual Studio Code 1.118.1 (user setup) on Windows 11 (build 10.0.26200) 2. Install the official "Chinese (Simplified)" language pack from the Extensions panel 3. Set display language to `zh-CN` via `Configure Display Language` command and restart VS Code 4. Open VS Code and check the interface **Actual result**: Some interface elements (menu bar, settings, extension panels) are in Chinese, while others remain in English, and the issue persists after reinstalling the language pack and VS Code. **Expected result**: All VS Code interface elements should display in Simplified Chinese after setting the language and restarting.
