author: kieferrm

title: [Claude Mode] claude-sonnet-4.6 model ID rejected by Anthropic API - "model_not_supported"
body:
- Copilot Chat Extension Version: 0.40.1
- VS Code Version: 1.112.0
- OS Version: Windows 11 Pro 10.0.22631
- Feature: Claude agent mode
- Selected model: Claude Sonnet 4.6
- Logs: see below

Steps to Reproduce:

1. Open GitHub Copilot Chat in VSCode
2. Switch to "Claude" mode (bottom selector)
3. Select Claude Sonnet 4.6 as model
4. Send any message

Expected behavior:
The request is forwarded to Anthropic's API with the correct model ID `claude-sonnet-4-6` (hyphens).

Actual behavior:
The [Claude Copilot Proxy] sends `claude-sonnet-4.6` (dot instead of last hyphen) to Anthropic's API, which rejects it with `model_not_supported`.
Claude Haiku works fine, only Sonnet 4.6 is affected.

Root cause (from extension.js analysis):
The proxy's internal model registry stores `claude-sonnet-4.6` (with dot) as the Anthropic model ID for Claude Sonnet 4.6. Anthropic's API expects `claude-sonnet-4-6` (all hyphens).

Logs:
Server error: 400 {"error":{"message":"The requested model is not supported.","code":"model_not_supported","param":"model","type":"invalid_request_error"}}
ccreq:a3b51329.copilotmd | failed | claude-sonnet-4.6 | 124ms | [Claude Copilot Proxy]
Error: Error during execution
  at BO.handleResultMessage (extension.js:2136:11960)

title: Sessions: In-progress sessions don't show stop button
body: I'm running into https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/302551 quite a bit, and I struggle to tell if the session stopped prematurely (and I'm also potentially encountering an incorrect status message https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/302808#issuecomment-4100068525), or if it's still working, since even sessions that are still in-progress don't show a stop button. Example session that's still working but doesn't include a stop button:

title: Terminal background color from workbench.colorCustomizations ignored when moved to new window (v1.112.0, Linux)
body:
Description:

## Environment
- OS: Linux
- VS Code version: 1.112.0 (commit 07ff9d6178ede9a1bd12ad3399074d726ebe6e43)
- First occurrence: March 19, 2026

## Issue
Terminal background color defined in `workbench.colorCustomizations` works correctly in the integrated terminal, but is lost when the terminal is moved to a separate window.

## Steps to reproduce
1. Configure `workbench.colorCustomizations` with a `terminal.background` color
2. Open integrated terminal → background color displays correctly ✅
3. Move terminal to new window (right-click terminal tab → "Move Terminal to New Window") → background color is lost ❌

## Settings (relevant excerpt)
```json
"workbench.colorCustomizations": {
  "terminal.background": "#0f0f0f"
}

title: Feature Request: Support ACP in Copilot Chat to combine best-in-class diff review with broader agent ecosystems
body: # Feature Request: Support ACP in Copilot Chat to combine best-in-class diff review with broader agent ecosystems I’d like to request first-class ACP support in Copilot Chat. Copilot Chat already has one of the best file-list diff experiences I’ve used across AI coding products. In particular, multi-file changes are significantly easier to review here than in many other CLI-based tools. That review UX is exactly why I want ACP support in Copilot Chat: I want to keep Copilot Chat’s superior review workflow while connecting to broader ACP-compatible agent/tool ecosystems. ## Problem Many AI CLIs can generate multi-file edits, but their post-change diff review is often harder to scan and trust at scale. This creates friction in real workflows where review quality matters as much as generation quality. Today, without ACP support in Copilot Chat, users may have to choose between: 1. Better ecosystem interoperability (in external ACP-capable tools), or 2. Better multi-file diff review ergonomics (in Copilot Chat) ## Proposed Feature Please add ACP support in Copilot Chat, including: - Ability to connect to ACP-compatible providers/servers - Discovery and invocation of ACP-exposed tools/actions - Consistent auth/session handling for ACP integrations - Clear permission/security controls for ACP calls - Strong diagnostics for ACP request/response flows - UX that preserves Copilot Chat’s current file-list diff and review flow ## Why This Matters - Preserves Copilot Chat’s excellent review experience for multi-file edits - Reduces context switching between tools - Improves trust and review speed for large changes - Enables interoperability with open ACP ecosystems - Lowers integration cost for teams using heterogeneous agent stacks ## Ecosystem Signal From the JetBrains AI Assistant UI (as shown in the screenshot), there is an agent menu entry labeled **"Install From ACP Registry..."**. This strongly suggests ACP-style ecosystem integration is already becoming practical in mainstream IDE workflows, and increases the value of offering similar interoperability in Copilot Chat. ## Reference Open-source ACP extension: https://github.com/formulahendry/vscode-acp.git Observed product signal: - JetBrains AI Assistant UI includes "Install From ACP Registry..." in the Agents menu (from screenshot evidence)
author: leliyou

Then the chat session seems to become corrupted and no chat messages work any more. Even when disk space is cleaned up and more disk space is available again, the chat session still always shows an error "Session file is corrupted (line 112: SyntaxError: Unterminated string in JSON at position 2632 (line 1 column 2633))" **Expected behaviour:** - When no disk space is available, it shows an error saying that no disk space is available - When more disk space is available again, it continues working normally **Actual behaviour**: - When no disk space is available, it shows a bit confusing error message: "Session file is corrupted" - When more disk space is available again, the specific chat is still permanently broken, and also can't be viewed anymore. One needs to create a new chat and start the task again from scratch.
author: SimonSiefke

title: Feature Request: Allow permanently hiding problems through json configuration.
body:
<!-- ⚠️⚠️ Do Not Delete This! feature_request_template ⚠️⚠️ -->
<!-- Please read our Rules of Conduct: https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/ -->
<!-- Please search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates. -->

<!-- Describe the feature you'd like. -->

There should be a `problems.exclude` configuration setting to permanently exclude certain files from the Problems panel. This should also prevent those problems from adding to the problem count.

For example, I often want to exclude the node modules from problems, but don't want to consistently have that as a filter.

Example configuration: 

```json
{
  "problems.exclude": {
    "**/node_modules/**
  }
}

title: Return key produces ANSI escape codes `^[[13u` in Terminal
body: On macOS using the fish shell, some terminal interactive prompts which require the user to hit the return key produce ANSI escape sequences instead of the desired "enter" result. Does this issue occur when all extensions are disabled?: Yes - VS Code Version: 1.111.0 - OS Version: macOS, Darwin arm64 24.6.0 Steps to Reproduce: 1. Launch VS Code without extensions from user terminal: ``` code --disable-extensions . ``` 2. Open the VS Code Terminal and make a new text file: ``` touch test.txt ``` 3. Attempt to delete the file interactively: ``` rm -i test.txt ```

title: Configure Tools window closes on scroll
body:
<!-- Please search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates -->
<!-- Please attach logs to help us diagnose your issue -->

- Copilot Chat Extension Version:
- VS Code Version: 1.111.0 (Universal) (Commit: ce099c1ed25d9eb3076c11e4a280f3eb52b4fbeb)
- OS Version: Darwin arm64 25.3.0
- Feature (e.g. agent/edit/ask mode): Configure Tools
- Selected model (e.g. GPT 4.1, Claude 3.7 Sonnet): GPT-5.4
- Logs: Doesn't show any logs?

Steps to Reproduce:

1. Open the `Configure Tools` selector next to the model selector
2. Scroll the tools list. Nothing unusual happens.
3. Select or unselect any of the tools.
4. Scroll the tools list. the `Configure Tools` window unexpectedly closes 😢 

When selecting `GitHub Copilot Log` in the output tab, no logs are shown. I also opened the developer tools and reproduced the bug, no errors are shown there either.

This is really annoying because I constantly have to readjust the tools anyway (which in itself is extremely cumbersome and annoying). But this bug makes it even harder to do so.

Find a screen recording attached that shows the bug.

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/8b6b9380-9634-478e-872a-eb5f64804b97

author: Isaac-Kingsley007

title: Extension Update Fails Silently - No Error Message Shown
body: ## Issue Description When attempting to update an extension from the Extensions panel, the update process fails without showing any error message to the user. ## Screenshot As shown in the screenshot, the Claude Code for VS Code extension displays an "Update" button, but clicking it results in a silent failure with no error feedback. ## Steps to Reproduce 1. Open VS Code 2. Navigate to the Extensions panel 3. Find an extension with an available update (showing "Update" button) 4. Click the "Update" button 5. The update appears to start but then fails silently ## Expected Behavior - The extension should update successfully, OR - If the update fails, a clear error message should be displayed explaining why ## Actual Behavior - The update button is clicked - No visual feedback or error message is shown - The extension remains at the old version - The "Update" button continues to be displayed ## Environment - VS Code Version: Latest stable - OS: macOS (based on screenshot UI) - Extension: Claude Code for VS Code (Anthropic) ## Additional Context The Extensions panel shows the update is available, but clicking the Update button does not complete the update process. This appears to be a general issue with the extension update mechanism rather than specific to any particular extension. ## Suggestions 1. Add proper error handling and user feedback when extension updates fail 2. Show a notification or inline error message explaining the failure reason 3. Provide retry options or troubleshooting steps
author: HomyeeKing

title: Extension installation fails: "End of central directory record signature not found" on both local and remote SSH
body:

Does this issue occur when all extensions are disabled?: Yes/No

<!-- 🪓 If you answered No above, use 'Help: Start Extension Bisect' from Command Palette to try to identify the cause. -->
<!-- 📣 Issues caused by an extension need to be reported directly to the extension publisher. The 'Help > Report Issue' dialog can assist with this. -->
- VS Code Version: 1.111.0 (user setup)
- OS Version: Windows_NT x64 10.0.26200

Steps to Reproduce:

1. Open VS Code (either locally or connect via Remote-SSH).
2. Go to the Extensions view.
3. Attempt to install ANY extension (e.g., Python, Claude, or even a simple Theme).
4. The installation progress bar starts, then immediately throws the error: End of central directory record signature not found. Either not a zip file, or file is truncated.

Expected Behavior:
The extension should be downloaded, verified, and extracted correctly.

Actual Behavior:
The ZIP extraction fails for every extension. This occurs consistently across different machines (Local and SSH), suggesting a potential issue with how the download stream is handled or cached.

title: Extension Signature Verification Failed: *, at custom-packaged nixos environment
body:
Type: Bug > 2026-03-16 09:32:00.233 [info] [Shared] Extension signature verification result for ms-dotnettools.csharp: UnknownError. Executed: false. Duration: 332ms. > 2026-03-16 09:32:00.235 [error] [Shared] Error while installing the extension ms-dotnettools.csharp Signature verification failed with 'UnknownError' error. vscode-userdata:/home/sob/.vscode-insiders/extensions/extensions.json > 2026-03-16 09:32:00.236 [error] [Shared] [uncaught exception in sharedProcess]: Signature verification failed with 'UnknownError' error.: SignatureVerificationInternal: Signature verification failed with 'UnknownError' error. title: Extension auto-update fails: downloaded .vsix is gzip-compressed but not decompressed
body:
<!-- ⚠️⚠️ Do Not Delete This! bug_report_template ⚠️⚠️ -->
<!-- Please read our Rules of Conduct: https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/ -->
<!-- 🕮 Read our guide about submitting issues: https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/wiki/Submitting-Bugs-and-Suggestions -->
<!-- 🔎 Search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates. -->
<!-- 🧪 Test using the latest Insiders build to see if your issue has already been fixed: https://code.visualstudio.com/insiders/ -->
<!-- 💡 Instead of creating your report here, use 'Report Issue' from the 'Help' menu in VS Code to pre-fill useful information. -->
<!-- 🔧 Launch with `code --disable-extensions` to check. -->

<!-- 🪓 If you answered No above, use 'Help: Start Extension Bisect' from Command Palette to try to identify the cause. -->
<!-- 📣 Issues caused by an extension need to be reported directly to the extension publisher. The 'Help > Report Issue' dialog can assist with this. -->

## Summary

VS Code fails to install/update extensions from the Marketplace with the error `End of central directory record signature not found. Either not a zip file, or file is truncated.` The root cause is that the Marketplace CDN returns `.vsix` files with `Content-Encoding: gzip`, but VS Code's download handler does not decompress the response body, resulting in a gzip stream being written to disk instead of the expected ZIP archive.

## VS Code Version

```
Version: 1.98.x (latest stable as of 2026-03-15)
OS: Linux x86_64 (Ubuntu)
```

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Configure a SOCKS5 or HTTP proxy in `settings.json`:
   ```json
   "http.proxy": "socks5://127.0.0.1:16668"
   ```
   (also reproducible with `"http.proxy": "http://127.0.0.1:8080"`)

2. Let VS Code auto-update extensions (or manually install via `code --install-extension`).

3. Observe repeated errors in the log (Help → Toggle Developer Tools → Console, or Window log):
   ```
   [error] End of central directory record signature not found.
   Either not a zip file, or file is truncated.
   ```

4. The same extensions can be downloaded correctly using `curl --compressed`:
   ```bash
   # Without --compressed: saves raw gzip stream (BROKEN)
   curl -x socks5h://127.0.0.1:16668 -L -o /tmp/ext.vsix \
     "https://marketplace.visualstudio.com/_apis/public/gallery/publishers/tuttieee/vsextensions/emacs-mcx/latest/vspackage"
   file /tmp/ext.vsix
   # => gzip compressed data, max speed, from FAT filesystem (MS-DOS, OS/2, NT)

   # With --compressed: correctly decompresses (WORKS)
   curl -x socks5h://127.0.0.1:16668 -L --compressed -o /tmp/ext.vsix \
     "https://marketplace.visualstudio.com/_apis/public/gallery/publishers/tuttieee/vsextensions/emacs-mcx/latest/vspackage"
   file /tmp/ext.vsix
   # => Zip archive data, at least v2.0 to extract

   code --install-extension /tmp/ext.vsix
   # => Extension 'emacs-mcx.vsix' was successfully installed.
   ```

## Root Cause Analysis

The Marketplace API endpoint returns responses with the following headers:

```http
content-type: application/vsix; api-version=7.2-preview.1
content-encoding: gzip
```

The `Content-Encoding: gzip` header indicates transport-level compression. Per HTTP/1.1 (RFC 7231 §3.1.2.2), the client **must** decode the content-encoding before processing the payload.

VS Code's extension download path (via Electron/Node.js) does not handle this decompression. The raw gzip-compressed bytes are written directly as the `.vsix` file. Since `.vsix` is a ZIP archive, the `yauzl` library then fails to parse it.

**Verification that this is proxy-independent:**

```bash
# Both proxy types exhibit the same behavior:

# SOCKS5 proxy
curl -x socks5h://127.0.0.1:16668 -L -v -o /dev/null \
  "https://...vspackage" 2>&1 | grep content-encoding
# => content-encoding: gzip

# HTTP proxy
curl -x http://127.0.0.1:8080 -L -v -o /dev/null \
  "https://...vspackage" 2>&1 | grep content-encoding
# => content-encoding: gzip
```

## Affected Extensions (examples)

All Marketplace extensions are affected. Tested with:

- `ms-python.python`
- `ms-python.vscode-python-envs`
- `saoudrizwan.claude-dev`
- `tuttieee.emacs-mcx`

## Relevant Log Output

```
2026-03-15 13:45:16.060 [info] Auto updating outdated extensions. ms-python.python, ms-python.vscode-python-envs, saoudrizwan.claude-dev, tuttieee.emacs-mcx
2026-03-15 13:45:21.244 [error] End of central directory record signature not found. Either not a zip file, or file is truncated.: Error: ...
    at Fb (file:///usr/share/code/resources/app/out/vs/code/electron-utility/sharedProcess/sharedProcessMain.js:450:28499)
    ...
    at /usr/share/code/resources/app/node_modules/yauzl/index.js:40:7
```

(Error repeats in a loop as VS Code retries the failed downloads.)

## Expected Behavior

VS Code should transparently decompress `Content-Encoding: gzip` responses before writing the `.vsix` file to disk, or send an `Accept-Encoding` header that prevents the server from applying content-encoding to binary payloads.

## Workaround

Download extensions manually using `curl --compressed` and install via CLI:

```bash
curl -L --compressed -o /tmp/ext.vsix \
  "https://marketplace.visualstudio.com/_apis/public/gallery/publishers/PUBLISHER/vsextensions/NAME/latest/vspackage"
code --install-extension /tmp/ext.vsix
```

title: [macOS] Extension installation fails with 'End of central directory record signature not found' error
body:
## Description

When trying to install extensions through the VS Code Extensions Marketplace GUI, the installation consistently fails with the error:
```
Error: End of central directory record signature not found. Either not a zip file, or file is truncated.
```

However, installing the same extensions via the command line (`code --install-extension <extension-id>`) works perfectly.

## Environment

- **VS Code Version**: 1.111.0 (ce099c1ed25d9eb3076c11e4a280f3eb52b4fbeb)
- **OS Version**: macOS 26.3.1 (Build 25D2128)
- **Architecture**: arm64 (Apple Silicon)

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Open VS Code
2. Go to Extensions panel (Cmd+Shift+X)
3. Search for any extension (e.g., `ms-python.python`)
4. Click Install
5. Error appears: "End of central directory record signature not found"

## Expected Behavior

Extension should install successfully.

## Actual Behavior

Extension installation fails with ZIP file error.

## Workaround

Installing via command line works:
```bash
code --install-extension ms-python.python
```

## Additional Information

### Network Tests
- Direct download via curl: ✅ File is complete and valid
- Download via proxy (Clash Verge): ✅ File is complete and valid
- CLI installation: ✅ Works
- GUI installation: ❌ Fails

### File Validation
Downloaded VSIX files are valid:
```bash
$ unzip -t ms-python-python.vsix
No errors detected in compressed data.
```

### Node.js yauzl Module Test
The yauzl module can successfully open and read the VSIX file:
```javascript
yauzl.open(file, (err, zipfile) => {
  console.log('File count:', zipfile.entryCount); // Works correctly
});
```

This suggests the issue is in VS Code's internal download/extract pipeline, not in the yauzl library itself or the network layer.

### What I Tried
1. Reinstalling VS Code (via Homebrew)
2. Completely removing VS Code and all config files
3. Clearing all caches
4. Disabling/enabling proxy
5. Different network configurations (direct vs proxy)
6. Creating `~/.vscode-server/extensions` directory manually
7. VS Code Insiders (1.112.0) - same issue

None of these resolved the GUI installation issue.

## Error Stack Trace

```
Error: End of central directory record signature not found. Either not a zip file, or file is truncated.
    at Ob (file:///Applications/Visual%20Studio%20Code.app/Contents/Resources/app/out/vs/code/electron-utility/sharedProcess/sharedProcessMain.js:450:28625)
    at file:///Applications/Visual%20Studio%20Code.app/Contents/Resources/app/out/vs/code/electron-utility/sharedProcess/sharedProcessMain.js:450:29929
    at /Applications/Visual Studio Code.app/Contents/Resources/app/node_modules/yauzl/index.js:40:7
    at /Applications/Visual Studio Code.app/Contents/Resources/app/node_modules/yauzl/index.js:190:5
    at /Applications/Visual Studio Code.app/Contents/Resources/app/node_modules/yauzl/index.js:712:5
    at /Applications/Visual Studio Code.app/Contents/Resources/app/node_modules/yauzl/fd-slicer.js:33:7
    at FSReqCallback.wrapper [as oncomplete] (node:fs:671:5)
```

## Logs

```
[error] [Network] https://marketplace.visualstudio.com/_apis/public/gallery/extensionquery - error POST Canceled
[error] [Window] Error: End of central directory record signature not found.
```

The issue appears to be in the `sharedProcessMain.js` file, specifically in the extension download/extraction pipeline.

## Related

This might be related to how VS Code handles the download stream in the Electron renderer process. The file appears to be corrupted during download, but only when using the GUI installer, not when using CLI or external tools.

ProductName:		macOS
ProductVersion:		15.7.3
BuildVersion:		24G419

Steps to Reproduce:

1. Open VS Code (any of the last 3 versions).  
2. Wait for the extension host and cloud sync initialization.  
3. Observe in the log the error:  

```text
Error: End of central directory record signature not found. Either not a zip file, or file is truncated.

    ...


Expected Behavior:

VS Code should start normally without zip/extraction errors.

Actual Behavior:

VS Code logs the error about “End of central directory record signature not found” and some features (like Settings Sync) fail to initialize properly.

Error seems related to the internal zip extraction of extensions or cloud sync data.

Additional Notes:

Happens even when all extensions are disabled (code --disable-extensions).

The error appears in the temporary AppTranslocation folder on macOS, which may indicate an issue with macOS Gatekeeper/App Translocation.

The problem occurs consistently across the last 3 VS Code releases.

title: Allow GitHub account preferences per profile for extensions (e.g. GitHub Copilot)
body: Type: Feature Request VS Code version: 1.110.1 (commit 61b3d0ab13be7dda2389f1d3e60a119c7f660cc3, x64) OS: Ubuntu 24.04.2 LTS, kernel 6.17.0-14-generic Relevant extensions installed: GitHub.copilot (built-in via VS Code) GitHub.copilot-chat vscode.github-authentication (built-in) Feature request: Currently, the preferred GitHub account assigned to an extension is stored globally and shared across all profiles. Switching profiles does not change the active account used by extensions like GitHub Copilot. Expected behavior: Each VS Code profile should store its own preferred GitHub account per extension, so switching profiles automatically uses the account associated with that profile — without manual intervention via "Manage Extension Account Preferences". Example use case: Profile Default → GitHub account personal-user → personal Copilot license Profile Work → GitHub account work-user → corporate Copilot license Reproducible steps: Sign in to VS Code with two different GitHub accounts (e.g. user-a and user-b), both having a Copilot license. Create two profiles: Default and Work. In profile Default, open the account menu (bottom-left) → "Manage Extension Account Preferences" → assign user-a to GitHub Copilot and GitHub Copilot Chat. Switch to profile Work → open the account menu → "Manage Extension Account Preferences". Assign user-b to GitHub Copilot and GitHub Copilot Chat. Switch back to profile Default. What you expect to see: Copilot uses user-a automatically in profile Default. What you actually see: Copilot still uses user-b (the last account set), regardless of the active profile. The account preference is not persisted per profile. Root cause (investigated): The key github.copilot-github is stored in the global ~/.config/Code/User/globalStorage/state.vscdb, not in the per-profile database at ~/.config/Code/User/profiles//globalStorage/state.vscdb. VS Code does not read or write this preference from the profile-scoped database when switching profiles. Current workaround: The user must manually reassign the preferred account every time they switch profiles, which defeats the purpose of profiles for multi-identity workflows. Why this matters: Profiles are designed to provide isolated, context-specific environments. Authentication preferences for extensions are a natural part of that context. Without per-profile account binding, users with multiple GitHub identities (e.g. personal + enterprise Copilot licenses) cannot benefit from profiles for account isolation. Dev Tools Console errors: None — this is a missing feature, not an error condition. VS Code version: Code 1.110.1 (61b3d0ab13be7dda2389f1d3e60a119c7f660cc3, 2026-03-06T23:03:27.520Z) OS version: Linux x64 6.17.0-14-generic snap Modes:

author: Limpidjunewind

title: `fontLigatures` stopped working when **Editor: Experimental GPU Acceleration** is enabled
body:
## Description

When the “Editor: Experimental GPU Acceleration” setting is turned on, the editor stops rendering
font ligatures. Even with a clearly ligature‑capable monospace font (e.g. Cascadia Code, Fira Code,
JetBrains Mono, etc.) selected and `editor.fontLigatures` set to `true`, sequences like `!=`, `=>`,
`->` are displayed as separate characters. Turning the experimental GPU acceleration off causes
ligatures to return immediately.

This can be reproduced reliably with the steps below.

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Add/ensure the following user settings in `settings.json`:

   ```json
   {
       "editor.fontFamily": "Cascadia Code, Consolas, monospace",
       "editor.fontLigatures": true,
       "editor.experimentalGpuAcceleration": "on"
   }
   ```

2. Restart VS Code.

3. Open any source file (.c/.cpp/.js/etc.) and type:

   ```c
   if (a != b && c == d) return ptr->field;
   ```

4. Observe that `!=`, `==`, `->` do **not** turn into ligatures.

5. In settings, set **Editor: Experimental GPU Acceleration** to `off` and restart the editor.

6. Type the same line again; all of the above glyphs now render as single ligatures.

## Actual Behaviour

With GPU acceleration enabled, font ligatures are ignored and the text falls back to the
backup font (Consolas), resulting in:

```
!=    ->    ==    ...
```

displayed as ordinary characters.

## Expected Behaviour

Ligatures should be honoured regardless of the GPU acceleration setting.
`editor.fontLigatures: true` and the selected font’s OpenType features should be respected even
when rendering is done on the GPU.

## Environment

- VS Code version: `1.91.0` (issue present on latest insiders/main as well)
- OS: Windows 11 23H2
- GPU: NVIDIA GTX 1650 / Intel UHD 750 (issue reproduced on multiple GPUs/drivers)
- Font used: `Cascadia Code PL 2107.01` (also tested with Fira Code, JetBrains Mono, etc.)
- Relevant settings:

  ```json
  "editor.fontFamily": "Cascadia Code, Consolas, monospace",
  "editor.fontLigatures": true,
  "editor.experimentalGpuAcceleration": "on"
  ```

## Additional Context

- In DevTools the computed `font-family` is correctly `"Cascadia Code"`, and
  `font-feature-settings` shows `"liga","calt"`, so the issue lies in the rendering pipeline,
  not font selection.
- Disabling GPU acceleration immediately fixes the problem.
- Unchecked on Linux/macOS.

Please investigate whether the GPU rendering path skips font-feature-settings or loads a
non‑ligature variant of the font.  
Thanks!
author: MOYUtianming

So, why can't the 'install from file' option use the *.VSIXPackage file directly?

title: GitHub Copilot extension v0.39.0 incompatible with VS Code 1.111
body:
<!-- ⚠️⚠️ Do Not Delete This! bug_report_template ⚠️⚠️ -->
<!-- Please read our Rules of Conduct: https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/ -->
<!-- 🕮 Read our guide about submitting issues: https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/wiki/Submitting-Bugs-and-Suggestions -->
<!-- 🔎 Search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates. -->
<!-- 🧪 Test using the latest Insiders build to see if your issue has already been fixed: https://code.visualstudio.com/insiders/ -->
<!-- 💡 Instead of creating your report here, use 'Report Issue' from the 'Help' menu in VS Code to pre-fill useful information. -->
<!-- 🔧 Launch with `code --disable-extensions` to check. -->
Does this issue occur when all extensions are disabled?: Yes/No - NA as this is issue with Vs code to copilot only.

<!-- 🪓 If you answered No above, use 'Help: Start Extension Bisect' from Command Palette to try to identify the cause. -->
<!-- 📣 Issues caused by an extension need to be reported directly to the extension publisher. The 'Help > Report Issue' dialog can assist with this. -->
After updating to the latest version of VS Code (1.111), the GitHub Copilot extension v0.39.0 becomes incompatible and is disabled by the editor.

VS Code reports that the extension is not compatible with the current version of the editor even though both components are the latest available versions at the time of writing.

This prevents using Copilot functionality such as inline suggestions and Copilot Chat in the editor.

- Copilot Chat Extension Version: 0.39.0
- VS Code Version: 1.111.0
- OS Version: Windows_NT x64 10.0.26100

**Steps to Reproduce:**

1. Install or update to VS Code 1.111
2. Install GitHub Copilot extension v0.39.0
3. Restart VS Code if prompted
4. Open the Extensions view
2. It doesn't anymore now, with message invalid extension detected

**Expected Behavior**
GitHub Copilot extension should load normally and provide inline suggestions and Copilot Chat functionality.

**Actual Behavior**
VS Code shows that the extension is incompatible with the current version and disables it automatically.

As a result:
Copilot suggestions are not available
Copilot Chat cannot be used
Extension appears disabled due to incompatibility


**Additional Context**
This issue appears to occur immediately after updating VS Code to version 1.111 with Copilot extension version 0.39.0 installed.
author: arvindkannan

title: PowerShell terminal auto-approve misparses --arg=value options and prompts for approval despite matching allowlist
body:
Type: Bug Steps to Reproduce: 1. Add a `git show` allow rule to `chat.tools.terminal.autoApprove`, for example: ```json { "chat.tools.terminal.autoApprove": { "/^git(\\s+(-C\\s+\\S+|--no-pager))*\\s+show\\b/": true } } ``` 2. Using the chat terminal tool in a PowerShell-backed terminal, run: ```powershell git -C c:/git/repro-repo show --stat --summary --format=fuller 398d21f64d663 ``` 3. Observe that VS Code asks for approval even though the command matches the `git show` allowlist rule. 4. Check the terminal log. The command is parsed as two subcommands: ```text ["git -C c:/git/repro-repo show --stat --summary --format", "398d21f64d663"] ``` 5. Compare with this workaround command: ```powershell git -C c:/git/repro-repo show --stat --summary "--format=fuller" 398d21f64d663 ``` 6. Observe that the quoted version is parsed as a single subcommand and auto-approves correctly. Expected behavior: The original command should be treated as a single PowerShell subcommand and auto-approved by the existing `git show` allowlist rule. Actual behavior: The auto-approve analyzer drops the `fuller` value from `--format=fuller`, splits the commit hash into a separate pseudo-command, and treats the overall command as not auto-approved. Relevant log excerpt: ```text 2026-03-10 11:59:59.779 [info] RunInTerminalTool#CommandLineAutoApproveAnalyzer: Parsed sub-commands via powershell grammar [["git -C c:/git/repro-repo show --stat --summary --format","398d21f64d663"]] 2026-03-10 11:59:59.780 [info] RunInTerminalTool#CommandLineAutoApproveAnalyzer: All sub-commands NOT auto-approved [null] 2026-03-10 11:59:59.780 [info] RunInTerminalTool#CommandLineAutoApproveAnalyzer: Command line NOT auto-approved [null] 2026-03-10 11:59:59.780 [info] RunInTerminalTool#CommandLineAutoApproveAnalyzer: - Command 'git -C c:/git/repro-repo show --stat --summary --format' is approved by allow list rule: /^git(\s+(-C\s+\S+|--no-pager))*\s+show\b/ [null] 2026-03-10 11:59:59.780 [info] RunInTerminalTool#CommandLineAutoApproveAnalyzer: - Command '398d21f64d663' has no matching auto approve entries [null] 2026-03-10 11:59:59.780 [info] RunInTerminalTool#CommandLineAutoApproveAnalyzer: - Command line 'git -C c:/git/repro-repo show --stat --summary --format=fuller 398d21f64d663' has no matching auto approve entries [null] 2026-03-10 15:41:47.679 [info] RunInTerminalTool#CommandLineAutoApproveAnalyzer: Parsed sub-commands via powershell grammar [["git -C c:/git/repro-repo show --stat --summary \"--format=fuller\" 398d21f64d663"]] 2026-03-10 15:41:47.679 [info] RunInTerminalTool#CommandLineAutoApproveAnalyzer: All sub-commands auto-approved [null] ``` Additional context: - There are no `matchCommandLine: true` entries involved in this repro. The existing `git show` subcommand allowlist rule should be enough. - This appears to be in VS Code's terminal auto-approve parsing rather than in the allowlist regex itself. - Possibly related, but not the same repro: #261794, #270612, #280390, #296145 VS Code version: Code 1.111.0 (ce099c1ed25d9eb3076c11e4a280f3eb52b4fbeb, 2026-03-06T23:06:10Z) OS version: Windows_NT x64 10.0.26200 Modes:

title: Bug: "Configure Tools..." dialog in Copilot Chat sometimes closes unexpectedly while scrolling
body: - Copilot Chat Extension Version: 0.39.0 - VS Code Version: 1.111.0 - OS Version: macOS 26.3.1 (25D2128) - Feature (e.g. agent/edit/ask mode): all modes - Selected model (e.g. GPT 4.1, Claude 3.7 Sonnet): all **Type:** Bug **Component:** Copilot Chat / Agent tools configuration #### Description When configuring tools for an agent through Copilot Chat, the **"Configure Tools..."** dialog sometimes closes unexpectedly while scrolling through the list of tools. Clicking **"Configure Tools..."** correctly opens a dialog that allows selecting or deselecting available tools. However, after toggling some tool selections, scrolling up or down with the mouse occasionally causes the dialog to disappear abruptly without any confirmation action. When this happens: - The dialog closes immediately. - None of the changes made in the dialog are saved. - All selections made during that session are discarded. This forces the configuration process to be repeated. Because the dialog may close at any time, the only reliable workaround is to enable tools **one category at a time** (e.g., selecting all *Python* tools, closing the dialog, reopening it, then selecting another category). Although this is a relatively small issue, it becomes frustrating during repeated configuration. #### Steps to Reproduce 1. Open **Copilot Chat**. 2. Configure or edit an agent. 3. Click **"Configure Tools..."**. 4. Select or deselect several tools in the dialog. 5. Scroll up or down in the tool list using the mouse. #### Expected Behavior The dialog should remain open while scrolling until the user explicitly confirms or cancels the configuration. Version: 1.111.0 Commit: ce099c1ed25d9eb3076c11e4a280f3eb52b4fbeb Date: 2026-03-06T23:06:10Z Electron: 39.6.0 ElectronBuildId: 13330601 Chromium: 142.0.7444.265 Node.js: 22.22.0 V8: 14.2.231.22-electron.0 OS: Darwin arm64 25.3.0
author: singularitti

title: Bug? integrated terminal font rendering appears thin / poorly antialiased in light themes on macOS
body:
<!-- ⚠️⚠️ Do Not Delete This! bug_report_template ⚠️⚠️ -->
<!-- Please read our Rules of Conduct: https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/ -->
<!-- 🕮 Read our guide about submitting issues: https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/wiki/Submitting-Bugs-and-Suggestions -->
<!-- 🔎 Search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates. -->
<!-- 🧪 Test using the latest Insiders build to see if your issue has already been fixed: https://code.visualstudio.com/insiders/ -->
<!-- 💡 Instead of creating your report here, use 'Report Issue' from the 'Help' menu in VS Code to pre-fill useful information. -->
<!-- 🔧 Launch with `code --disable-extensions` to check. -->
Does this issue occur when all extensions are disabled?: Yes/No

<!-- 🪓 If you answered No above, use 'Help: Start Extension Bisect' from Command Palette to try to identify the cause. -->
<!-- 📣 Issues caused by an extension need to be reported directly to the extension publisher. The 'Help > Report Issue' dialog can assist with this. -->

**Type:** Bug  
**Platform:** macOS  
**VS Code Version:** 1.111.0 (observed in recent updates)  
**Environment:** Occurs across multiple MacBook devices with different screen resolutions

### Description

In recent versions of VS Code, the integrated terminal appears to have a font rendering issue when using **light themes** on **macOS**.

When both the **editor theme and terminal theme are light**, terminal fonts appear **overly thin with visible pixel zig-zag artifacts**, suggesting that font antialiasing is not being applied correctly. This significantly reduces readability.

This behavior does **not occur** when:

- Using **dark editor themes**
- Using a **light editor theme with a dark terminal theme**

The issue appears to affect **all light themes tested**, not just a specific theme.

### Observed Effects

- Terminal text appears **very thin and jagged**
- **Antialiasing artifacts** are visible
- **Powerline / Nerd Fonts icons** become difficult to distinguish
- **TUI box-drawing characters** become hard to read

Examples:

- The **frame around the “OpenAI Codex” banner** in the screenshot becomes almost illegible.
- Similar rendering artifacts appear in other terminal-based tools that rely on text UI elements.

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/86ae5ba6-3a72-47fd-9266-c2c122cafb63)

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/5f9b5d31-687d-4566-a5b7-58332ae16082)

### Steps to Reproduce

1. Use **macOS**
2. Install the latest version of **VS Code**
3. Select a **light editor theme**
4. Use a **light terminal theme**
5. Open the **integrated terminal**
6. Use a terminal font such as **Nerd Fonts / Powerline fonts**
7. Run a TUI application (example: Codex CLI)

### Expected Behavior

Terminal fonts should render with proper **antialiasing and normal stroke thickness**, consistent with how they appear:

- In **dark VS Code themes**
- In **native macOS Terminal or iTerm**
- In **VS Code when using dark terminal themes**

### Troubleshooting Attempted

The following were tested but **did not resolve the issue**:

- Disabling macOS system font antialiasing
- Enabling or disabling terminal font weight settings
- Setting the following in `settings.json`:

```json
"workbench.fontAliasing": "antialiased"
```

- Testing across **multiple MacBook devices**
- Testing across **different display resolutions**
- Testing with **multiple light themes**

title: Typing Backspace, Space, Ctrl +C brokes in bash shell when bash is ran inside fish shell
body:
<!-- ⚠️⚠️ Do Not Delete This! bug_report_template ⚠️⚠️ -->
<!-- Please read our Rules of Conduct: https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/ -->

Does this issue occur when all extensions are disabled?: Yes

<!-- 🪓 If you answered No above, use 'Help: Start Extension Bisect' from Command Palette to try to identify the cause. -->
<!-- 📣 Issues caused by an extension need to be reported directly to the extension publisher. The 'Help > Report Issue' dialog can assist with this. -->
Version: 1.110.1
Commit: 61b3d0ab13be7dda2389f1d3e60a119c7f660cc3
Date: 2026-03-06T23:03:27.520Z
Electron: 39.6.0
ElectronBuildId: 13330601
Chromium: 142.0.7444.265
Node.js: 22.22.0
V8: 14.2.231.22-electron.0
OS: Linux x64 6.18.16-200.fc43.x86_64

**Steps to Reproduce:**

1. Set the default shell to `fish` (fish version: 4.2.0)
2. Run `bash` or `docker run --rm -it ubuntu bash`
3. Try typing `Space`, `Ctrl + C` or `Backspace`

**Expected results:**

Terminal work normally

**Actual results:**

Space becomes `u`
Backspace becomes `7u`
Ctrl + C becomes `9;5u`
author: lephuongbg

title: Confusion in delegating to background sessions from Local (the new session is not displayed)
body:
I now have a different issue with planning and the Copilot CLI: after sending the plan implementation to the Copilot CLI, I end up with 2 chats: one for the local plan agent (that still shows as if it's working), and a new one for the CLI that isn't cross-linked to that original session. I wouldn't know about the new session without manually opening the sessions list (as there's no cross-linking, and the original session remains in a working status).

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/aebd4a5a-2b0a-4732-a9a6-6901e3ace3ee

***

Also as a slightly tangential issue: if I go back to the sessions list and then return to a plan which I haven't yet implemented, I lose the UI button for `Start implementation`. This makes multi-agent management feel challenging (since I feel like I lose some guidance if I go check on other sessions before deciding to implement the plan).
author: DonJayamanne

title: VS Code Search Not Working error "A System error occurred (Spawn Unknown)”
body: VS Code Search has been working normally in the same Managed Virtual Desktop environment for over a year. Around mid‑February (Feb 11–12), after a VS Code update, the Search functionality started failing consistently. The issue occurs in both VS Code Desktop and VS Code Web and reproduces across multiple regions. I initially expected the issue to be resolved in a subsequent update, but it is still present. However, the same VS Code versions work correctly on non‑VM (corporate network) devices, where the Search functionality behaves as expected. current version: Version: 1.111.0 (system setup) Commit: ce099c1ed25d9eb3076c11e4a280f3eb52b4fbeb Date: 2026-03-06T23:06:10Z Electron: 39.6.0 ElectronBuildId: 13330601 Chromium: 142.0.7444.265 Node.js: 22.22.0 V8: 14.2.231.22-electron.0 OS: Windows_NT x64 10.0.26100 Error:

title: Control characters in terminal broken (with fish as default)
body: Does this issue occur when all extensions are disabled?: Yes - VS Code Version: 1.110.1 - OS Version: Fedora Linux 43 Steps to Reproduce: 1. Open VS Code Terminal with fish as your default shell 2. `ssh` to a remote server 3. Type `echo test` 4. Observe as control characters such as space, return, or backspace fail to execute. Alternative steps: 1. Open VS Code Terminal with fish as your default shell 2. Launch `bash` 3. Type `echo test` 4. Observe as control characters such as space, return, or backspace fail to execute.

title: Interactive terminal prompts (Enter, Ctrl+C) unresponsive in raw-mode stdin — regression in 1.110
body:
## Bug Report

### VS Code Version
- **Broken:** 1.110.1 (`61b3d0ab13be7dda2389f1d3e60a119c7f660cc3`, arm64)
- **Working:** 1.109.x

### OS
macOS (Apple Silicon / arm64)

### Shell
fish

### Description

After updating to VS Code 1.110.1, **Enter** and **Ctrl+C** no longer register in the integrated terminal when a CLI tool is using raw-mode stdin for interactive prompts. This is a regression — the same setup works correctly in 1.109.

### Steps to Reproduce

1. Open VS Code 1.110.1 integrated terminal (fish shell)
2. Run any CLI tool that uses raw-mode stdin interactive prompts, for example:
   - `npx npm-check-updates --interactive` (uses inquirer/prompts)
   - Any CLI built on `@clack/prompts` or `inquirer`
3. When the interactive prompt appears, press **Enter** to confirm a selection — nothing happens
4. Press **Ctrl+C** to cancel — nothing happens
5. The terminal becomes unresponsive to these keys while the interactive prompt is active

### Expected Behavior

Enter confirms the selection, Ctrl+C cancels the process — as it does in external terminals and in VS Code 1.109.

### Actual Behavior

Enter and Ctrl+C are not received by the process. The terminal appears stuck. Arrow keys and other input may still work, but confirmation/cancellation keys are swallowed.

### Troubleshooting Done

All of the following were tested individually and in combination — **none resolved the issue:**

- `"terminal.integrated.suggest.enabled": false`
- `"terminal.integrated.shellIntegration.enabled": false` (and `true`)
- `"terminal.integrated.sendKeybindingsToShell": true` (and `false`)
- `"terminal.integrated.gpuAcceleration": "off"`
- `"keyboard.dispatch": "code"` (and removed entirely)
- Launching with `code --disable-extensions` — **issue persists**, ruling out extensions
- **Downgrading to VS Code 1.109 resolves the issue immediately**

### Environment

The same interactive prompts work perfectly in:
- iTerm2 (external terminal)
- VS Code 1.109.x integrated terminal

No custom keybindings on bare `Enter` or `Ctrl+C`.
author: jimblue

title: Copilot: read tools cannot read/view image files
body:
Type: Bug 1) add a `test.png` file to your project 2) prompt `what do you see in the file test.png` -> Response: ``` I can't directly view image files — my tools only read text-based files. The file test.png is a binary image that I'm unable to interpret. ``` My scenario is that I have skill that can create diagrams. To validate the layout of the generated diagram - the skill instructs the model to generate a `.png` file - the skill instructs reading the generated png file and validate some layout constraints. These steps are done in a loop without user interaction until the layout constraints are followed. Therefore, attaching an image to the chat does not help. author: egamma

title: Extension API: Control Tree Item Expansion During Drag Operations
body:
<!-- ⚠️⚠️ Do Not Delete This! feature_request_template ⚠️⚠️ -->
<!-- Please read our Rules of Conduct: https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/ -->
<!-- Please search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates. -->

<!-- Describe the feature you'd like. -->
# VS Code Feature Request: Control Tree Item Expansion During Drag Operations

## Problem

When implementing drag-and-drop in `TreeViews` with hierarchical structures (folders + leaf items), VS Code automatically expands any collapsible tree item when hovering over it during a drag operation. This behavior is hardcoded and cannot be controlled by extensions.

**Real-world scenario**: We're building a connection management tree with folders and connection items. Connections are expandable (they contain databases), but resolving the children requires establishing a real network connection.

During drag-and-drop operations, when a user moves the mouse pointer *over* a connection item to reach a target folder, VS Code forcibly expands the item. **This automatically triggers an unwanted connection attempt**, blocking the UI or causing side effects, even though the user simply wanted to pass over the item.

This behavior makes drag-and-drop functionality essentially unusable for our scenario, as we cannot allow accidental connections during simple UI interactions. It strictly limits the extensibility of `TreeView` for any extension that uses "virtual nodes" -- items that are expandable but aren't simple folders, or require expensive operations to resolve children.

The current behavior assumes that expandable items are **always** valid drop containers, but this isn't true for many hierarchical models where:
- Leaf items can have children for viewing purposes but shouldn't accept drops
- The distinction between "drop onto parent folder" vs "drop into subfolder" matters
- Visual feedback and user intent need to be distinguished

## Impact

This limitation forces us to **disable drag-and-drop entirely** in our scenario to prevent poor user experience and accidental operations.

## Proposed Solution

We need a way to control the hover-expansion behavior programmatically. This could be achieved by:

1.  **A property on `TreeItem`** (e.g., `expandOnDragOver: boolean`) to explicitly opt-out of auto-expansion for specific items.
2.  **A callback in `TreeDragAndDropController`** (e.g., `handleDragOver`) that allows the extension to determine if expansion is allowed for a given target.

Either approach would solve the issue by giving extensions control over when an item should be expanded during user interaction.

---

## Keywords

`TreeView` `TreeDragAndDropController` `drag-and-drop` `dnd` `expansion` `auto-expand` `hover` `handleDrop` `TreeItem` `collapsibleState` `folder` `hierarchy` `drop-target` `extension-api`

## References

We've gathered 19 votes for this feature and are resubmitting it for more visibility https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/286332

title: Autopilot (Preview) hangs waiting for terminal input after running tests
body: - Copilot Chat Extension Version: 0.39.2026030601 - VS Code Version: 1.111.0 insiders - OS Version: windows 11 - Feature (e.g. agent/edit/ask mode): Autopilot (Preview) - Selected model (e.g. GPT 4.1, Claude 3.7 Sonnet): GPT-5 mini **Steps to Reproduce** 1. Enable Autopilot (Preview) in VS Code with GitHub Copilot 2. Ask Copilot to implement tests for a given piece of code 3. Autopilot runs the test command in the integrated terminal 4. The terminal output pauses — Autopilot does not read or acknowledge the result 5. The agent remains in a waiting/blocked state indefinitely 6. Only after manually pressing Enter in the terminal does Autopilot resume **Expected Behavior** Autopilot should read the terminal output automatically after the test command completes, without requiring any manual user interaction. **Actual Behavior** Autopilot blocks on terminal input. The process is stuck in an "awaiting input" state and does not proceed until the user presses Enter manually. This issue defeats the purpose of autonomous agent execution. The terminal appears to be waiting for acknowledgment that Autopilot does not send. It may be related to how the agent reads stdout/stderr from processes that do not exit cleanly or that buffer output differently depending on the test runner used.

With Copilot Chat 0.38, we show a short list of recently used models in the language. If you frequently use more than the predefined number of models, this is adding additional clicks for selecting your preferred model. The current Manage Models functionality provides sufficient flexibility to accommodate this scenario.

title: Copilot Chat repeatedly fails to read terminal output, causing retries and excessive token usage
body:
<!-- ⚠️⚠️ Do Not Delete This! bug_report_template ⚠️⚠️ -->
<!-- Please read our Rules of Conduct: https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/ -->
<!-- 🕮 Read our guide about submitting issues: https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/wiki/Submitting-Bugs-and-Suggestions -->
<!-- 🔎 Search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates. -->
<!-- 🧪 Test using the latest Insiders build to see if your issue has already been fixed: https://code.visualstudio.com/insiders/ -->
<!-- 💡 Instead of creating your report here, use 'Report Issue' from the 'Help' menu in VS Code to pre-fill useful information. -->
<!-- 🔧 Launch with `code --disable-extensions` to check. -->
Does this issue occur when all extensions are disabled?: Yes/No

<!-- 🪓 If you answered No above, use 'Help: Start Extension Bisect' from Command Palette to try to identify the cause. -->
<!-- 📣 Issues caused by an extension need to be reported directly to the extension publisher. The 'Help > Report Issue' dialog can assist with this. -->
- VS Code Version: `Darwin arm64 25.2.0`
- OS Version: `1.110.0`

```
Version: 1.110.0
Commit: 0870c2a0c7c0564e7631bfed2675573a94ba4455
Date: 2026-03-04T01:20:28.863Z
Electron: 39.6.0
ElectronBuildId: 13330601
Chromium: 142.0.7444.265
Node.js: 22.22.0
V8: 14.2.231.22-electron.0
OS: Darwin arm64 25.2.0
```

## Description

When using **Copilot Chat in Chat mode**, Copilot sometimes fails to read the output from terminal commands. Even though the command executes successfully, Copilot behaves as if **no output was returned**, and repeatedly retries different approaches to fetch the same information.

This results in:

* multiple unnecessary retries
* wasted tokens
* slower debugging workflow

I noticed this happening when Copilot tries to inspect files using shell commands.

## Steps to Reproduce:

1. Open a project in VS Code.
2. Use **Copilot Chat (Chat mode)**.
3. Ask Copilot to inspect a file and search for specific strings.
4. Copilot executes terminal commands similar to:

```bash
wc -l "<project_root>/app/(services)/services/split/[token]/_components/shared-split-edit-client.tsx"

grep -n "isClosed\|is_closed\|export\|SharedSplitEdit" \
"<project_root>/app/(services)/services/split/[token]/_components/shared-split-edit-client.tsx"
```

or

```bash
cat "app/(services)/services/split/[token]/_components/shared-split-edit-client.tsx" \
| grep -n "isClosed\|is_closed\|export\|SharedSplitEdit" \
| head -30
```

Copilot may also attempt something like:

```bash
cd <project_root> && python3 -c "..."
```

## Actual Behavior

Copilot reports something like:

> "No output was produced by the command. The terminal seems to not be showing output."

However:

* the command **does produce output when run manually**
* Copilot then **keeps retrying different commands**
* each retry consumes **additional tokens**

## Example behavior:

1. Run grep
2. Says no output
3. Runs Python wrapper
4. Says no output
5. Tries another method

## Expected Behavior

Copilot Chat should:

* correctly read terminal command output
* avoid retrying when output is already available
* minimize unnecessary command retries

## Impact

This issue can cause:

* unnecessary token usage
* slower debugging workflows
* confusion when Copilot reports that commands produced no output even though they did

For workflows where Copilot inspects files through shell commands, this behavior happens frequently.

author: KarstenB

title: chat.tools.global.autoApprove: true causes ask_questions tool calls to be skipped in agent mode
body:
- Copilot Chat Extension Version: 0.37.9
- VS Code Version: 1.109.4
- OS Version: Windows_NT x64 10.0.2263
- Feature: agent mode
- Selected model: GPT-5.2 (also reproducible with other models)
- Logs: N/A

## Steps to Reproduce:

1. Add `"chat.tools.global.autoApprove": true` to your `settings.json`
2. Start a conversation with the agent mode.
3. Observe that when the agent attempts to call the `ask_questions` tool, the call is immediately **skipped** — the question is never presented to the user.
4. Set `"chat.tools.global.autoApprove": false` and repeat. The agent now correctly presents the questions to the user.

## Expected Behavior

The `chat.tools.global.autoApprove` setting should auto-approve non-interactive tool calls (file edits, terminal commands, etc.), but **interactive/user-facing tools like `ask_questions` should still be presented to the user**. Skipping a question tool defeats its purpose — there is no meaningful action to "approve," the tool's entire function is to collect user input.

## Actual Behavior

When `chat.tools.global.autoApprove` is set to `true`, the `ask_questions` tool call is auto-approved/skipped without ever showing the question to the user. The agent logs show "Skipped" for the question step and then proceeds as if the user provided no answer, breaking the intended agent workflow.

title: Explorer find widget: filter mode fails to match filenames containing uppercase letters
body:
VS Code Version: 1.109.5
OS: Windows 11 Enterprise 10.0.22631
Tested with --disable-extensions — same result

Steps to reproduce:
1. Open any local folder with a file like UserHelper.cs
2. Expand the folder tree so the file is visible
3. Press Ctrl+Alt+F to open the Explorer find widget
4. Type elper.cs→ filter shows the file ✅
5. Type helper.cs→ filter shows nothing ❌
6. Type Helper.cs → filter shows nothing ❌
7. Toggle to highlight mode → helper.cs correctly highlights the file ✅

Expected: Filter mode should match the same way highlight mode does
Actual: Filter mode fails when search term includes characters that correspond to uppercase letters in filenames
Settings: "workbench.list.defaultFindMode": "filter", "workbench.list.defaultFindMatchType": "fuzzy"

author: Maxim-Mazurok

title: Update chat titles when todos are reset
body:
The problem with the current session titles is they get outdated as the conversation evolves, as we only set it from the first message with very minimal information, which basically makes it not very useful.

Most tasks in the agent evolve over time, and the to-do list is might be the best proxy what the agent is working on. If we add a to-do list title to the tool call, we could proactively use this as the chat title as the conversation evolves and the to-dos get updated. Sometimes the to-dos even get thrown away with a new set of two tools, and the new title could really well describe the current task in way more detail than we would ever predict when the conversation starts.

cc @bhavyaus
author: digitarald

title: Groovy syntax highlighter doesn't handle dollar slashy strings
body:
Type: Bug When Groovy syntax highlighting is enabled on a Groovy file with [dollar slashy strings](https://groovy-lang.org/syntax.html#_dollar_slashy_string), the highlighter does not handle those properly. The highlighter does not treat the multi-line string as a string, and instead attempts to highlight all the text within as new lines of Groovy code. Depending on what is inside the string, this can mess up the rest of the syntax highlighting for the rest of the file following the string. A screenshot is attached. Here is a sample code block for reproduction of the issue: ```groovy class MyClass { def myMethod() { def myvar1 = "aaa" def myvar2 = $/ bbb def myvar3 = "ccc" ddd https://groovy-lang.org/syntax.html#_dollar_slashy_string 'eee /$ def myvar4 = "fff" } } ``` VS Code version: Code - Insiders 1.110.0-insider (f5927e727c69039d3037f018f1790ebbbee596bc, 2026-02-28T02:04:19.045Z) OS version: Linux x64 6.18.13-100.fc42.x86_64 Modes:

title: Deleted files
body:
Type: Bug I run a chat and let AI refactor something. Somehow some generated files are corrupted (with many spaces " " and some chaos code). It asked to delete old files and rewrite, I allowed. title: Copilot chat history lost
body:
After update code chats do not appear. 
- Version: 1.109.5
- Commit: 072586267e68ece9a47aa43f8c108e0dcbf44622
- Date: 2026-02-19T19:43:32.382Z
- Electron: 39.3.0
- ElectronBuildId: 13313481
- Chromium: 142.0.7444.265
- Node.js: 22.21.1
- V8: 14.2.231.22-electron.0
- OS: Linux x64 6.18.13-arch1-1
- Copilot Chat Extension Version: 0.37.9
- Logs:

# Copilot Chat incident report (ready to send)

## Summary

After a VS Code / GitHub Copilot Chat update, recent chat sessions disappeared from the Copilot Chat history panel.

Reloading window and restarting VS Code did not restore the missing recent session.

## Environment

- OS: Linux
- VS Code profile path: `~/.config/Code/User`
- Extension data root: `~/.config/Code/User/globalStorage/github.copilot-chat`
- Date observed: 2026-03-01

## What I observed

1. In `~/.config/Code/User/globalStorage/github.copilot-chat`, only cache/embedding/tool files are present.
2. `copilot.cli.oldGlobalSessions.json` exists but is only 36 bytes.
3. `~/.config/Code/User/globalStorage/emptyWindowChatSessions` contains only old sessions (2025-09 / 2025-10).
4. `chat.ChatSessionStore.index` in `~/.config/Code/User/globalStorage/state.vscdb` contains old entries only and does not include the session from today.
5. `state.vscdb.backup` contains the same session index (no newer missing session).

## Key evidence

- Existing files in extension storage include:
  - `commandEmbeddings.json`
  - `projectTemplateEmbeddings.json`
  - `settingEmbeddings.json`
  - `toolEmbeddingsCache.bin`
  - `copilot.cli.oldGlobalSessions.json` (36 bytes)

- Existing local chat JSON sessions are limited to these IDs:
  - `1e666982-e637-46c8-bd17-1ce7df7d5208` (old)
  - `12704a8d-95d0-45ba-b8e0-6e3a66d5aceb` (old)
  - `2d9ff8e5-1aea-4363-a75f-079cea711255` (empty)
  - `8e400abf-64ae-4870-af26-2c8d625c2278` (old)

- Current chat session index in DB:
  - Key: `chat.ChatSessionStore.index`
  - Contains only old entries; missing recent session from 2026-03-01.

## Expected behavior

Recent chats should remain visible and restorable in Copilot Chat after update/reload/restart.

## Actual behavior

Recent chat session is not visible in UI and is not present in local session index/storage.

## Reproduction steps

1. Use Copilot Chat normally and create/reply in a session.
2. Update VS Code and/or Copilot Chat extension.
3. Reload window and restart VS Code.
4. Open Copilot Chat history.
5. Observe missing recent session.

## Troubleshooting already attempted

- Reload Window
- Full VS Code restart
- Inspection of:
  - `~/.config/Code/User/globalStorage/github.copilot-chat`
  - `~/.config/Code/User/globalStorage/emptyWindowChatSessions`
  - `~/.config/Code/User/globalStorage/state.vscdb`
  - `~/.config/Code/User/globalStorage/state.vscdb.backup`

## Request

Please help determine whether this is a known migration/regression issue and whether hidden recovery tooling exists for missing local chat sessions from around 2026-03-01.
author: quetdn

title: If one copilot session is in plan mode and another is in agent mode (both are running), switching to the plan mode agent disables tools for the agent mode.

author: raghavdasila

title: Frequent KV cache invalidation and forced full prompt re-processing (github.copilot-chat, insider))
body:
<!-- ⚠️⚠️ Do Not Delete This! bug_report_template ⚠️⚠️ -->
<!-- Please read our Rules of Conduct: https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/ -->
<!-- 🕮 Read our guide about submitting issues: https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/wiki/Submitting-Bugs-and-Suggestions -->
<!-- 🔎 Search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates. -->
<!-- 🧪 Test using the latest Insiders build to see if your issue has already been fixed: https://code.visualstudio.com/insiders/ -->
<!-- 💡 Instead of creating your report here, use 'Report Issue' from the 'Help' menu in VS Code to pre-fill useful information. -->
<!-- 🔧 Launch with `code --disable-extensions` to check. -->

Does this issue occur when all extensions are disabled?: Yes

- VS Code Version: 1.110.0-insider (user setup)  


- Extension: github.copilot-chat  
- Extension Version: 0.38.2026022801  
- OS Version: Windows 11 x64 (10.0.26200)

---

## Description

When using GitHub Copilot Chat with a locally hosted `llama.cpp` server (llama-b8183-bin-win-cuda-13.1-x64, Qwen3-Coder-Next-GGUF), I am observing frequent KV cache invalidation and forced full prompt re-processing on the server side.

This happens especially during larger reading / review workflows (multi-step reasoning, MCP-style interactions, larger file context). It appears that the extension repeatedly resends large rolling context, which leads to server-side checkpoint invalidation.

Server logs frequently show:

- `forcing full prompt re-processing due to lack of cache data`
- Erasing all context checkpoints
- Full prompt re-evaluation (~40k–50k tokens)
- Significant performance degradation

This makes iterative review workflows noticeably slower.

---

## Server Setup

Running:

lama-b8183-bin-win-cuda-13.1-x64

Startup script:

```bat
@echo off
"D:\llama_ai\llama-b8183-bin-win-cuda-13.1-x64\llama-server.exe" ^
  -m "C:\Users\chris\AppData\Local\llama.cpp\unsloth_Qwen3-Coder-Next-GGUF_UD-Q6_K_XL_Qwen3-Coder-Next-UD-Q6_K_XL-00001-of-00003.gguf" ^
  --alias "Qwen3-Coder-Next-GGUF:UD-Q6_K_XL" ^
  --gpu-layers all ^
  -ot "\.(1[9-9]|[2-9][0-9]|[0-9][0-9][0-9])\.ffn_(gate|up|down)_exps.=CPU" ^
  --flash-attn on ^
  --cache-type-k q8_0 ^
  --cache-type-v q8_0 ^
  --cache-ram 28384 ^
  --cache-reuse 956 ^
  --ctx-size 81536 ^
  --batch-size 1024 ^
  --ubatch-size 512 ^
  --threads 8 ^
  --kv-offload ^
  --op-offload ^
  --fit off ^
  --parallel 1 ^
  --host 0.0.0.0 ^
  --port 11434 ^
  --seed 3407 ^
  --temp 1.0 ^
  --top-p 0.9 ^
  --min-p 0.01 ^
  --top-k 40 ^
  --jinja

pause
```

---

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Connect Copilot Chat to the local llama.cpp server (compatible openai)
2. Open a larger file or perform multi-step reasoning / review.
3. Let Copilot perform read → analyze → follow-up steps.
4. Observe repeated full prompt re-processing in server logs.

---

## Expected Behavior

- Incremental requests should reuse prior context efficiently.
- No repeated full re-evaluation of ~40k–50k tokens for small incremental steps.
- Stable server-side checkpoint reuse.

---

## Actual Behavior

- Frequent cache invalidation.
- All checkpoints erased.
- Full prompt re-processing.
- Large latency spikes during review workflows.

---

## Logs


The issue appears more related to conversation/session handling than to raw model performance (although I hear swa issues recently)




Example logs

```
slot update_slots: id  0 | task 3487 | prompt done, n_tokens = 50966, batch.n_tokens = 54
slot init_sampler: id  0 | task 3487 | init sampler, took 4.03 ms, tokens: text = 50966, total = 50966

prompt eval time =     599.17 ms /    54 tokens (   11.10 ms per token,    90.12 tokens per second)

Extensions disabled ## Description When using the VSCode Insiders integrated terminal with the Fish shell on Linux, commands that enter interactive mode (such as selecting options with arrow keys, or opening a TUI application) experience severe key handling issues. ## Steps to Reproduce 1. Open VSCode Insiders on Linux (tested on Bazzite 43/Kinoite) 2. Set the integrated terminal shell to Fish (`fish`) 3. Run any of these specific interactive commands: - `bun update -i` - `gh run workflow` (and related gh interactive commands) - `gh run watch` - Any interactive GitHub CLI (`gh`) command 4. Attempt to interact with the interactive interface using keys like: - Enter - Space - Ctrl+C - Ctrl+X - Arrow keys (sometimes work partially, but not reliably) - Any other meta or control keys ## Important Observation Commands like `htop` or `btop` (non-Node/Bun applications) work perfectly well with all key bindings functioning as expected. The issue appears to be specific to Node.js or Bun-based interactive applications. ## Expected Behavior Keys should function normally in interactive mode, allowing navigation, selection, and control of the interactive interface. ## Actual Behavior When in interactive mode: - Pressing Enter has no effect - Pressing Space does nothing - Control keys like Ctrl+C and Ctrl+X don't work - Arrow keys may work partially but are unreliable - The interactive interface becomes unresponsive to most key inputs ## Environment - VSCode Version: 1.110.0-insider - Commit: f5927e727c69039d3037f018f1790ebbbee596bc - Date: 2026-02-28T02:04:19.045Z - Electron: 39.6.0 - ElectronBuildId: 13330601 - Chromium: 142.0.7444.265 - Node.js: 22.22.0 - V8: 14.2.231.22-electron.0 - OS Version: Bazzite 43 (Kinoite, Linux x64 6.17.7-ba25.fc43.x86_64) - Shell: Fish 4.2.0 - Extensions Disabled: Yes (restarted with `--disable-extensions`) ## Fish Shell Configuration Details - **Version**: Fish 4.2.0 - **Custom Functions**: None (directory `~/.config/fish/functions/` is empty) - **Custom Completions**: None (directory `~/.config/fish/completions/` is empty) - **Key Bindings**: Using default `fish_default_key_bindings` - **Terminfo**: TERM variable not explicitly set in the current context - **Environment**: Running on Wayland (KDE Plasma 6) - **Default Configuration**: No significant customizations to Fish shell configuration ## Additional Notes - The issue does NOT occur in the stable version of VSCode (1.109.5) - The issue persists after restarting VSCode Insiders - The issue occurs even with all extensions disabled - The interactive commands work perfectly well in: - The system's native terminal (Konsole) - The VSCode Stable integrated terminal - The Fish shell running outside VSCode ## Impact If this issue lands in the next stable release of VSCode, users who use interactive commands with the Fish shell will be forced to either: 1. Downgrade to the previous stable version 2. Switch to a different shell (losing Fish-specific workflow benefits) 3. Use an external terminal application --- I have attached videos demonstrating the issue: https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/25497128-932b-449f-8967-4294f1afe7a6 https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/a66bfdfb-ba3c-4472-b38a-709e928b5936

title: Agent loses access to terminal/file editing when switch to Plan mode in different agent
body:
Type: Bug In Github Copilot, when switching from one (running) agent to another and entering Plan mode (in the new agent), the other (non-plan-mode) agents lose access to terminal and file editing capabilities. After the switch, terminal commands cannot be executed and files cannot be modified, even though these capabilities were previously available in the prior agent session before starting plan mode in the new agent. Maybe there is a permissions or global state issue between agents, specifically triggered by entering Plan mode in a different agent. I don't know if this occurs when I merely click the button to enter plan mode, or when I actually start plan mode with a different agent. But this can be problematic for people who are running multiple, simultaneous local agents using Github Copilot. Extension version: 0.37.9 VS Code version: Code 1.109.5 (Universal) (072586267e68ece9a47aa43f8c108e0dcbf44622, 2026-02-19T19:43:32.382Z) OS version: Darwin arm64 25.3.0 Modes:

title: Integrated terminal fails to start on Windows 10 1607 (14393) after update to 1.109.5: Cannot launch conpty
body:
Type: Bug ### VS Code version - Working version: **1.108.2** - Commit: `c9d77990917f3102ada88be140d28b038d1dd7c7` - Date: `2026-01-21T13:52:09.270Z` - Electron: `39.2.7` - Chromium: `142.0.7444.235` - Broken version: **1.109.5** - Commit: `072586267e68ece9a47aa43f8c108e0dcbf44622` - Date: `2026-02-19T19:43:32.382Z` - Electron: `39.3.0` - Chromium: `142.0.7444.265` ### OS Windows 10 x64, build `10.0.14393` (1607) ### Repro steps 1. Install/update to VS Code `1.109.5` (user setup) on Windows 10 14393. 2. Open VS Code. 3. Open integrated terminal (`Ctrl+``). ### Actual result Integrated terminal fails to start with: `Terminal process failed to launch: A native exception occurred during launch (Cannot launch conpty).` ### Expected result Integrated terminal should start normally. ### Regression? Yes. - `1.108.2` works on the same machine and same environment. - `1.109.5` fails consistently. - Downgrading from `1.109.5` to `1.108.2` immediately restores terminal startup. ### Additional notes - This appears to be a compatibility regression for older Windows 10 builds (`14393`). - I also saw related renderer log entries such as: - `UriError: Scheme contains illegal characters` - terminal startup failure notifications - Extensions are not required to reproduce (issue still occurs after update on this OS/build). title: Windows + Git Bash: Running 4 sub-agents in parallel causes exit code 130/256 (reproducible on GPT-5.2/5.3)
body:
Does this issue occur when all extensions are disabled?: No

- VS Code Version: 1.109.5 (Stable)
- OS Version: Windows 11 (build 26200.7840)

Steps to Reproduce:

1. Set default integrated terminal profile to Git Bash (Git for Windows 2.32.0).
2. Open Agent mode with GitHub Copilot Chat (0.37.9), select GPT-5.2 or GPT-5.3.
3. Start 4 sub-agents simultaneously (parallel execution).
4. Let each sub-agent run terminal commands (run in terminal) for a few minutes.
5. Observe intermittent terminal failures: exit code 130 appears first, then exit code 256 may also appear; sub-agent workflows become unstable or stall.
6. Repeat the same scenario with GPT-5 mini and compare behavior.

Expected:
- Terminal execution remains stable with 4 parallel sub-agents.
- No intermittent exit code 130/256 under normal operation.

Actual:
- On GPT-5.2 / GPT-5.3, exit code 130 occurs intermittently, followed by 256 in some runs.
- On GPT-5 mini, this issue is not reproduced in the same setup.

title: Tools selection is reset every time the agent changes
body:
Type: Bug When I use the Plan agent my copilot-instructions.md explicitly say the plan must be recorded in `docs\active-plan.md` however every time I select Plan mode the tools are reset to exclude editing files and I must reset them each time. I have even added instructions to cold stop planning if the tools are disabled and copilot ignores them and prattles on making plans I ultimately have to ask it to make again because it won't write them to the active-plan.md document. author: seank-com

title: Two subagents can't use ask_questions at the same time
body:
From https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/291691

- Run a query like `Run two subagents and ask both of them to use the askquestions tool to ask a test question. I'm just testing this functionality.`
- One question carousel appears
- Then it is suddenly replaced by the second one

The first ask question tool call got skipped, I only get to answer the second one. Subagents should be able to ask questions independently and all get their answers. The questions should be queued. 

Bonus points, annotate the question carousel with which subagent it's associated with.

title: Chat history not refreshing after creating new session - requires extension restart
body:
Type: Bug **VS Code Version:** [votre version de VS Code] **Copilot Chat Extension Version:** 0.37.8 **OS:** Windows **Description:** After starting a new chat session and having a conversation with Copilot, the new session doesn't appear in the chat history list. The only workaround is to disable the GitHub Copilot Chat extension, restart extensions, and re-enable AI features to refresh the session list. **Steps to Reproduce:** 1. Start a new chat session 2. Have a conversation with Copilot 3. Return to the chat sessions list 4. The latest session is not visible **Expected Behavior:** New sessions should appear automatically in the session list without requiring an extension restart. **Workaround:** Extensions → GitHub Copilot Chat → Disable → Restart Extensions → Enable AI features Extension version: 0.37.8 VS Code version: Code 1.109.5 (072586267e68ece9a47aa43f8c108e0dcbf44622, 2026-02-19T19:43:32.382Z) OS version: Windows_NT x64 10.0.26200 Modes:

title: Dont replicate tips across sessions
body: We should only show a tip in one place. If a user duplicates sessions we shouldnt show tips across all entry points
author: cwebster-99

title: Copilot changes in file look like everything is changed, when in fact (git changes) only a few changes were made.
body:
Type: Bug I have not found a way to reproduce the problem, it just happens now and again. If you 'panic' and undo copilot's changes in the file, you end up with an old version of that file (not how it previously was). Looks like the editor is showing diffs not from the latest version. title: Fix Overlap Between "Keep/Review" Button Group and "Open Chat Customization" Button in Editor

title: Add Arabic Language Support as Default Display Language
body:
<!-- ⚠️⚠️ Do Not Delete This! feature_request_template ⚠️⚠️ -->
<!-- Please read our Rules of Conduct: https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/ -->
<!-- Please search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates. -->

<!-- Describe the feature you'd like. -->
Thank you for maintaining this project.

This pull request proposes adding Arabic language support to Visual Studio Code’s default language list.

Changes Included

Add Arabic translations for VSCode UI strings

Enable Arabic to appear in the default language selection list

Improve accessibility for Arabic-speaking users

Purpose

This change helps Arabic-speaking developers use VSCode with a more natural and localized experience, improving usability and accessibility.

How to Test

Pull the latest changes from this branch

Build and run VSCode

Open Display Language Settings

Verify that Arabic appears in the available languages list

Switch to Arabic and verify UI translations

Repository:
https://github.com/almhajer/Arabic-for-visual-studio-code

Thank you for your time and review.

author: TMANNAM

title: Queued / steering messages can move while trying to interact with them
body:
Testing #297145

1. Start the agent on some long task
2. While it's working, queue some messages
3. Try to interact with the messages while the chat continues moving
4. Sometimes the elements will scroll away, making them difficult to interact with

IMO they should be fixed above the input box rather than in the scrolling view
author: kycutler

title: Agent chat doesn't return anything legible
body:
<!-- Please search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates -->
<!-- Please attach logs to help us diagnose your issue -->

- Copilot Chat Extension Version: 0.37.8
- VS Code Version: 1.109.5
- OS Version: Windows 11 Pro 23H2 22631.6345
- Feature (e.g. agent/edit/ask mode): Agent
- Selected model (e.g. GPT 4.1, Claude 3.7 Sonnet): Claude Sonnet 4.5 (or anyone, really)
- Logs:

Steps to Reproduce:

1. Install latest VSCode version
2. Install GitHub Copilot Chat Extension
3. Open side bar with chat
4. Write "you active?" (or anything, really) in the prompt and press enter.
5. Get "spawn UNKNOWN (at tsx element GNe > iBe > n > Wrt > eV)" as response.

title: Using /plan in background agent proposes wrong keyboard shortcut
body: Testing #297119 When you generate a plan in a background session, it assumes you're running in the terminal and proposes to use `Shift+Tab` to switch out of plan mode. This is only valid in the terminal, but not in VS Code chat.
author: ntrogh

title: [Windows] Terminal fails to launch with EBUSY error on versions after 1.104.3 (conpty pipe locked by Code.exe) - Regression
body:
Type: Bug ## Issue Description The integrated terminal fails to launch on VS Code versions **after 1.104.3** with the following error: > The terminal process failed to launch: A native exception occurred during launch (EBUSY: resource busy or locked, open '.\pipe\conpty-XXXXX.YYYYYYY-in') This is a **regression** - version 1.104.3 and earlier work correctly, but all subsequent versions exhibit this issue. ## Steps to Reproduce 1. Install VS Code version 1.104.3 or earlier → Terminal works ✅ 2. Update to any version after 1.104.3 → Terminal fails ❌ 3. Open any workspace 4. Try to open integrated terminal (`Ctrl+``) 5. Error occurs immediately ## Expected Behavior Terminal should launch successfully as it does in version 1.104.3 ## Actual Behavior Terminal fails with EBUSY error. Process Explorer shows multiple `Code.exe` processes holding the same conpty named pipe handle, preventing new terminal instances from opening. ## Evidence ### 1. Working State (v1.104.3) ✅ Multiple handles are correctly managed and released. ### 2. Broken State (v1.109.5) ❌ Multiple `Code.exe` processes hold the same pipe handle, causing `EBUSY`. VS Code version: Code 1.109.5 (072586267e68ece9a47aa43f8c108e0dcbf44622, 2026-02-19T19:43:32.382Z) OS version: Windows_NT x64 10.0.26200 Modes:

title: Enable runSubagent tool to accept local images as vision context
body: Right now, the runSubagent tool in VS Code Chat only takes plain text prompts - there’s no built-in hook for attaching a local image file as vision context. That means the orchestrator can’t really do a “two-agent flow”, where Agent A generates something like a screenshot and then hands it off to Agent B (Vision) to look at the pixels without using OCR tools. That kind of hand-off does work in GitHub Copilot CLI and the Codex VS Code extension. In VS Code however Chat reports that runSubagent only accepts text input, so you can’t supply an image along with the request. VS Code Chat: Codex: Github Copilot CLI:
