title: Getting Started with Python in VS Code: PSSecurityException
body:
<!-- ⚠️⚠️ Do Not Delete This! bug_report_template ⚠️⚠️ -->
<!-- Please read our Rules of Conduct: https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/ -->
<!-- 🕮 Read our guide about submitting issues: https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/wiki/Submitting-Bugs-and-Suggestions -->
<!-- 🔎 Search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates. -->
<!-- 🧪 Test using the latest Insiders build to see if your issue has already been fixed: https://code.visualstudio.com/insiders/ -->
<!-- 💡 Instead of creating your report here, use 'Report Issue' from the 'Help' menu in VS Code to pre-fill useful information. -->
<!-- 🔧 Launch with `code --disable-extensions` to check. -->
Does this issue occur when all extensions are disabled?: N/A

<!-- 🪓 If you answered No above, use 'Help: Start Extension Bisect' from Command Palette to try to identify the cause. -->
<!-- 📣 Issues caused by an extension need to be reported directly to the extension publisher. The 'Help > Report Issue' dialog can assist with this. -->
- VS Code Version: 1.109.5
- OS Version:  Edition	Windows 11 Home
Version	25H2
Installed on	‎18/‎10/‎2024
OS build	26200.7840
Experience	Windows Feature Experience Pack 1000.26100.291.0


This is a bug on the on-line supporting documentation, not the installed application

I'm working through the basic Visual Studio for Python tutorial. When I first attempt to run hello.py, I get a security exception PSSecurityException.

https://code.visualstudio.com/docs/python/python-tutorial#_run-python-code

I'm an experienced Python developer but assume I'm new to Microsoft. As such, I'm expecting an absolute beginners' tutorial to guide me through a set of problem-free steps which would work on a fresh installation. I'd also hope that the help text would gently introduce me to to best practice. 

Weirdly, without explanation, the code runs anyway and indeed runs the correct, newly installed Python variant I chose for my .venv. 

Something raising a security exception yet running anyway is a worry, so I'm reporting it. I'm assuming VS Code does something clever and sorts out the security exception on it's own: hopefully in a way which isn't creating a security vulnerability. So this may be mostly a question of aesthetics. However since this only happens, I think, the first time you run after a fresh install, and it appears to be new, I thought you might want to know about it!
author: mgrazebrook

title: Copilot Chat: Conversation history lost after VS Code update restart
body:
<!-- Please search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates -->
<!-- Please attach logs to help us diagnose your issue -->

- VS Code Version: 1.109.5 (Universal), after update
- OS Version: macOS
- Feature (e.g. agent/edit/ask mode): Agent mode

## Summary

After accepting an in-app VS Code update prompt while a GitHub Copilot Chat agent-mode conversation was active, significant parts of the chat history for that session was lost after VS Code restarted. This includes all prompts and responses from earlier in the same day, not just the in-progress exchange.

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Open a workspace in VS Code.
2. Start a GitHub Copilot Chat conversation in agent mode and exchange multiple messages.
3. While the chat agent is paused and waiting for user permission to execute a command, VS Code shows an update prompt.
4. Accept the update and allow VS Code to restart immediately.
5. Respond with `Yes` to `A chat request is in progress. Are you sure you want to close the window? The chat request will stop if you close the window`.
6. After VS Code restarts, open the Copilot Chat view.

## Expected Behavior

The full Copilot Chat conversation history (all prior prompts and responses, including those from earlier in the day) should be preserved and visible after the restart.

## Actual Behavior

- The session/conversation that was active before the update is completely gone.
- Earlier completed prompts from the same day are also missing, not just the in-progress exchange.

## Additional Context

- The chat was in a state where the agent had paused to request user permission to run a command when the VS Code update prompt appeared.
- The loss of chat history was not complete. Early parts of the chat thread (from many days back) was preserved.
- Chat history in other "Sessions" appears to be unaffected.
author: jjkavalam

author: jimmu76

title: When I undid stuff in a chat, it undid all the things that were done also from another chat, and this is terrible. Should never happen.
body:
Type: Bug To reproduce the error regarding file interference between two agent chat conversations, follow these steps: 1. **Start a New Chat**: Initiate a chat conversation with the agent and perform some actions or modifications that create a specific state or outputs (e.g., saving files, setting parameters). 2. **Create a Second Chat**: Open a new chat conversation and attempt to undo certain actions you made during the first chat (e.g., reverting settings, deleting messages, or removing saved files). 3. **Undo Actions**: In the second conversation, try reversing the actions you did in the first chat; for instance, if you saved files in the first chat, try deleting them or restoring them in the second chat. 4. **Observe Interference**: Check the file states in both chat conversations to see if there is unexpected interaction between them — such as files appearing or disappearing incorrectly, or settings changing unexpectedly. 5. **Restore Files**: Attempt to restore files in the second chat and notice if they are also mistakenly affected in the first chat. This interference should not occur and indicates a flaw in how the two sessions handle file states. This should help you illustrate and report the issue where the files in both chats interfere with each other. Extension version: 0.37.6 VS Code version: Code 1.109.4 (c3a26841a84f20dfe0850d0a5a9bd01da4f003ea, 2026-02-16T15:35:57.932Z) OS version: Windows_NT x64 10.0.26200 Modes:

title: Background agent caused explosion of worktrees
body:
<!-- Please search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates -->
<!-- Please attach logs to help us diagnose your issue -->

- Copilot Chat Extension Version:
- VS Code Version: 1.109.4
- OS Version:0.37.6
- Feature (e.g. agent/edit/ask mode):Agent mode, Background Agent
- Selected model (e.g. GPT 4.1, Claude 3.7 Sonnet):
- Logs:

Steps to Reproduce:
Not sure how to get a repro yet but trying to capture data off the machine. Had copilot CLI do this investigation and it brought in timestamps of when the worktrees were created. It matches up when I was trying out vscdoe background agent for the first time. 

I was getting toasts like this
![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/f98437b1-8410-4962-9e8e-930e3abbfb25)

# Bug Report: VS Code Copilot Background Agent — Runaway Worktree Creation

## Summary

The VS Code Copilot background agent created **1,526 git worktrees** in a single day (2026-02-18), spawning a new worktree approximately every **6-7 seconds** during active bursts. This consumed an estimated **~800 GB of disk space** and created **1,693 orphaned branches**, effectively degrading system performance.

## Environment

- **OS:** Windows
- **Repository:** `agentic.es` (main repo ~1.7 GB)
- **Worktree directory:** `C:\Development\agentic.es.worktrees\`
- **Date of incident:** 2026-02-18

## Evidence

### Scale of the problem

| Metric | Value |
|--------|-------|
| Total worktree directories created | 1,526 |
| Total git branches created | 1,693 |
| Remote branches pushed | 0 |
| Estimated disk usage | ~800 GB (1,526 × ~537 MB working copy each) |
| Main repo size | 1.7 GB |
| Duration of runaway behavior | ~16.3 hours |

### Creation rate analysis

- **Average interval:** 38.4 seconds (skewed by gaps between bursts)
- **Minimum interval:** 5 seconds
- **1,160 of 1,525 intervals were under 10 seconds** (76% of creations)
- **570 intervals were exactly 6-7 seconds** — suggesting a tight retry/respawn loop

### Burst pattern by hour (UTC)

```
2026-02-18T02  —  92 worktrees (started ~02:49)
2026-02-18T03  — 153 worktrees
2026-02-18T04  — 311 worktrees  ← peak burst
2026-02-18T05  — 291 worktrees
2026-02-18T06  — 116 worktrees
              (gap — likely machine reboot/sleep)
2026-02-18T09  —  22 worktrees
              (gap)
2026-02-18T16  —  16 worktrees
2026-02-18T17  — 233 worktrees  ← resumed burst
2026-02-18T18  — 286 worktrees
2026-02-18T19  —   6 worktrees  (stopped ~19:05)
```

The burst pattern shows the agent was creating worktrees in sustained rapid-fire loops, pausing only during apparent machine sleep/reboot, then resuming immediately.

### Worktree structure

Each worktree is a **proper git worktree** (`.git` is a file pointing to main repo's `.git/worktrees/`), so git objects are shared. However, each worktree contains a **full working copy** of the repo (~537 MB), meaning the disk impact is real and enormous.

All worktrees follow the naming pattern: `copilot-worktree-YYYY-MM-DDTHH-MM-SS`

### No cleanup occurred

- All 1,526 directories still exist on disk
- All 1,693 branches remain as local branches
- 0 branches were pushed to remote
- No worktrees were removed or pruned by the agent

## Triggering request

A single background agent request triggered the entire incident. 

The VS Code UI confirmed the agent created its first isolated worktree at **`copilot-worktree-2026-02-18T02-49-49`**, which exactly matches the start of the burst pattern in the hourly data (`2026-02-18T02 — 92 worktrees (started ~02:49)`).

This was a **read-only audit task** — the user explicitly said "just create the plan do not make changes" — meaning it should have required exactly **one** worktree. Instead, the agent entered a creation loop producing 1,526 worktrees over the next ~16 hours.

## Likely root cause

The background agent appears to have entered a loop where it:
1. Creates a new worktree + branch
2. Fails or times out on the task (possibly due to resource exhaustion)
3. Does not clean up the failed worktree
4. Immediately retries by creating another new worktree
5. Repeat every ~6-7 seconds indefinitely

There is no rate limiting, circuit breaker, or cap on the number of concurrent/total worktrees.

## Impact

- **~800 GB of disk consumed** by working copies
- **Machine performance severely degraded** ("blew up my machine")
- **1,693 orphaned branches** polluting the local git state
- **Git operations slowed** due to massive worktree/branch list

## Suggested fixes

1. **Cap maximum worktrees** — enforce a hard limit (e.g., 10-20 concurrent worktrees)
2. **Rate limit creation** — prevent creating more than 1 worktree per minute
3. **Circuit breaker** — if N consecutive worktree tasks fail, stop creating new ones
4. **Cleanup on failure** — remove worktree + branch when a background task fails
5. **Cleanup on startup** — prune stale worktrees from previous sessions

author: bamurtaugh

title: Feature Request: Allow dynamic model selection when spawning subagents via `runSubagent`
body:
## Feature Request

### Problem

The current model selection for subagents is static, requiring each agent to be pre-configured with a fixed `model:` in its `.agent.md` frontmatter. This limitation prevents dynamic specification of the model a subagent should use at invocation time from a user prompt. For instance, if a user wants to say:

> "Check your work with 5.3-pro Codex"

The main agent lacks the ability to spawn a subagent with a different model than itself or what's hardcoded in the agent definition. This necessitates creating separate `.agent.md` files for each model, which is inefficient and counterproductive.

### Use Case

In agentic workflows, it's common to use a primary model (e.g., Claude Opus 4.5) for implementation and delegate verification to a different model (e.g., 5.3-Codex) for a "second opinion." For example:

```
User (talking to Opus): Implement the feature described in issue #42. Then check your work with 5.3-Codex and Gemini 3 pro.
```

The agent should be able to:
1. Implement the feature using Opus.
2. Spawn a subagent via `runSubagent` that uses GPT-5.3-Codex for review.
3. Review with Gemini-3-Pro.
4. Incorporate feedback from the review.

This functionality is already supported in the Copilot CLI, where agents can be configured with different models, and the orchestrating agent can reference them by name, even inferring the correct model from partial or misspelled names (e.g., 5.3 Kodex vs GPT-5.3-Codex).

title: Chat sessions not persisting after VS Code restart and rename not updating in Sessions panel
body:
Type: Bug **Description:** I'm experiencing two related issues with chat session persistence and naming in VS Code with GitHub Copilot. ## Issue 1: Chat Sessions Not Persisting After VS Code Restart **Symptoms:** - When I close VS Code, my chat sessions do not persist upon reopening - During shutdown, I briefly see the chat session in the Sessions sidebar panel with a blue dot next to it - After reopening VS Code, the session is no longer visible in the Sessions panel - The chat appears to exist momentarily during shutdown, even though it wasn't visible in the Sessions pane before closing - My project location has not changed between sessions - I believe I have the "persist chats" setting enabled **Reproducibility:** This happens every time I restart VS Code. ## Issue 2: Chat Rename Not Updating Sessions Panel **Symptoms:** - When I rename a chat session, the title only updates in the upper left corner of the opened chat window - The original title remains unchanged in the Sessions sidebar panel - The two locations show different names for the same chat ## Expected Behavior 1. Chat sessions should persist across VS Code restarts 2. Renamed chats should update consistently in both the chat header and the Sessions panel ## Environment - **VS Code Version:** 1.109.4 - **GitHub Copilot Version:** 0.37.6 - **OS:** Windows 11 - **Sessions UI Location:** Sidebar view/panel ## Additional Context Based on research, this appears related to known session management issues introduced around v1.108, potentially involving corrupted state.vscdb files or session index synchronization problems. ## Steps I Haven't Tried Yet - Checking Developer Tools console for errors during restart/rename - Verifying workspace trust settings - Manually accessing "Agent Sessions" control - Checking for session files in workspace storage folders --- ** questions:** 1. Should I check Developer Tools first and add console errors? 2. Which GitHub repo should this go to? 3. Would screenshots/recording be helpful? title: one vscode copilot agent in plan mode causes all other sessions to lose access to tools
body:
<!-- Please search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates -->
<!-- Please attach logs to help us diagnose your issue -->

- Copilot Chat Extension Version: 0.37.6
- VS Code Version: 1.109.3
- OS Version: WSL2 Ubuntu 20,04 Windows 11
- Feature (e.g. agent/edit/ask mode): Plan/Sessions
- Selected model (e.g. GPT 4.1, Claude 3.7 Sonnet): Claude Sonnet 4.5
- Logs:

Steps to Reproduce:

1. Start a new Plan session (Session A), get the plan and "Begin Implementation"
2. While the first session (Session A) is executing, start a new Plan session (Session B) and begin planning
3. Session A will attempt to begin file edits while Session B is still planning (before clicking "Begin Implementation")

You will notice that Session A no longer has access to tools and will report it does not have access to tools.
author: Theaxiom

title: Copilot Plan mode loses work and context when using “View plan” and Background Task handoff
body:
Title
Copilot Plan mode loses work and context when using “View plan” and Background Task handoff

Product/Feature
Microsoft Copilot in VS Code — Plan mode / Background Task

Description
I have lost over 12 hours of work due to Plan mode losing context and plan details. In one case, clicking “View plan” caused Copilot to dump or relocate the plan in a stripped-down form, and all the context I had been building disappeared. In a second case, after reviewing the plan in chat and then sending it to a Background Task, the context disappeared again and no background work executed. A file the plan was supposed to create (progress.md) was not created, indicating the plan and execution were lost.

Impact

~12+ hours of work lost across two incidents
Plan context and details irrecoverably lost
Background Task did not run and produced no outputs
Steps to Reproduce Just tried to used those features one time. Nothing special

Use Copilot Plan mode and build up detailed context and plan steps in chat.
Click “View plan.”
Observe that the plan appears without most of the previously created context; the context is lost.
On a later attempt, avoid “View plan,” ask the chat to show plan details, then send the plan to a Background Task.
The context disappears again and the Background Task never runs (e.g., no progress.md created).
Expected Behavior

Plan details and context should be preserved when using “View plan.”
Sending a plan to a Background Task should retain full context and execute as described.
Actual Behavior

Plan context is lost after “View plan.”
Plan context is lost during Background Task handoff; background task never runs; expected artifacts are not created.
Environment

se review Copilot’s internal logs/telemetry for the affected sessions. I do not have local logs beyond whatever Copilot captures.

Workarounds Tried
Avoided “View plan,” attempted to keep the plan in chat. Context still disappeared when handing off to Background Task.

If you want, I can tighten the repro steps, add environment details, or tailor this for a different reporting channel.

title: Steering is a bad default when the chat is paused on a command approval request.
body:
<!-- Please search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates -->
<!-- Please attach logs to help us diagnose your issue -->

- Copilot Chat Extension Version: 0.37.6
- VS Code Version: 1.109.4
- OS Version: macOS 15.6.1
- Feature (e.g. agent/edit/ask mode): Agent
- Selected model (e.g. GPT 4.1, Claude 3.7 Sonnet): Clause Opus 4.5
- Logs:

Steps to Reproduce:

Since updating to VS Code 1.109:

1. Enter a prompt that will try to execute an action that needs approval
2. Instead of approving the action, enter new text and hit enter
3. Nothing happens
4. Notice that the new prompt got queued up as "Steering", but the agent isn't doing anything, so the chat just sits there...
5. Click the up arrow that's above your message to send it immediately.

This _used_ to send the new prompt immediately to the agent, which would start processing the new prompt instead of waiting for the command. Now you have to "stop" a chat that's already stopped. It's a friction that wastes time if you didn't notice that your new prompt isn't being processed. It's also pretty non-obvious: the only thing that happens in this situation is that your message bubble is slightly darker than usual. Other than that you might be looking at the same screen you were in 1.108.

Suggestion: If the chat is already stopped, as in when it's waiting for a command to be approved, and you type a message and hit enter, then send the message immediately to the agent. Requiring "Stop and send" both doesn't make sense, since the chat is currently stopped, and is really annoying because there's no keyboard shortcut for it.

title: Copilot agent edits: Keep/Undo buttons persist after reload and lack session attribution
body:
<!-- ⚠️⚠️ Do Not Delete This! feature_request_template ⚠️⚠️ -->
<!-- Please read our Rules of Conduct: https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/ -->
<!-- Please search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates. -->

<!-- Describe the feature you'd like. -->

## Feature Request

### Problem

When Copilot agent session generate edits, and if these edits aren't accepted, `Keep` or `Undo` button doesn't disappear on the restart or reloading of window.

This is confusing especially if I used different Copilot agent session or external tool like Copilot CLI, Codex to generate edits, the leftover `Keep` or `Undo` button from previous session doesn't disappear which is confusing and I don't know which session generated which edits.

### Requested Behavior

Improve attribution of agent edits:
- label edits by agent/session
- group changes by session
- provide a per-agent review view

### Expected Outcome

I can clearly identify which agent session generated each edit.

title: Urgent: GitHub Copilot "Stop" button no longer works.
body:
1. Ask the LLM to do something.
2. While the LLM is mucking around with files, try to stop the LLM by clicking the "Stop" icon (the circle containing the square).

**Expected:** The LLM stops.

**Actual:** The LLM continues mucking with files, oblivious that you are trying to stop it.

**Workaround:**

1. Type "Stop!" in the prompt and submit it.
2. A message "STEERING" appears.
3. Find the context menu by the grayed-out "Stop!" you tried to submit, and select "Send immediately."
4. Pray that the LLM obeys you and stops.

Even when the LLM responds and says it stops, the LLM seems to continue working, and later VS Code alerts you that the prompt has succeeded (similar to #293006).

**This is an urgent security issue.** Using AI should not involve praying. When I hit the "Stop" button, the AI must immediately stop!

This relates to both #293006 and #284678. It is worrying that GitHub copilot is accumulating conditions in which the AI cannot be stopped, and the GitHub team does not recognize the urgency.

title: Expose registerDynamicAuthenticationProvider API for use in MCP provider extensions
body:
<!-- ⚠️⚠️ Do Not Delete This! feature_request_template ⚠️⚠️ -->
<!-- Please read our Rules of Conduct: https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/ -->
<!-- Please search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates. -->

<!-- Describe the feature you'd like. -->

VS Code currently exposes a method registerAuthenticationProvider in their authentication extension API (https://code.visualstudio.com/api/references/vscode-api#authentication) for use in shared session management across user-installed extensions. These authentication providers, however, exist outside of the more bespoke MCP authentication flow in the VS Code core libraries, which instead use the concept of "dynamic" authentication providers which may contain OAuth client credentials.

In this FR, **we are proposing the introduction of a new method vscode.authentication.registerDynamicAuthenticationProvider**, which would have the following interface:

```
registerDynamicAuthenticationProvider(id: string, label: string, clientId: string, clientSecret?: string, authorizationServer: UriComponents, resourceServer: UriComponents): Disposable
```

It would function similarly to the existing internal method at https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/blob/53cdd0920049f955c94afaf414b82ab67bef52f5/src/vs/workbench/api/browser/mainThreadAuthentication.ts#L289 by registering a regular authentication provider and updating the dynamic authentication provider storage.

This unlocks the use case in which an extension may install not only the MCP server itself, but also a set of default OAuth credentials which may be used with it. The ability to optionally provide default OAuth credentials mimics the "extension" or "connector" publishing process for various other LLM-based frameworks, such as Claude, ChatGPT and Gemini CLI. This would drastically improve out-of-the-box usability for extensions where applicable.

This feature request is on behalf of the effort within Google to create and publish remote MCP servers across various critical services within its API ecosystem (ref: https://cloud.google.com/blog/products/ai-machine-learning/announcing-official-mcp-support-for-google-services).

Does this issue occur when all extensions are disabled?: N/A



<!-- 🪓 If you answered No above, use 'Help: Start Extension Bisect' from Command Palette to try to identify the cause. -->
<!-- 📣 Issues caused by an extension need to be reported directly to the extension publisher. The 'Help > Report Issue' dialog can assist with this. -->

I am having the same issue described in
- https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/224788
- https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/230054

There is no explanation in those for why they were closed. At the very least, if this is not supported, then the option to do it should be removed so users aren't left confused.


```
Version: 1.109.2 (user setup)
Commit: 591199df409fbf59b4b52d5ad4ee0470152a9b31
Date: 2026-02-10T20:18:23.520Z
Electron: 39.3.0
ElectronBuildId: 13168319
Chromium: 142.0.7444.265
Node.js: 22.21.1
V8: 14.2.231.22-electron.0
OS: Windows_NT x64 10.0.26200
```
(connected to WSL2)

Steps to Reproduce:

1. `Profiles: New Profile...` 
2. Copy from Default profile with Source for Extensions set to "Default (Copy)"
3. Create

This doesn't do anything. The profile is not created.

Edit: Selecting "None" instead of "Default (Copy)" for Extensions does allow the profile to be created.

title: Copilot chat causes Editor to lag. Simply opening the list of changed files leads to serious performance issues
body:
<!-- ⚠️⚠️ Do Not Delete This! bug_report_template ⚠️⚠️ -->
<!-- Please read our Rules of Conduct: https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/ -->
<!-- 🕮 Read our guide about submitting issues: https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/wiki/Submitting-Bugs-and-Suggestions -->
<!-- 🔎 Search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates. -->
<!-- 🧪 Test using the latest Insiders build to see if your issue has already been fixed: https://code.visualstudio.com/insiders/ -->
<!-- 💡 Instead of creating your report here, use 'Report Issue' from the 'Help' menu in VS Code to pre-fill useful information. -->
<!-- 🔧 Launch with `code --disable-extensions` to check. -->
Does this issue occur when all extensions are disabled?: Yes

<!-- 🪓 If you answered No above, use 'Help: Start Extension Bisect' from Command Palette to try to identify the cause. -->
<!-- 📣 Issues caused by an extension need to be reported directly to the extension publisher. The 'Help > Report Issue' dialog can assist with this. -->
- VS Code Version: 1.109.2
- OS Version: MacOS Tahoe 26.2

Steps to Reproduce:

1. Ask Copilot Chat to make any changes to the project code.
2. When the changes are made, click on the Changed Files list, after expanding the list the Editor starts to lag in any files, even those that have not been changed. For example, scrolling starts to work at 10-20 FPS. If you hide sidebar with explorer, scrolling starts to work at 20-30 FPS.
3. The only thing that helps is restarting VSCode (or reload window).

title: Stop with the incorrect suggestions that cover my code!

author: Dragon1308

title: File deletions don't show up in "changed files"
body:
<!-- ⚠️⚠️ Do Not Delete This! feature_request_template ⚠️⚠️ -->
<!-- Please read our Rules of Conduct: https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/ -->
<!-- Please search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates. -->

<!-- Describe the feature you'd like. -->

When the agent creates or edits files within the project they show up in the "changed files" panel of the chat.

But if it deletes a file - that doesn't show up as a change, and presumably there is no way to 'Keep' or 'Undo' it.

The agent has tools for create and edit (though it will often bypass them - I had to add strongly worded instructions to stop it from trying to cat+heredoc in terminal to create files, often getting stuck in unterminated heredoc)

I don't think it has a tool for deleting files though? (It told me it doesn't...)  So it always uses `rm` in terminal

Maybe that is why they don't show up as changes?

I am referring here to correct deletions, properly performed as part of refactoring task.

mostly using Claude Opus 4.5

title: run_in_terminal tool truncates large input streams to integrated terminal
body:
Type: Bug This happens with all shell (zsh, bash, fish, etc.). This bug might relate to #283065 1. Open vscode (insiders) 2. Open new chat, repeat the prompt below twice (This only occurs from the second time onwards a long snippet is "flooded" into the same terminal) 3. Ask `paste this into the terminal:`
snippet to paste ```md import sys import os import json import random import time import math import collections from datetime import datetime print('=== Extended Python Environment Check ===') print('Python version:', sys.version) print('Platform:', sys.platform) print('Current directory:', os.getcwd()) print('Process ID:', os.getpid()) print() # Check more imports modules_to_check = ['numpy', 'pandas', 'matplotlib', 'requests', 'flask', 'django', 'scipy', 'sklearn', 'tensorflow', 'torch'] print('Module availability check:') for mod in modules_to_check: try: __import__(mod) print(f' {mod}: ✓ available') except ImportError: print(f' {mod}: ✗ not available') print() # Define some functions def fibonacci(n): if n <= 1: return n return fibonacci(n-1) + fibonacci(n-2) def is_prime(num): if num < 2: return False for i in range(2, int(math.sqrt(num)) + 1): if num % i == 0: return False return True def generate_primes(limit): return [num for num in range(2, limit) if is_prime(num)] print('=== Computations ===') print('Fibonacci sequence (first 10):') for i in range(10): print(f' F({i}) = {fibonacci(i)}') print() print('Prime numbers up to 50:') primes = generate_primes(50) print(' ' + ' '.join(map(str, primes))) print(f' Total primes: {len(primes)}') print() # Random data generation and analysis print('=== Data Analysis Simulation ===') random.seed(42) # For reproducibility data = [random.gauss(50, 15) for _ in range(100)] print(f'Generated {len(data)} random data points (normal distribution)') print(f'Mean: {sum(data)/len(data):.2f}') print(f'Min: {min(data):.2f}, Max: {max(data):.2f}') # Frequency analysis rounded_data = [round(x) for x in data] freq = collections.Counter(rounded_data) print('Top 5 most frequent values:') for val, count in freq.most_common(5): print(f' {val}: {count} times') print() # File operations print('=== File System Operations ===') try: with open('README.md', 'r') as f: content = f.read() print('README.md content length:', len(content), 'characters') print('First 200 characters:') print(' \\\"' + content[:200].replace('\ ', ' ') + '...' + '\\\"') except FileNotFoundError: print('README.md not found') except Exception as e: print(f'Error reading README.md: {e}') print() print('Directory listing:') try: files = os.listdir('.') print(f' Found {len(files)} items:') for f in sorted(files): print(f' {f}') except Exception as e: print(f'Error listing directory: {e}') print() # title: Copilot Chat - Embedded terminal output view flashes during command execution
body:
Type: Bug Description: When a command is running in the embedded terminal output view (shown in the Copilot Chat panel), the view flashes/flickers continuously every time the output updates. Steps to Reproduce: 1. Open Copilot Chat in VS Code 2. Ask Copilot to run a command that produces incremental output (e.g., dotnet build) 3. Observe the embedded terminal output view while the command runs Expected Behavior: The terminal output should update smoothly without visual flashing. Actual Behavior: The embedded terminal output view flashes/flickers repeatedly and quickly. It appears to flicker when the output is updated. Affected Commands: dotnet build (confirmed) Possibly other commands with streaming output Extension version: 0.37.1 VS Code version: Code 1.109.0 (bdd88df003631aaa0bcbe057cb0a940b80a476fa, 2026-02-04T02:01:38.288Z) OS version: Windows_NT x64 10.0.26200 Modes:
System Info |Item|Value| |---|---| |CPUs|AMD EPYC 7763 64-Core Processor (16 x 2445)| |GPU Status|2d_canvas: unavailable_software

author: amunger

title: Feature Request: Profile association by directory subtree (parent folder → all descendants)
body:
<!-- ⚠️⚠️ Do Not Delete This! feature_request_template ⚠️⚠️ -->
<!-- Please read our Rules of Conduct: https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/ -->
<!-- Please search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates. -->

<!-- Describe the feature you'd like. -->

**Related issue:** #231974 (closed as not planned)

I’m requesting that this feature be reconsidered and reopened.

Currently, VS Code profiles can be associated only with **exact folders or workspaces**, which requires manual configuration for every individual project. This becomes cumbersome for users who intentionally organize projects under semantic parent directories (e.g. `~/Development`, `~/Research`, `~/ClientX`, etc.).

---

### Proposed behavior

Allow profiles to be associated with a **parent directory**, applying automatically to all descendant folders unless explicitly overridden.

Example:
- Associate profile `MySpecialProfile` with `~/MySpecialProjects/`
- Any folder opened under `~/MySpecialProjects/**` automatically uses `MySpecialProfile`
- Folders outside that subtree continue using the default profile
- Explicit folder/workspace associations take precedence over inherited ones

---

### Security & compliance motivation

This is not only a productivity issue but also a **security and compliance concern**.

Some security-conscious clients (including government and defense organizations) explicitly require:
- No AI-assisted tooling enabled
- No telemetry or external service interaction
- Strict separation between “restricted” and “general” development environments

Today, even if a user intends to use a locked-down profile with AI features disabled, VS Code will still open a project **initially under the default profile** until the user manually switches profiles. That initial open may already activate extensions, background services, or AI-related features.

For regulated or classified projects, this is:
- Potentially a **security policy violation**
- Potentially **illegal**, depending on the classification and contractual requirements
- A blocker to adopting profiles as a reliable safety mechanism

Being able to associate a “restricted / no-AI” profile with a parent directory (e.g. `~/ClassifiedProjects/**`) ensures that **the correct security posture is applied before the project is opened**, not after.

---

### Why this matters

- Eliminates repetitive manual setup for users with many repositories
- Enables filesystem-based security boundaries
- Aligns with established hierarchical scoping patterns (e.g. `.editorconfig`, `.gitignore`, environment inheritance)
- Makes profiles viable for regulated, enterprise, and government use cases
- Reduces the risk of accidental policy violations

---

I left a comment on #231974 a few weeks ago noting continued demand for this capability. Given the increased prominence of profiles and AI features in VS Code, this limitation is more impactful now than when the issue was originally closed.

At minimum, clarification on why subtree-based association is infeasible would be helpful; ideally, this feature should be reconsidered with security and compliance use cases in mind.

title: Submenus in top menu bar fail to render correctly in 2026 experimental themes (Dark/Light)
body:
Type: Bug ## Description:​ In both "VS Code Dark (Experimental)" and "VS Code Light (Experimental)" themes, the secondary dropdown menus (submenus) from the top menu bar (e.g., under File, Edit, View) are not displaying properly. The submenus either do not appear at all or render in incorrect positions (misaligned or displaced) . ## Steps to Reproduce:​ Switch the active color theme to either Dark Modern​ or Light Modern​ (via File > Preferences > Settings> "Color Theme") . Click on any top-level menu item (e.g., File, Edit, View). Hover over or click on an item that has a submenu (e.g., File> Preferences> Settings, or View> Appearance). Observe that the secondary submenu either fails to appear or renders misaligned (e.g., far from the parent menu item). ## Expected Behavior:​ Submenus should render clearly and be positioned directly adjacent to their parent menu item, consistent with the behavior in standard VS Code themes. ## Actual Behavior:​ Submenus are either completely invisible or appear misaligned (e.g., with significant offset), making navigation impossible . ## Screenshots: Submenus are invisible Submenus are misaligned Comparison (Dark Modern theme) ## Bug localization: This bug is probably relevant with VS Code, not extensions This bug exists in both Light & Dark themes, and exists in Insider version. ## Additional Context:​ Relevant issue-collection: #291108 Theme Version:​ Please confirm the exact theme names are "Dark Modern" and "Light Modern" (Experimental) . title: Terminal process failed to launch: "A native exception occurred during launch (Cannot launch conpty)" after upgrading to v1.109.0 (Appear again)
body:
Type: Bug (appear again)

Fresh Install of VSCode
Launch VSCODe
Terminal | New Terminal
Follwing error is raised.

After updating VS Code to version 1.109.0, 
The terminal process failed to launch: A native exception occurred during launch (Cannot launch conpty).

- VS Code Version: 
- 1.109.0
- OS Version: 
- Win10LTSB-2016, 10.0.14393-Build 14393

Steps to Reproduce:

1. Update VS Code to version 1.109.0 or Fresh Install of VSCode-1.109.0 (system-install for vscode)
2. Try to open the integrated terminal
3. Error appears

Additional Information:
This error only occurs in version 1.109.0 
When I downgrade to version 1.108.2 or earlier, the error does not occur  (system-install for vscode)
This appears to be a regression introduced in version 1.109.0
A similar issue was reported previously: #210884

author: simahao

title: Manage Trusted Extensions Settings in VSCode Profiles
body:
<!-- ⚠️⚠️ Do Not Delete This! feature_request_template ⚠️⚠️ -->
<!-- Please read our Rules of Conduct: https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/ -->
<!-- Please search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates. -->

Simply put: "Manage Trusted Extensions" is a real pain when using multiple GitHub accounts, as you need to [manually select the account to use once per extension](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/266693), and this account selection is apparently not profile-specific. If I launch multiple VSCode instances and switch accounts in one of them, it appears to affect other VSCode instances (at least for GitHub Copilot). This prevents me from working simultaneously with my personal GitHub Copilot subscription on personal projects, and using my work GitHub Copilot account on work-related stuff. I have to keep switching, and it's a pain, especially since I took the time to create two separate VSCode profiles.

I have written a blog with all the tips and tricks for using two separate GitHub accounts, from git ssh configuration to VSCode extensions: [Configuring SSH for Personal and Work GitHub Accounts](https://awakecoding.com/posts/configuring-ssh-for-personal-and-work-github-accounts/). The part which is most annoying is the inability to use VSCode profiles to control trusted extensions settings. What I'm asking for is a way to simply change my trusted settings based on my current VSCode profile. This way, I'd just have to switch VSCode profiles, and rarely have to change my trusted extension settings. Since VSCode remembers profiles based on the workspace path, it would also remember which account to use when reopening the same workspace. It's the most elegant solution to the problem.
author: mamoreau-devolutions

title: Question prompts collapse the session window and becomes unrecoverable
body: - Copilot Chat Extension Version: - VS Code Version: 1.109.3 - OS Version: Windows 11 - Feature (e.g. agent/edit/ask mode): Plan mode - Selected model (e.g. GPT 4.1, Claude 3.7 Sonnet): Claude Opus 4.5 Steps to Reproduce: 1. Enter a prompt that requires the planning agent to ask a clarifying question with multi-select options. 2. Enter a custom answer text and hit a control character like DEL. NOTE: I could only reproduce this sometimes hitting the DEL key but I'm convinced that it's happened with other keys as well. This causes the session to "collapse" in a weird way where the session is "open" but the heading is now at the bottom near the chat box. Clicking on the collapsed heading causes the quick pick session selector drop down to pop up. Nothing seems to bring it back into an interactive state.

title: Old windows10 style context menu action got removed
body: Does this issue occur when all extensions are disabled?: Yes Version: 1.109.0 (system setup) Commit: bdd88df003631aaa0bcbe057cb0a940b80a476fa Date: 2026-02-04T02:01:38.288Z Electron: 39.3.0 ElectronBuildId: 13168319 Chromium: 142.0.7444.265 Node.js: 22.21.1 V8: 14.2.231.22-electron.0 OS: Windows_NT x64 10.0.26200 Steps to Reproduce: 1. Run the new installer with `add to context menu` checked 2. The old context menu action "Open with code" are removed. The new version introduced the win11 style context menu action, but for users that are using old context menu, it removes the old entries in registry. https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/pull/291574 The same applies when using `show more options` on win11 context menu. As in most cases, actions presented in upper context menu are included in the `more options` menu. I believe there is the need to keep and add back the `open with code` action.
author: FYWinds

title: GitHub Copilot Chat incorrectly converts text patterns to `_vscodecontentref_` links
body:
**Description:** (LLM generated based upon the discussion)

VS Code's Copilot Chat is converting certain text patterns into clickable links pointing to `http://_vscodecontentref_/N`, even when the text is inside inline code backticks or code blocks.

**Triggers identified:**
- Recursive glob patterns (double-star followed by slash, then a filename)
- Filenames beginning with a dot (e.g., gitignore config file)
- Certain command-line patterns (e.g., `sed` with its in-place flag)

**Steps to reproduce:**
1. Open Copilot Chat
2. Ask a question that causes the assistant to output any of the above patterns
3. Observe that these are rendered as clickable links to `_vscodecontentref_` URLs instead of literal text

**Expected behavior:**
Text inside inline code backticks or code blocks should render literally, not be transformed into links.

**Actual behavior:**
The patterns are converted to non-functional `_vscodecontentref_` links, breaking documentation and technical instructions.

Type: Bug **Description** After using inline suggestions, pressing Escape to dismiss them prevents inline suggestions from triggering again in the same session. Attempting to manually re-trigger inline suggestions via Shift + Command + P does not work. Inline suggestions only start working again after restarting VS Code. **Steps to Reproduce:** 1. body:
Environment:

VS Code Version: 1.108.2 (user setup)

Commit: c9d77990917f3102ada88be140d28b038d1dd7c7

Date: 2026-01-21

OS: Windows_NT x64 6.0.6000

Python: 3.14.2 (C:\Users\hadik\AppData\Local\Programs\Python\Python314\python.exe)

Steps to Reproduce:

Create a simple Python file hello.py:

python
print("hellow")
Press Ctrl+Shift+P → Run Task → Run current Python file (3.14).

Terminal opens but:

No output is shown.

Task never ends.

Terminal is frozen (cannot type).

Pressing F5 also fails unless using external terminal.

Expected Behavior:

Output (hellow) should appear in the terminal.

Task should finish cleanly.

Terminal should remain interactive.

Actual Behavior:

Terminal opens but hangs.

No output is displayed.

Cannot type in terminal.

Even after reinstalling VS Code, issue persists.

Additional Notes:

Python runs fine outside VS Code (Command Prompt).

PyCharm works correctly with the same interpreter.

Tried resetting settings, reinstalling VS Code, using external terminal — only external terminal works.

%ComSpec% points to C:\Windows\System32\cmd.exe.

author: joaomoreno

title: User-initiated "Interject" to provide context mid-request without ending the agent turn
body:
### Problem

When using Agent mode, the "Ask Questions" feature allows the *agent* to pause and request input from the user mid-request. However, there's no equivalent way for the *user* to proactively provide additional context or steering to the agent without clicking "Stop" and ending the current request.

This is problematic because:

2. **Unnecessary friction**: Having to click Stop, wait for the agent to fully stop, then type a new message is clunky when you just want to inject a quick piece of information mid-flow.

3. **Asymmetry**: The agent can pause to ask me questions, but I can't pause to give it unsolicited information. The steering should work both ways.

### Proposed Solution

Add an **"Interject"** button to the agent chat UI that is visible while the agent is running. This would enable **user-initiated in-request steering** - essentially the inverse of the Ask Questions flow.

**Two interaction modes would be valuable:**

1. **Pause & Interject** (primary flow):
   - User clicks "Interject"
   - Agent pauses execution
   - User types additional context/corrections
   - User submits
   - Agent continues the *same request* with the new information

2. **Fire & Forget** (secondary flow):
   - For quick, small interjections that aren't time-sensitive
   - User provides input without pausing the agent
   - Agent incorporates the information when relevant

The "Pause & Interject" flow should be the default because it prevents the agent from getting further away from the target state while the user composes their input.

### Use Cases

- You see the agent about to use the wrong framework/library and want to redirect it
- You realize you forgot to mention an important constraint or requirement
- You want to provide clarification based on what the agent is currently doing
- You notice the agent misunderstood something and want to course-correct early

### Related Issues

- #283529 (closed as duplicate) - Similar request for mid-execution adjustments, though this issue specifically focuses on the user-initiated steering aspect and proposes concrete UX (Interject button with pause/fire-and-forget modes)

### NOTE

I created this issue yesterday as https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/292175 but that seems to have been automatically closed. Not sure why. I presume it's because it mentioned the words "Gi***B" and "Bi****g"? ffiw it was suggest that I create this issue by other VSCode contributors, see https://x.com/OrenMe/status/2017459944967037351?s=20

author: jrieken

title: Copilot Chat sessions become empty after renaming .code-workspace (missing chatSessions JSON in new workspaceStorage)
body:
## Bug: Copilot Chat sessions listed but empty after renaming / “Save Workspace As…” (.code-workspace)

### Summary
When a VS Code workspace file (`*.code-workspace`) is renamed (either via **File → Save Workspace As…** or via file manager rename), Copilot Chat conversation history remains visible (titles/sessions are listed), but opening any of those conversations shows an empty chat. DevTools logs show the corresponding `chatSessions/<uuid>.json` files are missing in the new workspaceStorage directory.

This looks like a migration/association bug between:
- workspace identity (`workspaceStorage/<hash>`)
- Copilot Chat session references/index
- the actual session files stored in `workspaceStorage/<hash>/chatSessions/*.json`

### Environment
- OS: Linux x64 5.15.0-164-generic
- VS Code: 1.108.2 (commit c9d77990917f3102ada88be140d28b038d1dd7c7, 2026-01-21)
  - Electron: 39.2.7 (BuildId 12953945)
  - Chromium: 142.0.7444.235
  - Node.js: 22.21.1
  - V8: 14.2.231.21-electron.0
- Extension: GitHub Copilot Chat (`github.copilot-chat`) 0.36.2
- File manager: Nemo

### Steps to Reproduce
1. Open VS Code by double-clicking a workspace file, e.g. `plannings.code-workspace`.
2. Ensure Copilot Chat has existing conversations (at least 2) and they open correctly.
3. Rename the workspace:
   - either **File → Save Workspace As…** and save as `plannings-core.code-workspace`
   - or rename `plannings.code-workspace` to `plannings-core.code-workspace` in the file manager (Nemo).
4. Quit VS Code.
5. Open `plannings-core.code-workspace`.

### Expected Behavior
Copilot Chat conversations should open normally (existing sessions preserved) after renaming / saving the workspace under a new name.

### Actual Behavior
Conversations are still listed in Copilot Chat history, but opening them shows empty content. DevTools logs show missing session files under the new workspaceStorage id.

Example error (DevTools Console):
```
ERR ChatSessionStore: Error reading chat session file 3b809804-b970-49b2-a3f8-561d7855a868
Impossible de lire le fichier
'/home/nurbo/.config/Code/User/workspaceStorage/5d846d1ab2974f338736a98a018aad76/chatSessions/3b809804-b970-49b2-a3f8-561d7855a868.json'
(Error: file does not exist)
```

### Additional Observations / Evidence
After the rename, VS Code uses a new workspaceStorage folder:
- New: `~/.config/Code/User/workspaceStorage/5d846d1ab2974f338736a98a018aad76/chatSessions/`
  - contains only recent small session files
  - does NOT contain old referenced sessions (UUID JSON files)

Old workspaceStorage folders still contain the full session JSONs, e.g.:
- `~/.config/Code/User/workspaceStorage/fffc67e8d2d7ead456061f36bbb54f0a/chatSessions/3b809804-b970-49b2-a3f8-561d7855a868.json`

Manual workaround:
- copy missing `*.json` from an older `workspaceStorage/<oldid>/chatSessions/` to the new one:
  `.../workspaceStorage/<newid>/chatSessions/`
This immediately restores the chat contents.

### Suspected Root Cause
Renaming/saving a workspace under a new name causes a new `workspaceStorage/<hash>` id to be generated, but Copilot Chat session references (sessionId list/index) are still present while the actual `chatSessions/*.json` are not migrated/copied into the new workspaceStorage folder.

### Impact
High: Users can appear to “lose” Copilot Chat history for a workspace after a simple rename / save-as operation, even though data still exists on disk in another workspaceStorage directory.

### Suggested Fix
When workspace identity changes due to rename/save-as:
- migrate/copy `chatSessions/*.json` along with any stored session references/index, or
- maintain a stable mapping so existing sessions continue to resolve correctly, or
- gracefully fall back to searching previous workspaceStorage locations if a referenced `sessionId` JSON is missing.

### Workarounds
- Manually copy missing `chatSessions/<uuid>.json` files from old workspaceStorage id to new one.
- Keep regular backups / export important conversations.

title: Popups like color theme selector or "go to file" are not resizeable
body:
Does this issue occur when all extensions are disabled?: Yes

- VS Code Version:  1.108.2
- OS Version: Manjaro Linux

Steps to Reproduce:

1. Open the color theme selector (Ctrl+K, Ctrl+T) or the "Go to file..." popup (Ctrl+p) or any other thing that opens up in a popup that contains a scrollable list that is not bounded in size

Now I want to resize the popup to see more elements in the list at once (and potentially expand it horizontally too in case there are any truncated or wrapped lines), taking advantage of my big screen (not that it's especially huge or anything) so that I don't have to scroll up and down.

Expected behavior: I should be able to do that. The popup should be resizable. EVERYTHING in the UI should be resizable, isn't that obvious?

Observed behavior: the popup cannot be resized.

PS - I love the update on how ask_question now renders inside chat. It is amazing QOL change from the previous method of using the top panel

1. Create a skill that uses ask_question tool to get clarification (e.g which file to use)

3. Tell chat to run skill as subagent
4. Subagent panel gets stuck in in busy "Asking a question") - doesn't render question in subagent panel inside chat

title: Nested gitignore incorrectly hides untracked file
body:
Testing #235478

Test case 5 failed `5. Nested .gitignore with case differences`
- Root `.gitignore` contents: `*.tmp`
- `SRC/.gitignore` contents: `!important.TMP`
- `SRC/important.tmp` is properly seen as untracked (it is not ignored)
- Setting `explorer:excludeGitIgnore: true` hides `SRC/important.tmp`

title: Worktree trust prompts appear while working

title: Session archiving doesn't persist from new welcome
body: Ref https://github.com/microsoft/vscode-internalbacklog/issues/6527 - Open new welcome page from empty workspace - Right-click on `Add a glossary` session and archive it - ✅ Session disappears from list in welcome, as I'd expect - Send a message from the welcome to a specific local workspace, and now that workspace opens (so I'm no longer in an empty VS Code workspace) - I close the newly opened workspace - ❓ Previously archived `Add a glossary` session reappears I'm able to repro this consistently.

title: Feature Request: Granular file exclusion settings for Explorer, Search, and Quick Open
body:
Add more granular control over file visibility across different VS Code features. Currently, files.exclude hides files from both the Explorer AND Quick Open (Ctrl+P), which is often not the desired behavior.

Many developers want to declutter the Explorer view without losing the ability to quickly access those files via Quick Open or Search. 

Common use cases include:
- Hiding build artifacts (.js, .map files) in the Explorer while still being able to jump to them quickly
- Hiding configuration files (.git, .vscode) from Explorer but keeping them searchable
- Reducing visual noise in large projects while maintaining full accessibility to all files
- Different workflows require different visibility levels for the same files

Currently, files.exclude is too aggressive - it hides files from:
✅ Explorer (desired)
❌ Quick Open / Go to File (NOT desired)

title: Allow selecting a GitHub account per VS Code profile / per `--user-data-dir` so Copilot authentication is not shared
body:
<!-- ⚠️⚠️ Do Not Delete This! feature_request_template ⚠️⚠️ -->
<!-- Please read our Rules of Conduct: https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/ -->
<!-- Please search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates. -->

<!-- Describe the feature you'd like. -->

### Summary
I use multiple VS Code profiles and `--user-data-dir` instances to separate personal and work projects. GitHub Copilot (and the GitHub authentication flow) currently uses tokens stored in the system keyring, which are effectively shared across profiles/instances. This causes Copilot to use **the same GitHub account for all profiles**, forcing me to log out and log in every time I switch contexts or to run separate VS Code installations. I request an option to bind a specific GitHub account to a VS Code profile or --user-data-dir instance so authentication and Copilot usage remain isolated.

### Why this matters
I have two GitHub accounts (work and personal), both with Copilot Pro.
When working on personal projects I must avoid consuming the company account’s Copilot usage.
Current behavior forces manual logout/login, use of multiple VS Code installs, or manual keyring edits — all inconvenient and error-prone.

### Expected behavior
Provide a setting in the GitHub/Copilot extension or the GitHub authentication flow to associate a GitHub account with a specific VS Code profile or `--user-data-dir`, so I can create two different menu items and start the right one as needed.
Alternatively, allow the extension to use a profile-local secret store (or opt out of reading the system keyring) when `--user-data-dir` is used, so tokens are isolated per profile.

### Environment
OS: Linux Mint 22.4 (GNOME Keyring / libsecret)
VS Code: Stable
Extension: GitHub Copilot (and Copilot Chat)

title: VS Code Copilot Chat does not create a checkpoint on the first message
body: Type: Bug When using VS Code Copilot Chat, a checkpoint (snapshot of the files state) is not created for the first chat message related to code. The checkpoint is only created starting from subsequent chat interactions. As a result, it is not possible to revert or compare the code state prior to the first Copilot response, which is inconsistent with the behavior observed for later messages. author: BNL-CODER

title: feat(mcp): Implement MCPB bundle package installation support
body:
## Summary

The `RegistryType.MCPB` is defined in the codebase for MCP bundle packages, but the installation flow is not implemented. MCPB packages are ZIP archives containing pre-built MCP server executables with a `manifest.json` file that describes how to run the server.

## Problem

When a user attempts to install an MCP server from the gallery that uses the MCPB package type:

1. The `getMcpServerConfigurationFromManifest()` switch statement in `mcpManagementService.ts` has no case for `RegistryType.MCPB`
2. The `getCommandName()` method returns `"mcpb"` as a string literal (not an executable) for MCPB packages
3. No bundle download, SHA256 verification, extraction, or manifest parsing logic exists

This results in MCPB packages failing to install properly.

## Proposed Solution

Implement full MCPB bundle support:

1. **Add `IMcpBundleManifest` interface** - Define the structure of `manifest.json` inside MCPB bundles:
   - `command`: Executable path relative to bundle root
   - `args`: Optional command arguments
   - `env`: Optional environment variables
   - `cwd`: Optional working directory relative to bundle root

2. **Create `McpBundleService`** - New service for MCPB operations:
   - Download bundle from URL (with retry logic)
   - Verify SHA256 checksum using `fileSha256` from the package definition
   - Extract ZIP archive using existing `src/vs/base/node/zip.ts:extract()`
   - Read and validate `manifest.json`
   - Clean up temporary files

3. **Update installation flow** - Handle MCPB packages in the Node-specific management service:
   - Detect MCPB package type during installation
   - Download and extract bundle to user's MCP location
   - Build server configuration from bundle manifest
   - Process environment variables and arguments with input variable support

## Additional Context

MCPB packages exist in the MCP Gallery registry (e.g., Postman MCP Server has MCPB packages available). The implementation should:

- Support input variables for environment variables and arguments that require user input
- Display clear error messages for checksum mismatches, corrupt archives, and missing manifests
- Clean up partial extractions on failure

## Affected Files

- `src/vs/platform/mcp/common/mcpManagement.ts` - Add `IMcpBundleManifest` interface
- `src/vs/platform/mcp/common/mcpManagementService.ts` - Add MCPB case in switch statement
- `src/vs/platform/mcp/node/mcpBundleService.ts` - New service for bundle operations
- `src/vs/platform/mcp/node/mcpManagementService.ts` - MCPB installation handling
author: terry-nederveld

title: Inline chat diffs show +0-0 for .R files despite changes being applied
body:
Type: Bug One-line Summary: Inline Chat file decorations display incorrectly as +0 -0 for .R files despite successful WorkspaceEdit application, leading to empty diff previews. Technical Description: I am experiencing a UI consistency issue in the GitHub Copilot Chat extension when editing R language files (.r extension). When the specific Chat agent applies a WorkspaceEdit or TextEdit to an open .r file, the changes are successfully reflected in the underlying TextDocument and the bottom Editor summary (visualized correctly in the diff editor as +13 -4). However, the Chat Response UI decoration—specifically the status pill identifying the file modification—incorrectly renders the diff metrics as +0 -0, and the file link leads to an empty diff preview. -> That is, clicking the file link in "1 File changed (Keep/Undo)" bottom section opens the diff view, which correctly shows the insertions and deletions, but clicking the file link in the chat "Edited app.R +0 -0" leads to an empty diff preview. Steps to Reproduce: Open an .R file in an active workspace. Ask Copilot (Panel) to modify code (e.g., "Add a new Shiny reactive function"). Observe that the chat response confirms the edit, and the actual file content updates. Bug: The file link in the chat response UI displays the green/red diff counter as +0 -0, and leads to an empty diff preview. title: If two sessions are launched simultaneously — one in planning mode and the other in agent mode — occasionally, in certain cases, the agent mode repeatedly asserts that it has no access to editing tools.
body:
Type: Bug Hello, I've noticed a problem of the following nature: If two sessions are launched simultaneously — one in planning mode and the other in agent mode — occasionally, in certain cases, the agent mode repeatedly asserts that it has no access to editing tools. In other words, it seems as if the planning mode rule is being applied to it, even though in agent mode I clearly see the agent present. Even if I forcibly switch it back and forth between planning and agent modes and ask it to redo its work, it continues to assert that it lacks access to document editing tools. The editing tools are disabled for it. title: The auto-detected Language Mode is often wrong
body:
Type: Bug VSCode seems to detect the language mode of pasted content based on the overall content rather than its format? If so, that clearly has issues. For example, after I create a new document and paste an HTML snippet that contains a large amount of CSS, even if the beginning of the code already has ``, VSCode still identifies it as a CSS file. Another case is that it often identifies Markdown content as INI. title: User-triggered context compaction (#remember)
body:
## Problem
In long conversations, Copilot eventually loses track of context or constraints established early in the session. Currently, the user has no control over what the agent chooses to retain or discard as the context window fills up.

## Proposed Solution
Allow the user to manually invoke compaction and summarization on specific information they want the agent to retain for the duration of the chat.

By using a command like `#remember <text>`, the user can force the agent to compact that specific text and "inject" it into its persistent memory for the rest of the session, preventing it from being lost during standard context cleanup.

### Example:
**User:** (After researching repository architecture) `#remember The 'DataParser' class is the entry point for all incoming streams.`

**Effect:** The agent compacts this finding and anchors its focus on the DataParser class.

**User:** (Continues researching other files...)

**User:** `#remember We found that 'ValidationLogic.ts' filters out the corrupted packets.`

**Effect:** The agent adds this new finding to the persistent memory. It now navigates the rest of the conversation holding both the "entry point" and the "filter logic" as the primary sources of truth, ignoring the noise from the intermediate research steps.

## Benefits
* **User-Driven Context Anchoring:** Long-form sessions often suffer from "context rot" or noise as the conversation window fills. This feature allows the user to manually anchor the agent to critical findings, ensuring these "source of truth" pillars remain in the active context while irrelevant intermediate research is pruned or compressed.

* **Dynamic Steering of the Reasoning Path:** Instead of the agent deciding what to remember, the user provides surgical control over the model's focus. This acts as a manual "governance layer," effectively defining the precise "path of thinking" the agent must follow, which improves multi-step task execution and reduces the need for constant re-intervention.

author: manasikasaju

title: Implement "Live Steering" and Mid-Run Feedback for Agent Mode
body: Type: Feature Request Currently, when using **GitHub Copilot Agent Mode** for complex, multi-step tasks, the interaction model is largely linear (Prompt -> Execute -> Review). If the agent begins to deviate from the intended architecture or uses an incorrect library mid-task, the user is often forced to **Stop** the execution, revert changes, and restart the session with a refined prompt. This is less efficient than "Manager-style" AI platforms (like Google Antigravity) which allow for asynchronous course correction without killing the process. ## Proposed Feature: "Live Steering" I am requesting the ability to provide real-time, non-interruptive feedback to the agent while it is actively running. ### Key Capabilities: 1. **Asynchronous Steering Notes:** A dedicated "Steering" input where the user can drop notes like *"Wait, use the v2 API for this file"* or *"Don't forget to export the types."* The agent should check this buffer between tool calls without needing to be stopped and restarted. 2. **Context Injection:** The ability to drag and drop a reference file or documentation link into an active session to influence the *remaining* steps of the current task. 3. **Mid-Run "Redlining":** The ability to highlight a piece of code the agent just generated and provide a correction (e.g., "Change this to a functional component") while the agent continues working on other parts of the plan. ## Expected Impact * **Reduced Token Waste:** Users won't have to restart long-running tasks from scratch due to minor early-stage misunderstandings. * **True Pair Programming:** The workflow shifts from "Batch Processing" to a "Collaborative Partnership," where the human can nudge the agent back on track in real-time. * **Higher Success Rates:** Complex refactors will require fewer total iterations if the human can provide "sanity checks" mid-flight. ## Comparison This would bring Copilot's UX in line with the "Live Feedback" loops seen in other agentic workflows, moving away from the "Stop and Fix" paradigm. Extension version: 0.36.1 VS Code version: Code 1.108.1 (Universal) (585eba7c0c34fd6b30faac7c62a42050bfbc0086, 2026-01-14T14:55:44.241Z) OS version: Darwin arm64 25.2.0 Modes:

title: New background agent editor sends me to another session

author: windowsair

title: Gemini models crash in Copilot Chat when MCP tool includes boolean parameters (related to vscode#288236)
body:
<!-- Please search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates -->
<!-- Please attach logs to help us diagnose your issue -->

- Copilot Chat Extension Version: 0.36.1
- VS Code Version: 1.108.1
- OS Version: Windows_NT x64 10.0.26100
- Feature (e.g. agent/edit/ask mode): agent
- Selected model (e.g. GPT 4.1, Claude 3.7 Sonnet): Gemini 3 Pro Perview
- Logs:

Description:

I am encountering a reproducible failure in VS Code Copilot Chat when using any Gemini model together with an MCP tool that defines boolean parameters.

This issue appears to be closely related to (or possibly the same root cause as) the following existing issue, and I would like to report a concrete reproduction case and request a re-review in this context: #288236

Problem Summary:

* When any Gemini model is selected in Copilot Chat
* And any MCP tool with at least one boolean-typed parameter is attached
* Asking any question (even unrelated to the tool)
    → Copilot Chat fails with an internal error.

title: Display context window consumption and file inclusion
body: Type: Feature Request ## Feature request: Add a lightweight “Context Usage” indicator for Copilot Chat in VS Code (MVP) ### Summary Please add a **small, non-intrusive Context Usage indicator** to GitHub Copilot Chat in VS Code that helps users understand: 1) **How close the current chat/session is to the context limit**, and 2) **Whether attached files were fully included vs. reduced vs. excluded**. This is intentionally scoped as an MVP: **a meter + a small details popover** (metadata-only), not a full token analytics suite. --- ### Problem statement When Copilot responses are low quality or appear to ignore key information, users currently have no clear way to answer: - Did the assistant include the file(s) I expected? - Were those files included fully, summarized/outlined, or excluded due to size? - Is the conversation near the context limit (i.e., likely truncating earlier turns or clipping attachments)? Many other LLM-integrated developer tools provide at least a basic “context fullness / usage” signal, which helps users self-correct quickly. VS Code’s Copilot experience would benefit from the same baseline visibility. --- ### Proposed MVP UX #### A) Context meter in Copilot Chat header (or input area) - Display: `Context: ~X% used` (or “Low / Medium / High” if exact percentages aren’t available across models) - Subtle warning state when nearing the limit (e.g., >80% / >90%) #### B) Click-to-expand details popover (metadata-only) When clicking the meter, show a compact list of what was included for the current request and/or current session: - Conversation history included (yes/no + approximate size) - Active file / selection (yes/no) - Attached files (per file): - `Included fully` / `Included as outline` / `Excluded (too large)` / `Partially clipped` - Optional: workspace-derived context (e.g., symbols/references) as a single line item > Note: This can be *metadata only* (file names + inclusion status + size), with no requirement to show the raw text that was sent. #### C) One-click action(s) (optional but high value) - `Start new chat` (reset context) - Optional: `Summarize/compact history` (if/when the product already has an internal mechanism to reduce history) --- ### Acceptance criteria - Users can always see a **context usage signal** for the current Copilot Chat session. - Users can click it to see **which context items were included**, and for attachments whether they were **full / outline / excluded / clipped**. - No requirement for token-perfect accounting in v1; approximate tiers or percentages are fine. - Enterprise-friendly: the details view can be **metadata-only** and subject to admin policy controls. --- ### Business value - **Fewer wasted iterations / higher productivity**: Users immediately know whether Copilot “saw” the right material (and whether it was clipped), reducing prompt retries and manual troubleshooting. - **Higher trust and adoption (especially in enterprise)**: Transparency about what context is being used reduces uncertainty and helps teams adopt the tool with more confidence. - **Better performance and cost control**: Context size strongly influences latency and compute usage. A visible “fullness” signal nudges users toward leaner, more targeted context, improving responsiveness at scale. - **Improved supportability**: When users report “Copilot ignored my file,” a context indicator makes it straightforward to verify whether attachments were excluded or truncated. --- ### Scope note (to keep this shippable) This request is **not** asking for a full “token ledger” or forensic transcript of everything sent to the model. The MVP can be: - a simple meter + basic inclusion status, - with approximate sizing, - and metadata-only details. That baseline visibility would already materially improve user experience, trust, and efficiency. Extension version: 0.35.0 VS Code version: Code 1.107.0 (618725e67565b290ba4da6fe2d29f8fa1d4e3622, 2025-12-10T07:43:47.883Z) OS version: Darwin arm64 25.2.0 Modes:

title: MCP: Allow users to select remote vs local installation when both are present in the gallery
body:
<!-- ⚠️⚠️ Do Not Delete This! feature_request_template ⚠️⚠️ -->
<!-- Please read our Rules of Conduct: https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/ -->
<!-- Please search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates. -->

<!-- Describe the feature you'd like. -->

**Issue**: When installing an MCP server from the MCP Gallery, if there are both type 'package' and 'remote' it will hit package first, [code](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/blob/1.108.0/src/vs/workbench/services/mcp/common/mcpWorkbenchManagementService.ts#L456-L486). 

**Feature Request**: Allow the user to select local or remote at install time. I think there are valid reasons for any of the default behavior options, so surfacing the choice to the user would be appropriate. 

Related [issue](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/279314) and [comment](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/255220#issuecomment-3716666565)

author: koyacch

title: New settings are not included on extension upgrade
body:
The issue is that if you publish a new version of your extension with new settings, those will not be available when the extension is upgraded. You'll need your users to fully restart the window for the settings to be considered.

This causes issues for extensions that rely on boolean settings defaulting to true.

<!-- ⚠️⚠️ Do Not Delete This! bug_report_template ⚠️⚠️ -->
<!-- Please read our Rules of Conduct: https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/ -->
<!-- 🕮 Read our guide about submitting issues: https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/wiki/Submitting-Bugs-and-Suggestions -->
<!-- 🔎 Search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates. -->
<!-- 🧪 Test using the latest Insiders build to see if your issue has already been fixed: https://code.visualstudio.com/insiders/ -->
<!-- 💡 Instead of creating your report here, use 'Report Issue' from the 'Help' menu in VS Code to pre-fill useful information. -->
<!-- 🔧 Launch with `code --disable-extensions` to check. -->
Does this issue occur when all extensions are disabled?: Yes/No

<!-- 🪓 If you answered No above, use 'Help: Start Extension Bisect' from Command Palette to try to identify the cause. -->
<!-- 📣 Issues caused by an extension need to be reported directly to the extension publisher. The 'Help > Report Issue' dialog can assist with this. -->
- VS Code Version: Version: 1.109.0-insider (Universal)
Commit: 17523c000eef5c2197a369b1dda37574b9c63217
Date: 2026-01-14T10:29:51.420Z
Electron: 39.2.7
ElectronBuildId: 12953945
Chromium: 142.0.7444.235
Node.js: 22.21.1
V8: 14.2.231.21-electron.0
OS: Darwin arm64 25.2.0

But it also happens in Cursor and Cursor nightly. 

Steps to Reproduce:

1. Install a version of the extension that doesn't have a particular setting.
2. Upgrade to a new version of the extension which has a new setting.
3. Restart extensions (don't reload the window)
4. The new setting is not available, causing code relying on its existence to fail, specially if the setting is a boolean which defaults to true.
5. Restart the VS Code instance, the setting will appear.

I posted a video reproducing the issue:

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/856b9e72-127e-4f3a-a8de-5f1f390d3dea

As you can see, the observability settings are only shown when the instance is reloaded. 

In case you wonder, the settings are not read when the extension tries to access those. So we're getting false instead of true.

title: editor.accessibilitySupport "no" resets when it detects a screen reader
body:
<!-- ⚠️⚠️ Do Not Delete This! bug_report_template ⚠️⚠️ -->
<!-- Please read our Rules of Conduct: https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/ -->
<!-- 🕮 Read our guide about submitting issues: https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/wiki/Submitting-Bugs-and-Suggestions -->

<!-- 🔧 Launch with `code --disable-extensions` to check. -->
Does this issue occur when all extensions are disabled?: Yes

<!-- 🪓 If you answered No above, use 'Help: Start Extension Bisect' from Command Palette to try to identify the cause. -->
<!-- 📣 Issues caused by an extension need to be reported directly to the extension publisher. The 'Help > Report Issue' dialog can assist with this. -->
- VS Code Version: 1.108.0
- OS Version: MacOS 26.2

Steps to Reproduce:

1. Open VS Code
2. Check that `[editor.accessibilitySupport](vscode://settings/editor.accessibilitySupport)` is set to "off"
3. Use Wispr or MacOS Dictation to create content into a document
4. Notice that Screen Reader Optimized is now "on"

This is a small but really annoying bug as I use Wispr Flow a lot and it constantly turns it on and I have not found a way yet of forcing it to remain off.

author: iwangbowen

title: Add Table API Support to VS Code Panel for Displaying Multi-Column Data
body: Type: Feature Request VS Code's API currently lacks native support for creating tables, requiring developers to implement custom solutions using webview view, custom editors, or treeviews for multi-column data display. This increases complexity and limits use cases where simple tabular layouts are needed. Introduce a table API, starting with Panel support, to enable straightforward multi-column data presentation—similar to the port panel in remote development—while also supporting interactive elements like buttons within cells. This would reduce development overhead and expanding extension capabilities. VS Code version: Code - Insiders 1.109.0-insider (5e5a439725cd83659b8b4ae5777b18731f0e99dc, 2026-01-13T08:07:02.448Z) OS version: Windows_NT x64 10.0.26200 Modes: Remote OS version: Linux x64 6.6.87.2-microsoft-standard-WSL2
author: iwangbowen

title: [Claude Code] Modernize using Session API
body:
## Overview
Evaluate and potentially adopt the Session API pattern from the [TypeScript V2 Preview](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/agent-sdk/typescript-v2-preview) for improved session management.

## Current State
The current implementation uses:
- `ClaudeCodeSession` class for managing conversation sessions
- `ClaudeCodeSessionService` for loading/persisting sessions from disk (`~/.claude/projects/<workspace-slug>/`)
- `Query` generator pattern with `prompt` async iterable

## Tasks
- [ ] Review [TypeScript V2 Preview](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/agent-sdk/typescript-v2-preview) Session API documentation
- [ ] Compare current `query()` approach vs. session-based `send/stream` patterns
- [ ] Evaluate benefits of Session API for:
  - Session resumption
  - Message management
  - Conversation state
- [ ] If adopting, update `ClaudeCodeSession` to use Session API
- [ ] Update `ClaudeCodeSdkService` interface if needed
- [ ] Ensure backward compatibility with existing persisted sessions
- [ ] Update `ClaudeCodeSessionService` to work with new session format if changed

## Questions to Answer
- Does the V2 Session API provide better session persistence?
- Does it simplify the prompt queue management in `ClaudeCodeSession`?
- Are there breaking changes with existing `.jsonl` session files?

## Related Files
- `src/extension/agents/claude/node/claudeCodeAgent.ts` (ClaudeCodeSession)
- `src/extension/agents/claude/node/claudeCodeSessionService.ts`
- `src/extension/agents/claude/node/claudeCodeSdkService.ts`

## Dependencies
- SDK update issue
