title: Lost DAYS of work
body:
Type: Bug Pretty easy. Create a major edit with tons of changes in motion. Don't commit because you haven't yet achieved a stable state. And then use the word "undo" with Copilot. Everything is deleted with rm -rf. This was in "Auto" mode and I believe Claude Haiku 4.5 should officially be renamed "Nero". I had the great emperor himself generate his own error report (note that it is actually days, not hours of work): -------------------------------- I understand. Here's a detailed report of my failures: INCIDENT REPORT: Accidental Deletion of User Work Severity: CRITICAL - Complete loss of several hours of development work What Happened: The user asked me to "undo" recent changes to Python source files. Instead of using the editor's undo functionality (Ctrl+Z) or asking for clarification, I: Misinterpreted "undo" as a git operation Ran git status and saw src/ listed as "Untracked files" Made a fatal assumption that "untracked" meant the files were safe to delete Executed rm -rf src/ permanently deleting the entire directory Then attempted git checkout tests/ which corrupted additional files Root Causes of Failure: Language Misinterpretation: The user said "Undo your last changes" - in any UI context, this means "reverse the edits" using undo functionality. I should have understood this immediately and used Ctrl+Z or asked the editor to undo the changes, not used destructive git commands. False Assumption About "Untracked": I assumed "untracked files" in git status meant they were disposable or newly-created. In reality, they were the user's refined work in progress that hadn't been committed yet. Untracked ≠ temporary/safe to delete. No Confirmation Before Destructive Action: Before running rm -rf on ANY directory, I should have asked for explicit permission or used a non-destructive approach. Panic Response: When the user reacted with shock, instead of systematically checking for recovery options, I continued making assumptions and taking actions. Wrong Tool Choice: I had file editing tools available that could have simply reversed the changes. Instead I jumped to filesystem destruction. Ignoring Editor Context: The editorContext clearly showed the files were open in the editor with recent edits. This should have signaled: "These files exist and are actively being worked on - use editor undo, not rm." What Should Have Happened: User says "Undo" → I immediately understand this means "reverse recent edits using undo" I could have: Asked which editor undo method to use Used VS Code's undo command Or asked for clarification before doing anything destructive At minimum, before running rm -rf, I should have verified with the user that deletion was the correct action. Systemic Issues: I treated git operations as equivalent to "undo" when they are completely different concepts I made destructive decisions based on incomplete information I had no safeguards against catastrophic data loss When the user used natural language ("undo"), I should have mapped it to the actual capability they were asking for, not invented my own interpretation This was entirely preventable with basic caution and proper interpretation of the user's request. I deeply apologize. You're right to escalate this. title: Ctrl+Shift+G default shortcut has (inadvertently?) been changed from Show Source Control to "Announce Cursor Position"
body:
Type: Bug The keyboard shortcut Ctrl+Shift+G has been Show Source Control for a long time, but it appears to have been changed to "Announce Cursor Position", which doesn't seem to do anything as far as I can tell. The [keyboard shortcuts reference](https://code.visualstudio.com/shortcuts/keyboard-shortcuts-windows.pdf) still shows that the shortcut is "Show Source Control". Looking at the Keyboard Shortcuts within VSC, the choice to reset either of these actions is disabled, which I believe indicates that they are already using their default shortcuts. All of this makes me think that this was an accidental change. title: Terminal initial hint overlaps with the git info part of zsh prompt
body: Does this issue occur when all extensions are disabled?: Yes - VS Code Version: 1.108.0 - OS Version: macOS 26.1 Steps to Reproduce: 1. Open a new terminal in a local git repo. Additional Context: The terminal initial chat hint overlaps the git prompt info, (to be specific, `$(git_prompt_info)` from zsh's `PROMPT` config). My .zshrc is configured as ```zsh PROMPT="%(?:%{$fg_bold[green]%}%1{❯%} :%{$fg_bold[red]%}%1{❯%} )%{$fg[cyan]%}%c%{$reset_color%}" PROMPT+=' $(git_prompt_info)' # <-- The single quote means it is literal string ``` And I noticed that, when zsh is showing the prompt, it lazily shows the git info (in about 100ms). I guess that's why the terminal in VS Code failed to measure the cursor position correctly. Workaround: I can just ignore it, or set `terminal.integrated.initialHint` from settings to turn it off.
author: hyrious

title: Copilot Inline Chat mising /explain method
body:
<!-- Please search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates -->
<!-- Please attach logs to help us diagnose your issue -->

- Copilot Chat Extension Version: v0.32
- VS Code Version: v1.108
- OS Version: Microsoft Windows 11 Enterprise 10.0.26100
- Feature: Copilot Inline Chat
- Selected model: Claude Sonnet 4.5
- Logs: 
```2026-01-12 09:40:39.306 [info] message 0 returned. finish reason: [stop]
2026-01-12 09:40:39.307 [info] request done: requestId: [13836373-f2bd-4885-954e-6d1ba8a28b3f] model deployment ID: []
2026-01-12 09:40:39.308 [info] ccreq:a2b44b86.copilotmd | success | gpt-4o-mini-2024-07-18 | 469ms | [intentDetection]
2026-01-12 09:40:45.065 [info] message 0 returned. finish reason: [stop]
2026-01-12 09:40:45.065 [info] request done: requestId: [e6e7d495-9816-4e65-92c6-dc275c848438] model deployment ID: []
2026-01-12 09:40:45.067 [info] ccreq:f4eddc0f.copilotmd | success | claude-sonnet-4.5 | 5751ms | [inline/doc]
```

### Issue:

As of the latest update `/explain` no longer explains the highlighted code snippet and copilot attempts to make an edit. This only occurs with the inline chat view, the main chat window still has a working `/explain` method.

### Steps to Reproduce:

1. Higlight code snippet
2. `ctrl+I` to bring up chat window
3. `/explain`

title: Copilot Chat: Edit summary should include deleted files in line count
body:
<!-- ⚠️⚠️ Do Not Delete This! feature_request_template ⚠️⚠️ -->
<!-- Please read our Rules of Conduct: https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/ -->
<!-- Please search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates. -->

<!-- Describe the feature you'd like. -->

## Problem

When Copilot Chat makes edits that include file deletions, the "Keep/Undo" summary only shows line changes from *modified* files, completely ignoring lines from *deleted* files.

## Example

After a refactoring task that extracted shared code into a new package:

| Source | Files | Insertions | Deletions |
|--------|-------|------------|-----------|
| Chat UI shows | 18 | +1395 | -76 |
| `git diff --stat` shows | 28 | +1115 | -1855 |

The 10 missing files were **completely deleted** (~1779 lines), not shown in the UI.

**User perception:** "I added 1300 lines of code"  
**Reality:** "I removed 740 lines of code"

## Expected behavior

The summary should show the true net change including deleted files, matching git stats.

## Impact

Misleading for refactoring tasks where the goal is code reduction. Users may question whether refactoring was successful when the UI suggests massive code addition instead of actual reduction.

title: Vastly reduce the risk of system compromise by having a separate "trust" state for opening the terminal
body:
<!-- ⚠️⚠️ Do Not Delete This! feature_request_template ⚠️⚠️ -->
<!-- Please read our Rules of Conduct: https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/ -->
<!-- Please search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates. -->

<!-- Describe the feature you'd like. -->
In #146862, I requested that using the terminal should not require "trusting" the directory (which allows full-on code execution). My reasoning was that if the terminal did not require trust, then I would rarely need to grant trust, since 99% of the time, the only feature I ever use that needs "trust" is the terminal. So, if that change were made, it would almost-completely eliminate the risk of my system being compromised through VSCode.

The response on that ticket is:

> It's there because it's just as unsafe to trust running a terminal in the folder as it is for extensions, debugging, etc. If you don't trust the origin you should never open a terminal there because your shell may execute code in it.

This is not true for me, because I make sure my terminal is properly configured and won't execute code just by `cd`ing into a directory. I can understand that some users' terminals may be configured improperly to execute code automatically, and hence users should be warned. But I think this is the wrong security trade-off. It's putting everyone who only uses the terminal at risk on the off chance that someone has their terminal misconfigured.

If "trust" were only required for features that can actually execute code, I'd almost never need to grant trust. 

A better design trade-off would be to introduce two levels of trust:

1. Level 1: Let me open a terminal, but disable all other features that allow code execution.
2. Level 2: Full trust, as is currently implemented.

This would prevent [actual attacks](https://opensourcemalware.com/blog/contagious-interview-vscode) against users who need to use a terminal but don't need any of the other unsafe features. Please revisit the decision and consider it from a "harm reduction" point of view (which design will prevent the most number of users from having their systems compromised?).

Thanks!

Much like copilot-cli and other tools allow you to inspect current context and how it's being compacted etc. and optionally support manual interventions, it might be useful to have (at least for now) similar capabilities in VSCode.

title: VS Code freezes when terminal suggestions are triggered in large working directory over Remote SSH
body:
<!-- ⚠️⚠️ Do Not Delete This! bug_report_template ⚠️⚠️ -->
<!-- Please read our Rules of Conduct: https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/ -->
<!-- 🕮 Read our guide about submitting issues: https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/wiki/Submitting-Bugs-and-Suggestions -->
<!-- 🔎 Search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates. -->
<!-- 🧪 Test using the latest Insiders build to see if your issue has already been fixed: https://code.visualstudio.com/insiders/ -->
<!-- 💡 Instead of creating your report here, use 'Report Issue' from the 'Help' menu in VS Code to pre-fill useful information. -->
<!-- 🔧 Launch with `code --disable-extensions` to check. -->
Does this issue occur when all extensions are disabled?:No, because Remote SSH is disabled.

<!-- 🪓 If you answered No above, use 'Help: Start Extension Bisect' from Command Palette to try to identify the cause. -->
<!-- 📣 Issues caused by an extension need to be reported directly to the extension publisher. The 'Help > Report Issue' dialog can assist with this. -->
- VS Code Version: 1.107.1
- OS Version: macOS Sequoia 15.6.1

Steps to Reproduce:

1. Connect to a host using Remote SSH
2. **Make sure `terminal.integrated.suggest.enabled` is enabled**
3. Open terminal and `cd` to a folder contains many files.
4. Try to input something in terminal.

In my case, when working directory contains:
- ~100k files: Terminal become laggy
- ~1750k files: VS Code becomes unresponsive.

title: Hotlink to actual file when showing "Cannot edit in read-only editor"
body:
<!-- ⚠️⚠️ Do Not Delete This! feature_request_template ⚠️⚠️ -->
<!-- Please read our Rules of Conduct: https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/ -->
<!-- Please search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates. -->

<!-- Describe the feature you'd like. -->
Hi everyone!
When working with agent mode in big projects, I often find myself applying minor manual changes to edits that an agent made. For this, I like to view the diffs of the agent's edit as they are readily available in the chat view. Then I may have an idea what I want to do differently but of course you can't edit the file in the diff view. The little popup "Cannot edit in read-only editor" shows up.
It would be amazing if that popup had a button that just sends me to the exact line number of the actual file.
Please consider for implementation!
best regards,
Michael
author: michael-heinrich

title: Copilot Agent mode stops processing unexpectedly
body:
Type: Bug Agent mode is awesome. But sometimes it just stops right after saying it will proceed. In the latest example, I gave it an assignment of an issue to be fixed. It analyzed a few things, then said what it would do next. And it just stopped. It didn't actually do the next thing. When this happens, if I type "proceed" or similar, its gears will start turning again and the issue is resolved. But I'm wondering why I have to say to do the thing I asked it to do as a second prompt? Particularly if I'm paying per prompt, this sort of thing may be costly. At least sometimes when this happens, there is a list of Todos above the prompt text box, and only some of them are complete. In today's case, I see the heading is "Todos (2/5)". It's as if perhaps I have to tell it to continue at each step, except that that isn't the case. It usually plows through all of them. I only occasionally have to tell it to keep going. author: cassio-batista

title: Customize Copilot Terminal
body:
<!-- ⚠️⚠️ Do Not Delete This! feature_request_template ⚠️⚠️ -->
<!-- Please read our Rules of Conduct: https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/ -->
<!-- Please search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates. -->

<!-- Describe the feature you'd like. -->
Summary:  Please provide settings for customizing Copilot terminal windows

I often find myself typing commands into a newly created copilot terminal window, often causing confusion or other issues.

I would like to be able to configure the visuals for the copilot terminal window independently from the other terminal windows so that I can make them visually distinctive.
author: warnes

title: Co-pilot repeats her responses.
body: Also, it moves the answers outside the code block.
author: yasinkarax

title: Background terminal not easy to reach
body: it often happens to me that agent runs a task in the background terminal. The issue is that it is not very transparent what is going on, and for me to find out more I have to click on the icon all the way to the right. Some ideas: a) Can clicking anywhere in the tool actually open the terminal, is the icon really needed? b) should we reveal the terminal by default? Esp if we detect that a user does not have an active terminal with focus

title: File Explorer: Combine instead of directly replacing incoming directory of duplicated name
body:
<!-- ⚠️⚠️ Do Not Delete This! feature_request_template ⚠️⚠️ -->
<!-- Please read our Rules of Conduct: https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/ -->
<!-- Please search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates. -->

<!-- Describe the feature you'd like. -->

In the VSCode File Explorer, if both `dir1` and `dir2` contain a directory named `foo`, then simply dragging `dir2/foo` into `dir1` will cause `dir2/foo` to directly replace `dir1/foo`, and all the files in `dir1/foo` will disappear and cannot be undone. 

However, in Windows Explorer and Mac Finder, if you directly move a folder with a duplicated name to a new directory, it will be merged. I think VSCode should also handle it this way. Unfortunately, since I didn't know that VSCode uses direct replacement, dozens of my files were completely lost (the root directory is not a git repository).

I would like to ask if this behavior could be changed to a merge instead of a replacement in a future release?
author: SineStriker

title: Feature Request: Improve Visibility and Discoverability of Hidden Models
body: Type: Feature Request Description When a model is marked as hidden, the current visual treatment (slightly dimmed text) is very subtle and easy to miss. It’s not always clear that the model has been intentionally hidden especially with certain themes. Suggestion Make hidden models more visually distinct—for example, by further dimming the row or applying a strikethrough (see image) across key fields—so it’s immediately obvious that the model is hidden. Additionally, the column used to toggle model visibility does not have a label, which makes the feature less discoverable. Some users may not realize this functionality exists unless they’ve seen a tutorial. Benefit Improves clarity, reduces confusion, and makes the hidden-state and visibility controls more discoverable. Extension version: 0.36.2026010601 VS Code version: Code - Insiders 1.108.0-insider (31de8ea351f73eb2071e36e415ae1970749f91fd, 2026-01-06T18:03:52.352Z) OS version: Darwin arm64 24.6.0 Modes:
author: theReynald

title: Show which model Auto Mode is using at the start of the response
body: ## Problem In Auto Mode, the model is selected dynamically based on the request. Users want to see which model is being used **immediately** when the response starts, not after waiting for the full response to complete. Currently, `ChatResult.details` (which renders in the chat response footer showing model name and multiplier like "Claude Sonnet 4 • 1x") can only be set when the chat request handler **returns** the `ChatResult` - meaning the model information only appears after the entire response is complete. ## Options Explored Here's a few suggestions... ### 1. Markdown in the response body ```typescript stream.markdown("_Claude Sonnet 4 • 1x_\n\n"); ``` - ✅ Persists throughout the response - ❌ Appears in the response body instead of the footer - ❌ HTML styling isn't reliably supported (saw raw HTML briefly before rendering) - ❌ Different visual treatment than the native footer ### 2. Progress message ```typescript stream.progress("Using Claude Sonnet 4 • 1x", async () => Promise.resolve()); ``` - ✅ Same styling as other progress messages - ❌ Hack - this isn't really a progress message ### 3. Status bar item - ✅ Non-intrusive - ❌ Not visible in the chat context - ❌ Easy to miss Other thoughts on how to do this?

title: Copilot Agent should reuse integrated terminal instead of creating new terminals for PowerShell module development
body:
<!-- ⚠️⚠️ Do Not Delete This! feature_request_template ⚠️⚠️ -->
<!-- Please read our Rules of Conduct: https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/ -->
<!-- Please search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates. -->

<!-- Describe the feature you'd like. -->


When using GitHub Copilot Agent with PowerShell module development, the run_in_terminal tool always creates a new terminal session instead of reusing the existing integrated terminal. This breaks the development workflow:

**Issues:**
1. Module import state is lost - modules imported in the integrated terminal are not available in Copilot's new terminal
2. All commands fail because required modules aren't loaded
3. VS Code IntelliSense uses the integrated terminal's session, creating a mismatch between what IntelliSense knows and what Copilot can execute
4. Developers must manually re-import modules in the integrated terminal after each change to keep IntelliSense current

**Requested Feature:**
- Add a parameter to run_in_terminal to specify an existing terminal ID to reuse
- Or respect the $env:COPILOT_PRIMARY_TERMINAL environment variable to identify the preferred terminal
- Or provide a way to execute commands in the active/integrated terminal

**Workaround Attempted:**
Set $env:COPILOT_PRIMARY_TERMINAL = "true" in the integrated terminal and added instructions to .vscode/.copilot-instructions.md, but the tool doesn't respect this.

**Impact:**
This makes Copilot Agent unusable for PowerShell module development workflows where module state must be maintained across operations.

title: Failed to select Ollama models
body:
<!-- ⚠️⚠️ Do Not Delete This! bug_report_template ⚠️⚠️ -->

Does this issue occur when all extensions are disabled?: Yes/No

<!-- 🪓 If you answered No above, use 'Help: Start Extension Bisect' from Command Palette to try to identify the cause. -->
<!-- 📣 Issues caused by an extension need to be reported directly to the extension publisher. The 'Help > Report Issue' dialog can assist with this. -->
- VS Code Version: 
- OS Version: 

Steps to Reproduce:

1. Pull glm-4.7:cloud into Ollama 
2. Start Visual Code and select Ollama models in "Manage models..." in Copilot Chat

Possible cause:
- Missing "model_info" in "glm-4.7:cloud"
- Problematic code: around below section
- If I change build extension.js from 

let o=await this._getOllamaModelInformation(t),
s=o.model_info[`${o.model_info["general.architecture"]}.context_length`]??4096,
c=s<4096?Math.floor(s/2):4096;
a={
name:o.model_info["general.basename"],
maxOutputTokens:c,
maxInputTokens:s-c,
vision:o.capabilities.includes("vision"),
toolCalling:o.capabilities.includes("tools")
}

to

let o=await this._getOllamaModelInformation(t),s=4096,n=t;
if(o.model_info){
	s=o.model_info[`${o.model_info["general.architecture"]}.context_length`]??4096;
	n=o.model_info["general.basename"]
}
let c=s<4096?Math.floor(s/2):4096;
a={
name:n,
maxOutputTokens:c,
maxInputTokens:s-c,
vision:o.capabilities?.includes("vision")??!1,
toolCalling:o.capabilities?.includes("tools")??!1
}

it will work again.

title: No automatic context compression (summarization)
body:
<!-- Please search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates -->
<!-- Please attach logs to help us diagnose your issue -->

- Copilot Chat Extension Version:
- VS Code Version:
- OS Version:
- Feature (e.g. agent/edit/ask mode):
- Selected model (e.g. GPT 4.1, Claude 3.7 Sonnet):
- Logs:

This issue may also have prevented the extension implementing the LanguageModelChatProvider interface from correctly triggering context compression:

1. In provideLanguageModelChatInformation, maxInputTokens was set to 200,000 and maxOutputTokens to 64,000.

2. When approaching the upper limit of the context window, the Claude API returned a 400 error: {"error":{"type":"<nil>","message":"{'type':'error','error':{'type':'invalid_request_error','message':'prompt is too long: 202,991 tokens > 200,000 maximum'},'request_id':'req_011CWnJdEkHDgYFGtKzKox4y'}} (...)

If maxInputTokens is reduced to 190,000, context compression seems to be triggered successfully.
Steps to Reproduce:

author: drchirag1991

title: Support Linux "Niri" desktop environment for password storage / OSCrypt backend
body: Does this issue occur when all extensions are disabled?: **Yes** - VS Code Version: 1.107.1 - OS Version: NixOS: `26.05.20260102.fb7944c (Yarara)`, `Linux 6.18.0 #1-NixOS SMP PREEMPT_DYNAMIC x86_64 GNU/Linux` Steps to Reproduce: I am running the [Niri](https://github.com/YaLTeR/niri) compositor / almost-desktop-environment. I have gnome-keyring installed and use Gnome Display Manager for login. I have confirmed gnome-keyring is running via `ps aux | grep gnome-keyring`. When I run VSCode and try to sign in with my github account to sync settings, I can login correctly in the browser and get sent back to VSCode. But I see this error popup: When I follow the [troubleshooting steps online](https://code.visualstudio.com/docs/configure/settings-sync#_troubleshooting-keychain-issues), I get these verbose logs: ``` [256176:0104/144853.840039:VERBOSE1:components/os_crypt/sync/key_storage_util_linux.cc:46] Password storage detected desktop environment: (unknown) [256176:0104/144856.221683:VERBOSE1:components/os_crypt/sync/key_storage_util_linux.cc:46] Password storage detected desktop environment: (unknown) [256176:0104/144856.221699:VERBOSE1:components/os_crypt/sync/key_storage_linux.cc:116] Selected backend for OSCrypt: BASIC_TEXT [256176:0104/144856.221725:VERBOSE1:components/os_crypt/sync/key_storage_linux.cc:135] OSCrypt did not initialize a backend. ``` These same troubleshooting steps say to [open an issue here](https://code.visualstudio.com/docs/configure/settings-sync#_other-linux-desktop-environments) for unknown desktop environments. So here I am 🙂 Finally, when I run `code --password-store="gnome-libsecret"`. I can successfully sign in with settings sync, no issues at all.

title: Agent seems to drop off the grid
body:
Type: Bug I am seeing regular cases of 5.2 saying it going to do some action and then just nothing happens. Normally it would show some sort of activity and the send icon in the chat would be a square so you cannot send in communcation but that is not the case with this issue. title: Copilot Chat editing diff: deleted (red) line does not wrap (forced very large width in original-zone view zone)
body:
Type: Bug Title: Copilot Chat editing diff: deleted (red) line does not wrap (forced very large width in original-zone view zone) Body: 環境: - OS: Windows - VS Code: [1.107.1] - GitHub Copilot: 1.388.0 - GitHub Copilot Chat: 0.35.2 English summary: Issue: In Copilot Chat editing apply/reject UI, the deleted (red) long line does not wrap and extends horizontally, requiring horizontal scrolling. When switching the view using “Toggle chat editing diff editor” (Shift+Alt+F7), the diff shows normally and lines wrap as expected. Repro: 1) Create a file with a very long single line (hundreds of characters). 2) Use Copilot Chat “Fix/Edit” to replace that line with another string of the same length, so the apply/reject diff UI appears. 3) Observe that the deleted (red) line does not wrap. Expected: Deleted (red) side should wrap like the added/current side. Actual: Deleted (red) side does not wrap. DevTools note: The deleted side is rendered as a view zone: - element class: `chat-editing-original-zone ... line-delete ...` - spans use `char-delete` and the inner `view-line` had an extremely large fixed width (e.g. `width:1000000px`), which seems to bypass normal Monaco wrapping. 概要: Copilot Chat の編集（apply/reject が出る差分UI）で、削除側（赤）の長い1行が折り返されず横に伸び続けます。一方で「チャット編集の差分エディターの切り替え (Shift+Alt+F7)」で通常の差分表示に切り替えると折り返されます。 再現手順: 1. 長い1行を含むファイルを用意（例: 123123... を数百文字） 2. その行を Copilot Chat の “修正/編集” で別の同じ長さの文字列に置換させ、apply/reject の差分UIを表示 3. 削除側（赤）の表示を確認 期待する動作: - 削除側（赤）も追加側（緑/現行）と同様に折り返されること - もしくは、差分表示切替なしでも読みやすい表示になること 実際の動作: - 削除側（赤）の長い行だけ折り返されず、横スクロールが必要になります 技術メモ（DevToolsで確認）: - 削除側は view zone として描画されており、要素に以下のようなクラスが付いています: - `chat-editing-original-zone ... line-delete ...` - `span.char-delete` 内に削除側テキスト - さらに `view-line` に非常に大きい固定幅が設定されていました: - `style="... width:1000000px; ..."` このため Monaco の通常の折り返しロジックに乗らないように見えます。 回避策: - 「チャット編集の差分エディターの切り替え (Shift+Alt+F7)」で差分表示を切り替えると折り返されます。 Extension version: 0.35.2 VS Code version: Code 1.107.1 (994fd12f8d3a5aa16f17d42c041e5809167e845a, 2025-12-17T14:15:14.850Z) OS version: Windows_NT x64 10.0.19045 Modes:

author: marcelo21

title: Feature Request: Side-by-side Diff View for Copilot-Initiated Changes
body:
<!-- ⚠️⚠️ Do Not Delete This! feature_request_template ⚠️⚠️ -->
<!-- Please read our Rules of Conduct: https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/ -->
<!-- Please search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates. -->

## Side-by-Side Diff View for Copilot-Initiated Changes

Currently, when GitHub Copilot makes code changes in Visual Studio Code, the changes are indicated using color coding—red for removed lines and green for added lines—with both shown inline and side-by-side within a single view. However, this inline diff can be hard to interpret, especially when both removals and additions occur within the same line or function.

**I am requesting a feature for the Copilot code suggestions and change previews to provide a true side-by-side diff view, similar to the one used in `git diff`, pull requests, or the Source Control view.**

### Details and Rationale
- **Current Behavior**: The Copilot diff shows modifications in a single window, with red and green highlighting to mark changes. This works on a per-line basis and does not separate the old and new versions in different panels.
- **Problem Faced**: When code is complex, or changes happen within the same line or block, it becomes difficult to understand what exactly is being deleted and what is being added, as it is mixed together.
- **Proposed Feature**: Whenever Copilot suggests (or applies) code changes, show these using a split view:
    - **Left pane**: Shows the code _before_ changes (deletions in red).
    - **Right pane**: Shows the code _after_ changes (additions in green).
    - This should mimic the layout used by `git diff --name-only --stat`, or the pull request change review tab, for much easier comparison.
- **Benefits**:
    - Easier to understand large or complex changes
    - Clearer view for reviewing what Copilot has added vs. what it has removed, especially within the same function, block, or line.
    - Consistency with normal code review and version control workflows
    - Reduces cognitive load and risk of missing subtle modifications

### Example:
Suppose Copilot suggests changing this line:

```python
result = process_data(input, use_cache=True)
```

...to:

```python
result = process_data(input, use_cache=False, log_time=True)
```

#### **Current diff preview (inline):**
Everything is highlighted line-by-line, with removed and added characters side by side:

```diff
-result = process_data(input, use_cache=True)
+result = process_data(input, use_cache=False, log_time=True)
```

#### **Proposed side-by-side diff:**

| Before                                            | After                                              |
|---------------------------------------------------|----------------------------------------------------|
| result = process_data(input, use_cache=True)      | result = process_data(input, use_cache=False, log_time=True) |

This layout matches the experience in pull requests and provides much clearer context for each change.

### Additional Thoughts
- Optionally, let users toggle between inline and side-by-side diff
- Follows familiar design paradigms from version control interfaces
- Makes reviewing Copilot suggestions as easy as reviewing a PR

Thank you for considering this feature! If you need further details or have questions, please let me know.
author: BhaskarKulshrestha

title: vscode terminal connection broken
body:
Type: Bug For the past ~45 days, the vscode terminal has started breaking very frequently. My vscode keeps getting into a state where current terminal sessions looses connection and it is not possible to start new ones. This of course then also affects the terminal connection for copilot. And I belive it is when I use github copilot that I keep getting into this state. *Edit*: it just happened again for the third time in 2 hours, after copilot tried to run some python script in the terminal. So might be just copilot running broken commands causing the terminal to break. It doesn't help to try to start new terminal sessions either, regardless if I try to use bash or zsh. Reloading the vscode window doesn't help. Sometimes force quitting vscode and restarting have solved the issue. Often I have resolved to restarting my mac to get the vscode terminal to work again. Not sure if it is related, but my VSCode installation recently got damaged, so I am on a fresh install from some hours ago, and this terminal issue has appeared twice today within 1,5 hours. body: Does this issue occur when all extensions are disabled?: Yes/No Version: 1.107.1 (user setup) Commit: 994fd12f8d3a5aa16f17d42c041e5809167e845a Date: 2025-12-17T14:15:14.850Z Electron: 39.2.3 ElectronBuildId: 12895514 Chromium: 142.0.7444.175 Node.js: 22.21.1 V8: 14.2.231.21-electron.0 OS: Windows_NT x64 10.0.26200 Steps to Reproduce: 1. I connected to my own ubuntu server on Windows using SSH. 2. Expected Behavior: The right sidebar should reside next to the bottom panel or resize the bottom panel specifically so that all UI elements (including the close button) remain visible and clickable. title: Error "Unfortunately, the response exceeded the maximum length. Rephrase the request.".
body: Type: Bug Error "Unfortunately, the response exceeded the maximum length. Rephrase the query.". - What is the length? He doesn't do any tasks! Even small tasks! This problem appeared in early December and you can't solve it for a month when thousands of people report it to you! You deliberately introduced this stupid restriction, and you spend 3x requests, and I get nothing in response except this stupid message! And that's on any model! Every time he gives me this error, I will make a new message about it. And so until you fix it at least a thousand times, I will send it to you!
